fr.endangerment: La chasse de viande de brousse et la déforestation généralisée au Liberia, en Sierra Leone, en Guinée et en Côte d'Ivoire ont considérablement réduit les populations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une forêt humide dense et des zones marécageuses près des rivières en Afrique occidentale, avec une végétation non perturbée pour l'abri et des plantes aquatiques pour le pâturage nocturne.
fr.actions: Soutenez la gestion des zones protégées dans le Parc national de Sapo et la Forêt du Taï, financez des enquêtes par caméras-pièges et soutenez la législation anti-chasse au Liberia.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The aye-aye is the world's largest nocturnal primate, using its elongated middle finger to tap on wood and extract insect larvae, filling the ecological niche of woodpeckers on Madagascar.
endangerment: Habitat loss to slash-and-burn agriculture and logging, combined with persecution due to superstitious beliefs that it is an evil omen, threaten remaining populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Mature tropical and dry deciduous forest across Madagascar with large trees harbouring wood-boring insect larvae and sufficient fruit sources.
actions: Support community education programmes to counter negative superstitions, fund reforestation in northeast Madagascar, and back protected area expansion.

en.description: The aye-aye is the world's largest nocturnal primate, using its elongated middle finger to tap on wood and extract insect larvae, filling the ecological niche of woodpeckers on Madagascar.
en.endangerment: Habitat loss to slash-and-burn agriculture and logging, combined with persecution due to superstitious beliefs that it is an evil omen, threaten remaining populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Mature tropical and dry deciduous forest across Madagascar with large trees harbouring wood-boring insect larvae and sufficient fruit sources.
en.actions: Support community education programmes to counter negative superstitions, fund reforestation in northeast Madagascar, and back protected area expansion.

es.description: El aye-aye es el primate nocturno más grande del mundo, utiliza su alargado dedo medio para golpear la madera y extraer larvas de insectos, ocupando el nicho ecológico de los carpinteros en Madagascar.
es.endangerment: La pérdida de hábitat por la agricultura de tala y quema y la tala, combinada con la persecución debida a creencias supersticiosas de que es un presagio del mal, amenazan a las poblaciones restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque tropical maduro y caducifolio seco en toda Madagascar con grandes árboles que albergan larvas de insectos que perforan la madera y suficientes fuentes de fruta.
es.actions: Apoya programas de educación comunitaria para contrarrestar supersticiones negativas, financia la reforestación en el noreste de Madagascar y respalda la expansión de áreas protegidas.

pt.description: O aye-aye é o maior primata noturno do mundo, usando seu dedo médio alongado para bater na madeira e extrair larvas de insetos, ocupando o nicho ecológico dos pica-paus em Madagascar.
pt.endangerment: A perda de habitat pela agricultura de corte e queima e pelo desmatamento, combinada com a perseguição devida a crenças supersticiosas de que é um presságio do mal, ameaçam as populações restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical madura e caducifólia seca em toda Madagascar com grandes árvores que abrigam larvas de insetos que perfuram a madeira e fontes de fruta suficientes.
pt.actions: Apoie programas de educação comunitária para combater superstições negativas, finance o reflorestamento no nordeste de Madagascar e respalda a expansão de áreas protegidas.

fr.description: L'aye-aye est le plus grand primate nocturne du monde, utilisant son long majeur pour tapoter le bois et extraire les larves d'insectes, occupant la niche écologique des pics à Madagascar.
fr.endangerment: La perte d'habitat due à l'agriculture sur brûlis et à l'exploitation forestière, combinée aux persécutions liées à des croyances superstitieuses selon lesquelles il serait un mauvais présage, menacent les populations restantes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: La forêt tropicale mature et la forêt caducifoliée sèche de toute Madagascar, avec de grands arbres abritant des larves d'insectes xylophages et des sources de fruits suffisantes.
fr.actions: Soutenez les programmes d'éducation communautaire pour contrer les superstitions négatives, financez le reboisement dans le nord-est de Madagascar et appuyez l'expansion des zones protégées.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The hirola, or Hunter's hartebeest, is the world's rarest antelope, restricted to a narrow strip of arid grassland on the Kenya–Somalia border.
endangerment: Drought, disease, habitat degradation from overgrazing, and predation pressure have reduced the population to a few hundred animals in the wild.
ecosystemNeeds: Open short-grass plains and bushland in northeastern Kenya with seasonal waterholes and minimal competition from livestock.
actions: Support the Hirola Conservation Programme's community conservancy model, fund predator management, and back wildlife corridor creation in the Tana River region.

en.description: The hirola, or Hunter's hartebeest, is the world's rarest antelope, restricted to a narrow strip of arid grassland on the Kenya–Somalia border.
en.endangerment: Drought, disease, habitat degradation from overgrazing, and predation pressure have reduced the population to a few hundred animals in the wild.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open short-grass plains and bushland in northeastern Kenya with seasonal waterholes and minimal competition from livestock.
en.actions: Support the Hirola Conservation Programme's community conservancy model, fund predator management, and back wildlife corridor creation in the Tana River region.

es.description: El hirola, o hartebeest de Hunter, es el antílope más raro del mundo, restringido a una estrecha franja de pastizales áridos en la frontera Kenia-Somalia.
es.endangerment: La sequía, las enfermedades, la degradación del hábitat por el sobrepastoreo y la presión depredadora han reducido la población a unos pocos cientos de animales en la naturaleza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Llanuras abiertas de hierba corta y matorrales en el noreste de Kenia con pozas de agua estacionales y mínima competencia del ganado.
es.actions: Apoya el modelo de conservancia comunitaria del Programa de Conservación del Hirola, financia la gestión de depredadores y respalda la creación de corredores de vida silvestre en la región del río Tana.

pt.description: O hirola, ou hartebeest de Hunter, é o antílope mais raro do mundo, restrito a uma estreita faixa de pastagens áridas na fronteira Quênia-Somália.
pt.endangerment: A seca, as doenças, a degradação do habitat pelo superpastoreio e a pressão de predação reduziram a população a algumas centenas de animais na natureza.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Planícies abertas de gramíneas curtas e mato no nordeste do Quênia com aguadas sazonais e competição mínima do gado.
pt.actions: Apoie o modelo de conservância comunitária do Programa de Conservação do Hirola, finance o manejo de predadores e respalda a criação de corredores de vida selvagem na região do Rio Tana.

fr.description: L'hirola, ou bubale de Hunter, est la plus rare des antilopes, cantonnée à une étroite bande de prairies arides à la frontière Kenya-Somalie.
fr.endangerment: La sécheresse, les maladies, la dégradation de l'habitat due au surpâturage et la pression des prédateurs ont réduit la population à quelques centaines d'animaux à l'état sauvage.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des plaines ouvertes à herbes courtes et des zones arbustives dans le nord-est du Kenya, avec des points d'eau saisonniers et une concurrence minimale du bétail.
fr.actions: Soutenez le modèle de conservancy communautaire du Programme de conservation de l'hirola, financez la gestion des prédateurs et appuyez la création de corridors fauniques dans la région de la rivière Tana.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Ganges river dolphin is functionally blind, navigating entirely by echolocation in the turbid waters of the Ganges, Brahmaputra, and Meghna river systems.
endangerment: Bycatch in fishing nets, water pollution, damming of rivers that fragments populations, and hunting for oil have all contributed to severe decline.
ecosystemNeeds: Deep, fast-flowing sections of large South Asian rivers with high fish density, undisturbed by heavy commercial vessel traffic.
actions: Support India's Ganges River Dolphin sanctuary network, back bycatch mitigation measures, and advocate for stricter industrial pollution controls on the Ganges.

en.description: The Ganges river dolphin is functionally blind, navigating entirely by echolocation in the turbid waters of the Ganges, Brahmaputra, and Meghna river systems.
en.endangerment: Bycatch in fishing nets, water pollution, damming of rivers that fragments populations, and hunting for oil have all contributed to severe decline.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Deep, fast-flowing sections of large South Asian rivers with high fish density, undisturbed by heavy commercial vessel traffic.
en.actions: Support India's Ganges River Dolphin sanctuary network, back bycatch mitigation measures, and advocate for stricter industrial pollution controls on the Ganges.

es.description: El delfín del río Ganges es funcionalmente ciego, navegando completamente por ecolocalización en las turbias aguas de los sistemas fluviales del Ganges, Brahmaputra y Meghna.
es.endangerment: La captura incidental en redes de pesca, la contaminación del agua, el represamiento de ríos que fragmenta las poblaciones y la caza para obtener aceite han contribuido a un grave declive.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Secciones profundas y de flujo rápido de grandes ríos del sur de Asia con alta densidad de peces, sin perturbaciones por intenso tráfico comercial de embarcaciones.
es.actions: Apoya la red de santuarios del Delfín del río Ganges de India, respalda medidas de mitigación de la captura incidental y aboga por controles industriales de contaminación más estrictos en el Ganges.

pt.description: O boto-do-ganges é funcionalmente cego, navegando completamente por ecolocalização nas águas turvas dos sistemas fluviais do Ganges, Brahmaputra e Meghna.
pt.endangerment: A captura acidental em redes de pesca, a poluição da água, o represamento de rios que fragmenta as populações e a caça para obtenção de óleo contribuíram para um grave declínio.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Seções profundas e de fluxo rápido de grandes rios do sul da Ásia com alta densidade de peixes, sem perturbações pelo intenso tráfego comercial de embarcações.
pt.actions: Apoie a rede de santuários do Boto do Rio Ganges da Índia, respalda medidas de mitigação da captura acidental e defenda controles industriais de poluição mais rigorosos no Ganges.

fr.description: Le dauphin du Gange est fonctionnellement aveugle, naviguant entièrement par écholocation dans les eaux turbides des systèmes fluviaux du Gange, du Brahmapoutre et du Meghna.
fr.endangerment: Les prises accessoires dans les filets de pêche, la pollution de l'eau, les barrages fragmentant les populations et la chasse pour l'huile ont contribué à un grave déclin.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des sections profondes et à débit rapide des grands fleuves d'Asie du Sud avec une haute densité de poissons, sans perturbations liées au trafic commercial intense des navires.
fr.actions: Soutenez le réseau de sanctuaires du dauphin du Gange en Inde, appuyez les mesures d'atténuation des prises accessoires et plaidez pour des contrôles industriels plus stricts de la pollution du Gange.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The greater bamboo lemur is the largest bamboo lemur, endemic to Madagascar and uniquely adapted to eat giant bamboo pith containing toxic cyanide, which it tolerates through special biology.
endangerment: Destruction of bamboo and rainforest for slash-and-burn agriculture, logging, and hunting have reduced the species to a few tiny populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Mature humid rainforest with abundant giant bamboo stands in southeastern Madagascar, particularly near Ranomafana and Andringitra.
actions: Support Ranomafana National Park management, fund community-based forest protection near bamboo habitat, and back captive breeding insurance populations.

en.description: The greater bamboo lemur is the largest bamboo lemur, endemic to Madagascar and uniquely adapted to eat giant bamboo pith containing toxic cyanide, which it tolerates through special biology.
en.endangerment: Destruction of bamboo and rainforest for slash-and-burn agriculture, logging, and hunting have reduced the species to a few tiny populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Mature humid rainforest with abundant giant bamboo stands in southeastern Madagascar, particularly near Ranomafana and Andringitra.
en.actions: Support Ranomafana National Park management, fund community-based forest protection near bamboo habitat, and back captive breeding insurance populations.

es.description: El lemur bambú mayor es el lemur bambú más grande, endémico de Madagascar y únicamente adaptado para comer la médula de bambú gigante que contiene cianuro tóxico, que tolera a través de una biología especial.
es.endangerment: La destrucción del bambú y del bosque lluvioso para la agricultura de tala y quema, la tala y la caza han reducido la especie a unas pocas poblaciones pequeñas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque lluvioso húmedo maduro con abundantes cañaverales de bambú gigante en el sureste de Madagascar, particularmente cerca de Ranomafana y Andringitra.
es.actions: Apoya la gestión del Parque Nacional Ranomafana, financia la protección forestal comunitaria cerca del hábitat de bambú y respalda las poblaciones de reproducción en cautividad como seguro.

pt.description: O lêmure-de-bambu-maior é o maior lêmure-de-bambu, endêmico de Madagascar e exclusivamente adaptado para comer a medula de bambu gigante contendo cianeto tóxico, que tolera através de biologia especial.
pt.endangerment: A destruição do bambu e da floresta tropical para a agricultura de corte e queima, a extração de madeira e a caça reduziram a espécie a poucas populações minúsculas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical úmida madura com abundantes bambuzais de bambu gigante no sudeste de Madagascar, particularmente perto de Ranomafana e Andringitra.
pt.actions: Apoie a gestão do Parque Nacional de Ranomafana, finance a proteção florestal comunitária perto do habitat de bambu e respalda as populações de criação em cativeiro como seguro.

fr.description: Le grand lémur brun de bambou est le plus grand lémur brun, endémique de Madagascar et uniquement adapté pour manger la moelle de bambou géant contenant du cyanure toxique, qu'il tolère grâce à une biologie spéciale.
fr.endangerment: La destruction du bambou et de la forêt pluviale pour l'agriculture sur brûlis, l'exploitation forestière et la chasse ont réduit l'espèce à quelques minuscules populations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt pluviale humide mature avec d'abondants peuplements de bambou géant dans le sud-est de Madagascar, notamment près de Ranomafana et d'Andringitra.
fr.actions: Soutenez la gestion du Parc national de Ranomafana, financez la protection forestière communautaire près de l'habitat de bambou et appuyez les populations d'élevage en captivité à titre d'assurance.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Bornean orangutan is one of humanity's closest relatives and the largest arboreal mammal, spending most of its life high in the rainforest canopy of Borneo.
endangerment: Industrial-scale deforestation for palm oil, timber, and mining has destroyed more than half of its habitat since 1950, and illegal hunting continues.
ecosystemNeeds: Large areas of intact lowland and dipterocarp rainforest with abundant fruiting trees, particularly in Sabah, Sarawak, and Kalimantan.
actions: Choose certified sustainable palm oil, support Bornean orangutan rehabilitation centres, and advocate for stronger forest protection laws in Indonesia and Malaysia.

en.description: The Bornean orangutan is one of humanity's closest relatives and the largest arboreal mammal, spending most of its life high in the rainforest canopy of Borneo.
en.endangerment: Industrial-scale deforestation for palm oil, timber, and mining has destroyed more than half of its habitat since 1950, and illegal hunting continues.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large areas of intact lowland and dipterocarp rainforest with abundant fruiting trees, particularly in Sabah, Sarawak, and Kalimantan.
en.actions: Choose certified sustainable palm oil, support Bornean orangutan rehabilitation centres, and advocate for stronger forest protection laws in Indonesia and Malaysia.

es.description: El orangután de Borneo es uno de los parientes más cercanos de la humanidad y el mayor mamífero arborícola, pasando la mayor parte de su vida en las alturas del dosel del bosque lluvioso de Borneo.
es.endangerment: La deforestación a escala industrial para el aceite de palma, la madera y la minería ha destruido más de la mitad de su hábitat desde 1950, y la caza ilegal continúa.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes áreas de bosque lluvioso de tierras bajas y dipterocárpeo intacto con abundantes árboles frutales, especialmente en Sabah, Sarawak y Kalimantan.
es.actions: Elige aceite de palma certificado sostenible, apoya los centros de rehabilitación de orangutanes de Borneo y aboga por leyes más sólidas de protección forestal en Indonesia y Malasia.

pt.description: O orangotango-de-borneo é um dos parentes mais próximos da humanidade e o maior mamífero arborícola, passando a maior parte de sua vida nas alturas do dossel da floresta tropical de Borneo.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento em escala industrial para o óleo de palma, madeira e mineração destruiu mais da metade de seu habitat desde 1950, e a caça ilegal continua.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes áreas de floresta tropical de planície e de dipterocarpo intacta com árvores frutíferas abundantes, especialmente em Sabah, Sarawak e Kalimantan.
pt.actions: Escolha óleo de palma certificado sustentável, apoie os centros de reabilitação de orangotangos de Borneo e defenda leis mais rigorosas de proteção florestal na Indonésia e na Malásia.

fr.description: L'orang-outan de Bornéo est l'un des parents les plus proches de l'humanité et le plus grand mammifère arboricole, passant la majeure partie de sa vie dans les hauteurs de la canopée de la forêt pluviale de Bornéo.
fr.endangerment: La déforestation à grande échelle pour l'huile de palme, le bois et l'exploitation minière a détruit plus de la moitié de son habitat depuis 1950, et la chasse illégale se poursuit.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: De grandes zones de forêt pluviale de basse altitude et de diptérocarpacées intacte avec des arbres fruitiers abondants, notamment à Sabah, Sarawak et Kalimantan.
fr.actions: Choisissez de l'huile de palme certifiée durable, soutenez les centres de réhabilitation des orangs-outans de Bornéo et plaidez pour des lois de protection forestière plus strictes en Indonésie et en Malaisie.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The southern muriqui is South America's largest primate, a peaceful, social monkey known for its long limbs and spider-like agility through Atlantic Forest canopy in Brazil.
endangerment: Destruction of Brazil's Atlantic Forest — reduced to less than 12% of its original extent — has fragmented muriqui habitat into tiny isolated patches.
ecosystemNeeds: Large, connected tracts of Atlantic Forest in Minas Gerais and São Paulo states with mature trees, lianas, and abundant foliage for browsing.
actions: Support Atlantic Forest restoration initiatives in Brazil, donate to muriqui research stations like RPPN Feliciano Miguel Abdala, and back forest corridor projects.

en.description: The southern muriqui is South America's largest primate, a peaceful, social monkey known for its long limbs and spider-like agility through Atlantic Forest canopy in Brazil.
en.endangerment: Destruction of Brazil's Atlantic Forest — reduced to less than 12% of its original extent — has fragmented muriqui habitat into tiny isolated patches.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, connected tracts of Atlantic Forest in Minas Gerais and São Paulo states with mature trees, lianas, and abundant foliage for browsing.
en.actions: Support Atlantic Forest restoration initiatives in Brazil, donate to muriqui research stations like RPPN Feliciano Miguel Abdala, and back forest corridor projects.

es.description: El muriquí del sur es el primate más grande de América del Sur, un mono pacífico y social conocido por sus largas extremidades y su agilidad similar a una araña a través del dosel del bosque atlántico en Brasil.
es.endangerment: La destrucción del bosque atlántico de Brasil —reducido a menos del 12% de su extensión original— ha fragmentado el hábitat del muriquí en pequeños parches aislados.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes extensiones conectadas de bosque atlántico en los estados de Minas Gerais y São Paulo con árboles maduros, lianas y abundante follaje para ramonear.
es.actions: Apoya las iniciativas de restauración del bosque atlántico en Brasil, dona a estaciones de investigación de muriquí y respalda proyectos de corredores forestales.

pt.description: O muriqui-do-sul é o maior primata da América do Sul, um macaco pacífico e social conhecido por seus longos membros e agilidade similar a uma aranha pelo dossel da Mata Atlântica no Brasil.
pt.endangerment: A destruição da Mata Atlântica do Brasil — reduzida a menos de 12% de sua extensão original — fragmentou o habitat do muriqui em pequenos fragmentos isolados.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes extensões conectadas de Mata Atlântica nos estados de Minas Gerais e São Paulo com árvores maduras, cipós e folhagem abundante para pastejo.
pt.actions: Apoie as iniciativas de restauração da Mata Atlântica no Brasil, doe para estações de pesquisa de muriqui e respalda projetos de corredores florestais.

fr.description: Le muriqui du Sud est le plus grand primate d'Amérique du Sud, un singe pacifique et sociable connu pour ses longs membres et son agilité d'araignée dans la canopée de la forêt atlantique au Brésil.
fr.endangerment: La destruction de la forêt atlantique du Brésil — réduite à moins de 12% de son étendue d'origine — a fragmenté l'habitat du muriqui en de minuscules îlots isolés.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: De vastes étendues connectées de forêt atlantique dans les États du Minas Gerais et de São Paulo, avec des arbres matures, des lianes et un feuillage abondant pour le broutage.
fr.actions: Soutenez les initiatives de restauration de la forêt atlantique au Brésil, faites des dons aux stations de recherche sur le muriqui et appuyez les projets de corridors forestiers.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Ethiopian wolf is Africa's most endangered carnivore and the world's rarest canid, restricted to the Afroalpine highlands of Ethiopia where it hunts Afroalpine rodents.
endangerment: Rabies and canine distemper spreading from domestic dogs have caused devastating epidemics. Agricultural encroachment is shrinking its high-altitude habitat.
ecosystemNeeds: Open Afroalpine grassland above 3,000 metres in the Ethiopian highlands, particularly the Bale Mountains, with abundant rodent prey and minimal domestic dog contact.
actions: Support the Ethiopian Wolf Conservation Programme's vaccination campaigns, back livestock exclusion zones in core wolf habitat, and fund monitoring of disease outbreaks.

en.description: The Ethiopian wolf is Africa's most endangered carnivore and the world's rarest canid, restricted to the Afroalpine highlands of Ethiopia where it hunts Afroalpine rodents.
en.endangerment: Rabies and canine distemper spreading from domestic dogs have caused devastating epidemics. Agricultural encroachment is shrinking its high-altitude habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open Afroalpine grassland above 3,000 metres in the Ethiopian highlands, particularly the Bale Mountains, with abundant rodent prey and minimal domestic dog contact.
en.actions: Support the Ethiopian Wolf Conservation Programme's vaccination campaigns, back livestock exclusion zones in core wolf habitat, and fund monitoring of disease outbreaks.

es.description: El lobo etíope es el carnívoro más amenazado de África y el cánido más raro del mundo, restringido a las tierras altas afroalpinas de Etiopía donde caza roedores afroalpinos.
es.endangerment: La rabia y el moquillo canino que se propagan desde perros domésticos han causado epidemias devastadoras. La invasión agrícola está reduciendo su hábitat de gran altitud.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Pastizales afroalpinos abiertos por encima de los 3.000 metros en las tierras altas etíopes, especialmente las montañas Bale, con abundantes presas de roedores y contacto mínimo con perros domésticos.
es.actions: Apoya las campañas de vacunación del Programa de Conservación del Lobo Etíope, respalda las zonas de exclusión de ganado en el hábitat principal del lobo y financia el monitoreo de brotes de enfermedades.

pt.description: O lobo-etíope é o carnívoro mais ameaçado de África e o canídeo mais raro do mundo, restrito às terras altas afroalpinas da Etiópia onde caça roedores afroalpinos.
pt.endangerment: A raiva e a cinomose canina se espalhando de cães domésticos causaram epidemias devastadoras. A invasão agrícola está reduzindo seu habitat de grande altitude.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pastagens afroalpinas abertas acima de 3.000 metros nas terras altas etíopes, especialmente as Montanhas Bale, com abundantes presas de roedores e contato mínimo com cães domésticos.
pt.actions: Apoie as campanhas de vacinação do Programa de Conservação do Lobo-etíope, respalda as zonas de exclusão de gado no habitat principal do lobo e finance o monitoramento de surtos de doenças.

fr.description: Le loup d'Éthiopie est le carnivore le plus menacé d'Afrique et le canidé le plus rare du monde, cantonné aux hautes terres afroalpines d'Éthiopie où il chasse les rongeurs afroalpins.
fr.endangerment: La rage et la maladie de Carré se propageant depuis les chiens domestiques ont causé des épidémies dévastatrices. L'empiètement agricole réduit son habitat de haute altitude.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des prairies afroalpines ouvertes au-dessus de 3 000 mètres dans les hautes terres éthiopiennes, notamment les Monts Bale, avec d'abondantes proies de rongeurs et un contact minimal avec les chiens domestiques.
fr.actions: Soutenez les campagnes de vaccination du Programme de conservation du loup d'Éthiopie, appuyez les zones d'exclusion du bétail dans l'habitat principal du loup et financez la surveillance des épidémies.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Tonkin snub-nosed monkey is a striking primate with an upturned nose, found only in a few fragmented forest patches in northern Vietnam, with fewer than 250 individuals estimated in the wild.
endangerment: Hunting for meat, traditional medicine, and deforestation for shifting cultivation have reduced this species to critically small numbers.
ecosystemNeeds: Limestone karst forest and tropical montane forest in northern Vietnam, particularly in Na Hang and Khau Ca nature reserves, with seasonal fruit and leaf availability.
actions: Support Vietnam's Tonkin snub-nosed monkey sanctuaries, fund community conservation around Khau Ca, and back strict hunting prohibition enforcement.

en.description: The Tonkin snub-nosed monkey is a striking primate with an upturned nose, found only in a few fragmented forest patches in northern Vietnam, with fewer than 250 individuals estimated in the wild.
en.endangerment: Hunting for meat, traditional medicine, and deforestation for shifting cultivation have reduced this species to critically small numbers.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Limestone karst forest and tropical montane forest in northern Vietnam, particularly in Na Hang and Khau Ca nature reserves, with seasonal fruit and leaf availability.
en.actions: Support Vietnam's Tonkin snub-nosed monkey sanctuaries, fund community conservation around Khau Ca, and back strict hunting prohibition enforcement.

es.description: El mono de nariz chata de Tonkín es un llamativo primate con una nariz respingona, encontrado solo en unos pocos parches de bosque fragmentados en el norte de Vietnam, con menos de 250 individuos estimados en la naturaleza.
es.endangerment: La caza para carne, la medicina tradicional y la deforestación para la agricultura itinerante han reducido esta especie a números críticamente pequeños.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque kárstico de piedra caliza y bosque tropical montano en el norte de Vietnam, particularmente en las reservas naturales de Na Hang y Khau Ca, con disponibilidad estacional de frutos y hojas.
es.actions: Apoya los santuarios del mono de nariz chata de Tonkín de Vietnam, financia la conservación comunitaria alrededor de Khau Ca y respalda la aplicación estricta de la prohibición de caza.

pt.description: O macaco-narigudo-de-tonquim é um primata marcante com nariz arrebitado, encontrado apenas em alguns fragmentos de floresta no norte do Vietnã, com menos de 250 indivíduos estimados na natureza.
pt.endangerment: A caça para carne, a medicina tradicional e o desmatamento para a agricultura itinerante reduziram esta espécie a números criticamente pequenos.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta cárstica de calcário e floresta tropical montana no norte do Vietnã, particularmente nas reservas naturais de Na Hang e Khau Ca, com disponibilidade sazonal de frutos e folhas.
pt.actions: Apoie os santuários do macaco-narigudo-de-tonquim do Vietnã, finance a conservação comunitária ao redor de Khau Ca e respalda a aplicação estrita da proibição de caça.

fr.description: Le rhinopithèque du Tonkin est un primate frappant au nez retroussé, trouvé uniquement dans quelques fragments de forêt dans le nord du Vietnam, avec moins de 250 individus estimés à l'état sauvage.
fr.endangerment: La chasse pour la viande, la médecine traditionnelle et la déforestation pour l'agriculture itinérante ont réduit cette espèce à des effectifs critiquement faibles.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt karstique calcaire et forêt tropicale montagnarde dans le nord du Vietnam, notamment dans les réserves naturelles de Na Hang et Khau Ca, avec une disponibilité saisonnière de fruits et de feuilles.
fr.actions: Soutenez les sanctuaires du rhinopithèque du Tonkin au Vietnam, financez la conservation communautaire autour de Khau Ca et appuyez l'application stricte de l'interdiction de chasse.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The riverine rabbit is one of Africa's most endangered mammals, restricted to the dense riverine scrub along seasonal rivers in South Africa's Karoo semi-desert.
endangerment: Conversion of riparian scrub to irrigated cropland has destroyed the specialised habitat this rabbit entirely depends on, leaving tiny fragmented populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Dense, tall riverine vegetation — particularly Karoo bush — along the banks of seasonal rivers in the central Karoo, South Africa, for shelter and breeding.
actions: Support the Endangered Wildlife Trust's riverine rabbit programme, fund riparian habitat restoration on private farms, and engage Karoo landowners in conservation stewardship.

en.description: The riverine rabbit is one of Africa's most endangered mammals, restricted to the dense riverine scrub along seasonal rivers in South Africa's Karoo semi-desert.
en.endangerment: Conversion of riparian scrub to irrigated cropland has destroyed the specialised habitat this rabbit entirely depends on, leaving tiny fragmented populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense, tall riverine vegetation — particularly Karoo bush — along the banks of seasonal rivers in the central Karoo, South Africa, for shelter and breeding.
en.actions: Support the Endangered Wildlife Trust's riverine rabbit programme, fund riparian habitat restoration on private farms, and engage Karoo landowners in conservation stewardship.

es.description: El conejo ribereño es uno de los mamíferos más amenazados de África, restringido al denso matorral ribereño a lo largo de ríos estacionales en el Karoo semidesértico de Sudáfrica.
es.endangerment: La conversión del arbusto ribereño en tierras de cultivo irrigadas ha destruido el hábitat especializado del que depende completamente este conejo, dejando pequeñas poblaciones fragmentadas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Vegetación ribereña densa y alta —particularmente arbustos del Karoo— a lo largo de las orillas de los ríos estacionales en el Karoo central, Sudáfrica, para refugio y reproducción.
es.actions: Apoya el programa de conejo ribereño del Endangered Wildlife Trust, financia la restauración del hábitat ribereño en granjas privadas y atrae a los propietarios de tierras del Karoo hacia la corresponsabilidad de conservación.

pt.description: O coelho-ribeirinho é um dos mamíferos mais ameaçados da África, restrito ao denso arbusto ribeirinho ao longo dos rios sazonais no Karoo semidesértico da África do Sul.
pt.endangerment: A conversão do arbusto ribeirinho em terras de cultivo irrigadas destruiu o habitat especializado do qual este coelho depende completamente, deixando populações fragmentadas minúsculas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Vegetação ribeirinha densa e alta — particularmente arbustos do Karoo — ao longo das margens dos rios sazonais no Karoo central, África do Sul, para abrigo e reprodução.
pt.actions: Apoie o programa de coelho-ribeirinho do Endangered Wildlife Trust, finance a restauração do habitat ribeirinho em fazendas privadas e envolva os proprietários de terras do Karoo na corresponsabilidade de conservação.

fr.description: Le lapin des rivières est l'un des mammifères les plus menacés d'Afrique, cantonné aux épaisses broussailles riveraines le long des rivières saisonnières dans le semi-désert de Karoo en Afrique du Sud.
fr.endangerment: La conversion des broussailles riveraines en terres cultivées irriguées a détruit l'habitat spécialisé dont ce lapin dépend entièrement, laissant de minuscules populations fragmentées.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une végétation riveraine dense et haute — notamment les broussailles du Karoo — le long des berges des rivières saisonnières dans le Karoo central, en Afrique du Sud, pour l'abri et la reproduction.
fr.actions: Soutenez le programme du lapin des rivières de l'Endangered Wildlife Trust, financez la restauration de l'habitat riverain sur les terres privées et engagez les propriétaires terriens du Karoo dans des accords de gestion pour la conservation.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Cuban solenodon is a small, venomous insectivore that is one of the most ancient surviving mammals in the Caribbean, resembling early Mesozoic insectivores from 76 million years ago.
endangerment: Introduced mongoose and cats prey on this ground-dwelling mammal that evolved without mammalian predators. Deforestation further shrinks its forest refuge.
ecosystemNeeds: Intact humid forest with deep leaf litter and soil for burrowing and foraging in eastern Cuba, particularly in Alejandro de Humboldt National Park.
actions: Support invasive predator control in key Cuban forest reserves, fund camera trap surveys to determine remaining population size, and back Cuba's national parks management.

en.description: The Cuban solenodon is a small, venomous insectivore that is one of the most ancient surviving mammals in the Caribbean, resembling early Mesozoic insectivores from 76 million years ago.
en.endangerment: Introduced mongoose and cats prey on this ground-dwelling mammal that evolved without mammalian predators. Deforestation further shrinks its forest refuge.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact humid forest with deep leaf litter and soil for burrowing and foraging in eastern Cuba, particularly in Alejandro de Humboldt National Park.
en.actions: Support invasive predator control in key Cuban forest reserves, fund camera trap surveys to determine remaining population size, and back Cuba's national parks management.

es.description: El solenodon cubano es un pequeño insectívoro venenoso que es uno de los mamíferos supervivientes más antiguos del Caribe, parecido a los insectívoros del Mesozoico de hace 76 millones de años.
es.endangerment: Las mangostas y los gatos introducidos depredan a este mamífero terrestre que evolucionó sin depredadores mamíferos. La deforestación reduce aún más su refugio forestal.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque húmedo intacto con hojarasca profunda y suelo para excavar y forrajear en el este de Cuba, particularmente en el Parque Nacional Alejandro de Humboldt.
es.actions: Apoya el control de depredadores invasores en las principales reservas forestales cubanas, financia estudios con cámaras trampa para determinar el tamaño de la población restante y respalda la gestión de los parques nacionales de Cuba.

pt.description: O solenodonte-cubano é um pequeno insectívoro venenoso que é um dos mamíferos sobreviventes mais antigos do Caribe, parecido com insectívoros mesozóicos de 76 milhões de anos atrás.
pt.endangerment: Mangustos e gatos introduzidos predam este mamífero terrestre que evoluiu sem predadores mamíferos. O desmatamento reduz ainda mais seu refúgio florestal.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta úmida intacta com folhagem profunda e solo para escavar e forragear no leste de Cuba, particularmente no Parque Nacional Alejandro de Humboldt.
pt.actions: Apoie o controle de predadores invasores nas principais reservas florestais cubanas, finance pesquisas com armadilhas fotográficas para determinar o tamanho da população restante e respalda a gestão dos parques nacionais de Cuba.

fr.description: Le solénodon de Cuba est un petit insectivore venimeux et l'un des mammifères survivants les plus anciens des Caraïbes, ressemblant aux insectivores mésozoïques vieux de 76 millions d'années.
fr.endangerment: Les mangoustes et les chats introduits prédatent ce mammifère terrestre qui a évolué sans prédateurs mammifères. La déforestation réduit encore davantage son refuge forestier.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une forêt humide intacte avec une litière profonde et un sol pour fouiller et fourrager dans l'est de Cuba, notamment dans le Parc national Alejandro de Humboldt.
fr.actions: Soutenez le contrôle des prédateurs envahissants dans les principales réserves forestières cubaines, financez des enquêtes par caméras-pièges pour déterminer la taille de la population restante et appuyez la gestion des parcs nationaux de Cuba.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Amami rabbit is a primitive, dark-furred rabbit restricted to two small Japanese islands — Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima — where it lives in dense subtropical forest and is considered a living fossil.
endangerment: Introduced mongooses, cats, and dogs prey heavily on this ground-dwelling rabbit; deforestation for forestry and development further reduces available habitat.
ecosystemNeeds: Dense subtropical forest with a closed canopy, leaf litter for foraging, and safe burrow sites on Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima islands, Japan.
actions: Support ongoing mongoose eradication on Amami-Oshima, fund feral cat and dog control programmes, and back forest protection on both islands.

en.description: The Amami rabbit is a primitive, dark-furred rabbit restricted to two small Japanese islands — Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima — where it lives in dense subtropical forest and is considered a living fossil.
en.endangerment: Introduced mongooses, cats, and dogs prey heavily on this ground-dwelling rabbit; deforestation for forestry and development further reduces available habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense subtropical forest with a closed canopy, leaf litter for foraging, and safe burrow sites on Amami-Oshima and Tokunoshima islands, Japan.
en.actions: Support ongoing mongoose eradication on Amami-Oshima, fund feral cat and dog control programmes, and back forest protection on both islands.

es.description: El conejo de Amami es un conejo primitivo y de pelaje oscuro restringido a dos pequeñas islas japonesas —Amami-Oshima y Tokunoshima— donde vive en denso bosque subtropical y es considerado un fósil viviente.
es.endangerment: Las mangostas, los gatos y los perros introducidos depredan fuertemente a este conejo terrestre; la deforestación para la silvicultura y el desarrollo reduce aún más el hábitat disponible.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque subtropical denso con dosel cerrado, hojarasca para forrajear y lugares de madriguera seguros en las islas Amami-Oshima y Tokunoshima, Japón.
es.actions: Apoya la erradicación continua de mangostas en Amami-Oshima, financia programas de control de gatos y perros salvajes y respalda la protección forestal en ambas islas.

pt.description: O coelho-de-amami é um coelho primitivo e de pelagem escura restrito a duas pequenas ilhas japonesas — Amami-Oshima e Tokunoshima — onde vive em densa floresta subtropical e é considerado um fóssil vivo.
pt.endangerment: Mangustos, gatos e cães introduzidos predam intensamente este coelho terrestre; o desmatamento para silvicultura e desenvolvimento reduz ainda mais o habitat disponível.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta subtropical densa com dossel fechado, folhagem para forrageamento e locais de toca seguros nas ilhas Amami-Oshima e Tokunoshima, Japão.
pt.actions: Apoie a erradicação contínua de mangustos em Amami-Oshima, finance programas de controle de gatos e cães selvagens e respalda a proteção florestal em ambas as ilhas.

fr.description: Le lapin d'Amami est un lapin primitif à la fourrure sombre cantonné à deux petites îles japonaises — Amami-Oshima et Tokunoshima — où il vit dans une forêt subtropicale dense et est considéré comme un fossile vivant.
fr.endangerment: Les mangoustes, chats et chiens introduits prédatent fortement ce lapin terrestre ; la déforestation pour la sylviculture et le développement réduit encore davantage l'habitat disponible.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une forêt subtropicale dense à canopée fermée, avec une litière pour le fourrage et des sites de terrier sûrs sur les îles d'Amami-Oshima et de Tokunoshima, au Japon.
fr.actions: Soutenez l'éradication continue des mangoustes à Amami-Oshima, financez des programmes de contrôle des chats et chiens sauvages et appuyez la protection forestière sur les deux îles.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Indus river dolphin is a nearly blind river dolphin found only in the lower Indus River of Pakistan, navigating by sophisticated echolocation in murky, sediment-rich waters.
endangerment: A series of barrages on the Indus have fragmented the population into isolated sub-populations. Bycatch, water diversion, and pollution add further pressure.
ecosystemNeeds: Deep, slow stretches of the lower Indus River between barrages in Sindh and Punjab, Pakistan, with adequate fish prey and low sediment contamination.
actions: Support WWF Pakistan's Indus dolphin programme, advocate for coordinated water releases to maintain minimum flow, and back bycatch reduction measures.

en.description: The Indus river dolphin is a nearly blind river dolphin found only in the lower Indus River of Pakistan, navigating by sophisticated echolocation in murky, sediment-rich waters.
en.endangerment: A series of barrages on the Indus have fragmented the population into isolated sub-populations. Bycatch, water diversion, and pollution add further pressure.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Deep, slow stretches of the lower Indus River between barrages in Sindh and Punjab, Pakistan, with adequate fish prey and low sediment contamination.
en.actions: Support WWF Pakistan's Indus dolphin programme, advocate for coordinated water releases to maintain minimum flow, and back bycatch reduction measures.

es.description: El delfín del río Indo es un delfín de río casi ciego que solo se encuentra en el bajo río Indo de Pakistán, navegando por ecolocalización sofisticada en aguas turbias y ricas en sedimentos.
es.endangerment: Una serie de embalses en el Indo han fragmentado la población en subpoblaciones aisladas. La captura incidental, el desvío de agua y la contaminación añaden presión adicional.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Tramos profundos y lentos del bajo río Indo entre embalses en Sindh y Punjab, Pakistán, con suficientes presas de peces y baja contaminación de sedimentos.
es.actions: Apoya el programa de delfines del Indo del WWF Pakistán, aboga por liberaciones coordinadas de agua para mantener el caudal mínimo y respalda medidas de reducción de la captura incidental.

pt.description: O boto-do-indo é um boto quase cego encontrado apenas no baixo Rio Indo do Paquistão, navegando por ecolocalização sofisticada em águas turvas e ricas em sedimentos.
pt.endangerment: Uma série de açudes no Indo fragmentou a população em subpopulações isoladas. A captura acidental, o desvio de água e a poluição acrescentam pressão adicional.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Trechos profundos e lentos do baixo Rio Indo entre açudes no Sindh e Punjab, Paquistão, com presas de peixes suficientes e baixa contaminação de sedimentos.
pt.actions: Apoie o programa de botos do Indo do WWF Paquistão, defenda liberações coordenadas de água para manter o fluxo mínimo e respalda medidas de redução da captura acidental.

fr.description: Le dauphin de l'Indus est un dauphin fluvial presque aveugle que l'on ne trouve que dans le bas Indus au Pakistan, naviguant par écholocation sophistiquée dans des eaux troubles et riches en sédiments.
fr.endangerment: Une série de barrages sur l'Indus a fragmenté la population en sous-populations isolées. Les prises accessoires, le détournement des eaux et la pollution ajoutent des pressions supplémentaires.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des tronçons profonds et lents du bas Indus entre les barrages au Sind et au Pendjab, Pakistan, avec une densité de poissons suffisante et une faible contamination sédimentaire.
fr.actions: Soutenez le programme de dauphins de l'Indus du WWF Pakistan, plaidez pour des lâchers d'eau coordonnés pour maintenir un débit minimum et appuyez les mesures de réduction des prises accessoires.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The wild water buffalo is the ancestor of all domestic water buffaloes, the largest bovine in Asia, now surviving in tiny populations in India, Nepal, Bhutan, and Thailand.
endangerment: Hybridisation with feral domestic buffaloes, disease transmission from livestock, habitat loss, and hunting have brought wild populations to fewer than 4,000.
ecosystemNeeds: Tall-grass floodplains, riverine forests, and marshy areas in South and Southeast Asia, with access to deep wallowing pools and minimal livestock encroachment.
actions: Support hybridisation control programmes, fund population surveys in Assam, advocate for livestock-exclusion zones in key wild buffalo habitats.

en.description: The wild water buffalo is the ancestor of all domestic water buffaloes, the largest bovine in Asia, now surviving in tiny populations in India, Nepal, Bhutan, and Thailand.
en.endangerment: Hybridisation with feral domestic buffaloes, disease transmission from livestock, habitat loss, and hunting have brought wild populations to fewer than 4,000.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Tall-grass floodplains, riverine forests, and marshy areas in South and Southeast Asia, with access to deep wallowing pools and minimal livestock encroachment.
en.actions: Support hybridisation control programmes, fund population surveys in Assam, advocate for livestock-exclusion zones in key wild buffalo habitats.

es.description: El búfalo de agua salvaje es el ancestro de todos los búfalos de agua domésticos, el mayor bovino de Asia, que ahora sobrevive en pequeñas poblaciones en India, Nepal, Bután y Tailandia.
es.endangerment: La hibridación con búfalos domésticos asilvestrados, la transmisión de enfermedades del ganado, la pérdida de hábitat y la caza han llevado las poblaciones silvestres a menos de 4.000.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Llanuras de hierba alta, bosques ribereños y áreas pantanosas en el sur y sureste asiático, con acceso a pozas de inmersión profundas y mínima invasión del ganado.
es.actions: Apoya los programas de control de hibridación, financia estudios de población en Assam y aboga por zonas de exclusión de ganado en hábitats clave de búfalo salvaje.

pt.description: O búfalo-d'água-selvagem é o ancestral de todos os búfalos-d'água domésticos, o maior bovino da Ásia, agora sobrevivendo em pequenas populações na Índia, Nepal, Butão e Tailândia.
pt.endangerment: A hibridização com búfalos domésticos asselvajados, a transmissão de doenças do gado, a perda de habitat e a caça reduziram as populações selvagens para menos de 4.000.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Planícies de gramíneas altas, florestas ribeirinhas e áreas pantanosas no sul e sudeste asiático, com acesso a poças de imersão profundas e invasão mínima do gado.
pt.actions: Apoie os programas de controle de hibridização, finance pesquisas de população em Assam e defenda zonas de exclusão de gado em habitats-chave de búfalo selvagem.

fr.description: Le buffle d'eau sauvage est l'ancêtre de tous les buffles d'eau domestiques, le plus grand bovidé d'Asie, survivant maintenant dans de petites populations en Inde, au Népal, au Bhoutan et en Thaïlande.
fr.endangerment: L'hybridation avec des buffles domestiques retournés à l'état sauvage, la transmission de maladies du bétail, la perte d'habitat et la chasse ont réduit les populations sauvages à moins de 4 000 individus.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des plaines d'herbes hautes, des forêts riveraines et des zones marécageuses en Asie du Sud et du Sud-Est, avec accès à des mares profondes et une invasion minimale du bétail.
fr.actions: Soutenez les programmes de contrôle de l'hybridation, financez des études sur les populations en Assam et plaidez pour des zones d'exclusion du bétail dans les habitats clés du buffle sauvage.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The La Plata dolphin, or franciscana, is the only river dolphin to live in saltwater, inhabiting shallow coastal and estuarine waters of southeastern South America.
endangerment: Incidental capture in gillnets set for sharks and other fish is the primary cause of death, with tens of thousands estimated killed over recent decades.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow (<30 m) coastal and estuarine waters along the Atlantic coast from Brazil to Argentina, with sandy or muddy bottoms and abundant small fish and squid.
actions: Support gillnet bycatch mitigation research, advocate for dolphin exclusion zones in key nursery areas, and back regional management agreements between Brazil, Uruguay, and Argentina.

en.description: The La Plata dolphin, or franciscana, is the only river dolphin to live in saltwater, inhabiting shallow coastal and estuarine waters of southeastern South America.
en.endangerment: Incidental capture in gillnets set for sharks and other fish is the primary cause of death, with tens of thousands estimated killed over recent decades.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow (<30 m) coastal and estuarine waters along the Atlantic coast from Brazil to Argentina, with sandy or muddy bottoms and abundant small fish and squid.
en.actions: Support gillnet bycatch mitigation research, advocate for dolphin exclusion zones in key nursery areas, and back regional management agreements between Brazil, Uruguay, and Argentina.

es.description: El delfín del Río de la Plata, o franciscana, es el único delfín de río que vive en agua salada, habitando las aguas costeras y de estuario poco profundas del sureste de América del Sur.
es.endangerment: La captura incidental en redes de enmalle tendidas para tiburones y otros peces es la principal causa de muerte, con decenas de miles estimados muertos en las últimas décadas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas costeras (<30 m) y de estuario poco profundas a lo largo de la costa atlántica desde Brasil hasta Argentina, con fondos arenosos o fangosos y abundantes peces y calamares pequeños.
es.actions: Apoya la investigación de mitigación de captura incidental con redes de enmalle, aboga por zonas de exclusión de delfines en áreas clave de crianza y respalda acuerdos de gestión regional entre Brasil, Uruguay y Argentina.

pt.description: O boto-da-plata, ou franciscana, é o único boto que vive em água salgada, habitando as águas costeiras e estuarinas rasas do sudeste da América do Sul.
pt.endangerment: A captura acidental em redes de emalhe colocadas para tubarões e outros peixes é a principal causa de morte, com dezenas de milhares estimados mortos nas últimas décadas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas costeiras (<30 m) e estuarinas rasas ao longo da costa atlântica do Brasil à Argentina, com fundos arenosos ou lamosos e peixes pequenos e lulas abundantes.
pt.actions: Apoie pesquisas de mitigação de captura acidental em redes de emalhe, defenda zonas de exclusão de botos em áreas-chave de criação e respalda acordos de gestão regional entre Brasil, Uruguai e Argentina.

fr.description: Le dauphin de La Plata, ou franciscaine, est le seul dauphin de rivière à vivre en eau salée, habitant les eaux côtières et estuariennes peu profondes du sud-est de l'Amérique du Sud.
fr.endangerment: Les prises accessoires dans les filets maillants tendus pour les requins et autres poissons sont la principale cause de mort, avec des dizaines de milliers tués au cours des dernières décennies.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des eaux côtières (<30 m) et estuariennes peu profondes le long de la côte atlantique du Brésil à l'Argentine, avec des fonds sableux ou boueux et une abondance de petits poissons et calmars.
fr.actions: Soutenez la recherche sur l'atténuation des prises accessoires dans les filets maillants, plaidez pour des zones d'exclusion des dauphins dans les zones clés de mise bas et appuyez les accords de gestion régionale entre le Brésil, l'Uruguay et l'Argentine.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The blue-eyed black lemur is one of only two non-human primates with blue eyes, endemic to a small area of northwestern Madagascar and exhibiting striking sexual dichromatism.
endangerment: Slash-and-burn agriculture has destroyed most of the humid forest it depends on; illegal capture for the pet trade and bushmeat hunting add further pressure.
ecosystemNeeds: Humid and dry forest fragments in the Sahamalaza Peninsula area of northwestern Madagascar, with fruiting trees, nectar sources, and forest edge habitat.
actions: Support Sahamalaza–Iles Radama National Park management, fund community reforestation and livelihood alternatives, and back ex-situ assurance colonies in zoos.

en.description: The blue-eyed black lemur is one of only two non-human primates with blue eyes, endemic to a small area of northwestern Madagascar and exhibiting striking sexual dichromatism.
en.endangerment: Slash-and-burn agriculture has destroyed most of the humid forest it depends on; illegal capture for the pet trade and bushmeat hunting add further pressure.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Humid and dry forest fragments in the Sahamalaza Peninsula area of northwestern Madagascar, with fruiting trees, nectar sources, and forest edge habitat.
en.actions: Support Sahamalaza–Iles Radama National Park management, fund community reforestation and livelihood alternatives, and back ex-situ assurance colonies in zoos.

es.description: El lemur negro de ojos azules es uno de los dos únicos primates no humanos con ojos azules, endémico de una pequeña área del noroeste de Madagascar y que exhibe un llamativo dicromatismo sexual.
es.endangerment: La agricultura de tala y quema ha destruido la mayor parte del bosque húmedo del que depende; la captura ilegal para el comercio de mascotas y la caza para carne añaden presión adicional.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Fragmentos de bosque húmedo y seco en el área de la Península de Sahamalaza en el noroeste de Madagascar, con árboles frutales, fuentes de néctar y hábitat de borde de bosque.
es.actions: Apoya la gestión del Parque Nacional Sahamalaza-Iles Radama, financia la reforestación comunitaria y alternativas de medios de vida, y respalda las colonias de seguro en cautividad en zoológicos.

pt.description: O lêmure-negro-de-olhos-azuis é um dos dois únicos primatas não humanos com olhos azuis, endêmico de uma pequena área do noroeste de Madagascar e exibindo um marcante dicromatismo sexual.
pt.endangerment: A agricultura de corte e queima destruiu a maior parte da floresta úmida da qual depende; a captura ilegal para o comércio de animais de estimação e a caça para carne acrescentam pressão adicional.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Fragmentos de floresta úmida e seca na área da Península de Sahamalaza no noroeste de Madagascar, com árvores frutíferas, fontes de néctar e habitat de borda de floresta.
pt.actions: Apoie a gestão do Parque Nacional Sahamalaza-Iles Radama, finance o reflorestamento comunitário e alternativas de meios de vida, e respalda as colônias de seguro em cativeiro em zoológicos.

fr.description: Le lémur noir aux yeux bleus est l'un des deux seuls primates non humains aux yeux bleus, endémique d'une petite zone du nord-ouest de Madagascar et présentant un frappant dichromatisme sexuel.
fr.endangerment: L'agriculture sur brûlis a détruit la majeure partie de la forêt humide dont il dépend ; la capture illégale pour le commerce des animaux de compagnie et la chasse pour la viande ajoutent des pressions supplémentaires.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des fragments de forêt humide et sèche dans la zone de la Péninsule de Sahamalaza dans le nord-ouest de Madagascar, avec des arbres fruitiers, des sources de nectar et un habitat de lisière forestière.
fr.actions: Soutenez la gestion du Parc national de Sahamalaza-Iles Radama, financez le reboisement communautaire et les alternatives de subsistance, et appuyez les colonies d'assurance en captivité dans les zoos.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Javan slow loris is a small nocturnal primate with a venomous bite — unique among primates — found only on Java, Indonesia, where it feeds on nectar, gum, and insects.
endangerment: Illegal capture for the exotic pet trade, fuelled by viral internet videos, is the primary threat. Deforestation of Java removes the forest habitat it requires.
ecosystemNeeds: Intact secondary and primary forest in West and Central Java at low to mid altitudes, with bamboo and flowering trees for nectar and gum feeding.
actions: Never share or encourage slow loris videos online, support Little Fireface Project's community-based conservation in Java, and back stricter trade enforcement.

en.description: The Javan slow loris is a small nocturnal primate with a venomous bite — unique among primates — found only on Java, Indonesia, where it feeds on nectar, gum, and insects.
en.endangerment: Illegal capture for the exotic pet trade, fuelled by viral internet videos, is the primary threat. Deforestation of Java removes the forest habitat it requires.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact secondary and primary forest in West and Central Java at low to mid altitudes, with bamboo and flowering trees for nectar and gum feeding.
en.actions: Never share or encourage slow loris videos online, support Little Fireface Project's community-based conservation in Java, and back stricter trade enforcement.

es.description: El lento loris de Java es un pequeño primate nocturno con una mordida venenosa —único entre los primates— que solo se encuentra en Java, Indonesia, donde se alimenta de néctar, goma e insectos.
es.endangerment: La captura ilegal para el comercio de mascotas exóticas, impulsada por videos virales de internet, es la principal amenaza. La deforestación de Java elimina el hábitat forestal que requiere.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque secundario y primario intacto en el oeste y centro de Java a bajas y medias altitudes, con bambú y árboles en flor para alimentarse de néctar y goma.
es.actions: Nunca compartas ni fomentes videos de loris lentos en línea, apoya el proyecto de conservación comunitaria Little Fireface en Java y respalda una aplicación más estricta del comercio.

pt.description: O loris-lento-de-java é um pequeno primata noturno com uma mordida venenosa — único entre os primatas — encontrado apenas em Java, Indonésia, onde se alimenta de néctar, goma e insetos.
pt.endangerment: A captura ilegal para o comércio de animais de estimação exóticos, impulsionada por vídeos virais na internet, é a principal ameaça. O desmatamento de Java elimina o habitat florestal que necessita.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta secundária e primária intacta no oeste e centro de Java em baixas a médias altitudes, com bambu e árvores em flor para alimentação de néctar e goma.
pt.actions: Nunca compartilhe nem incentive vídeos de loris-lentos online, apoie o Projeto Little Fireface de conservação comunitária em Java e respalda uma aplicação comercial mais rigorosa.

fr.description: Le lori lent de Java est un petit primate nocturne à la morsure venimeuse — unique parmi les primates — que l'on ne trouve qu'à Java, en Indonésie, où il se nourrit de nectar, de gomme et d'insectes.
fr.endangerment: La capture illégale pour le commerce des animaux de compagnie exotiques, alimentée par des vidéos virales sur internet, est la principale menace. La déforestation de Java supprime l'habitat forestier dont il a besoin.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt secondaire et primaire intacte dans l'ouest et le centre de Java à basse et moyenne altitude, avec du bambou et des arbres en fleurs pour se nourrir de nectar et de gomme.
fr.actions: Ne partagez jamais ni n'encouragez les vidéos de loris lents en ligne, soutenez le projet Little Fireface de conservation communautaire à Java et plaidez pour une application plus stricte des lois commerciales.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Arabian leopard is the smallest leopard subspecies, a pale, elusive predator surviving in the rugged mountains and escarpments of the Arabian Peninsula in tiny numbers.
endangerment: Decline of prey animals due to poaching, direct killing by herders to protect livestock, and habitat disturbance have reduced the wild population to under 200 individuals.
ecosystemNeeds: Steep rocky wadis and mountainous terrain in Oman, Yemen, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE, with sufficient prey such as Arabian gazelle and ibex.
actions: Support Oman's Arabian Leopard Programme and Yemen conservation initiatives, advocate for prey species protection, and back captive breeding efforts at regional facilities.

en.description: The Arabian leopard is the smallest leopard subspecies, a pale, elusive predator surviving in the rugged mountains and escarpments of the Arabian Peninsula in tiny numbers.
en.endangerment: Decline of prey animals due to poaching, direct killing by herders to protect livestock, and habitat disturbance have reduced the wild population to under 200 individuals.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Steep rocky wadis and mountainous terrain in Oman, Yemen, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE, with sufficient prey such as Arabian gazelle and ibex.
en.actions: Support Oman's Arabian Leopard Programme and Yemen conservation initiatives, advocate for prey species protection, and back captive breeding efforts at regional facilities.

es.description: El leopardo árabe es la subespecie de leopardo más pequeña, un depredador pálido y esquivo que sobrevive en las montañas y escarpes rugosos de la Península Arábiga en números ínfimos.
es.endangerment: El declive de los animales presa por la caza, la muerte directa por parte de pastores para proteger el ganado y la perturbación del hábitat han reducido la población silvestre a menos de 200 individuos.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Wadis rocosos y abruptos y terreno montañoso en Omán, Yemen, Arabia Saudita y los Emiratos Árabes Unidos, con presas suficientes como la gacela árabe y el íbice.
es.actions: Apoya el Programa del Leopardo Árabe de Omán y las iniciativas de conservación de Yemen, aboga por la protección de las especies presa y respalda los esfuerzos de cría en cautividad en instalaciones regionales.

pt.description: O leopardo-árabe é a menor subespécie de leopardo, um predador pálido e elusivo que sobrevive nas montanhas rugosas e escarpas da Península Arábica em números ínfimos.
pt.endangerment: O declínio dos animais presa pela caça, a morte direta por pastores para proteger o gado e a perturbação do habitat reduziram a população selvagem para menos de 200 indivíduos.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Wadis rochosos e íngremes e terreno montanhoso em Omã, Iêmen, Arábia Saudita e Emirados Árabes Unidos, com presas suficientes como a gazela-árabe e o íbex.
pt.actions: Apoie o Programa do Leopardo Árabe de Omã e as iniciativas de conservação do Iêmen, defenda a proteção das espécies presa e respalda os esforços de criação em cativeiro em instalações regionais.

fr.description: Le léopard d'Arabie est la plus petite sous-espèce de léopard, un prédateur pâle et insaisissable survivant dans les montagnes et escarpements accidentés de la péninsule Arabique en très petit nombre.
fr.endangerment: Le déclin des proies dû au braconnage, la mise à mort directe par les éleveurs pour protéger le bétail et les perturbations de l'habitat ont réduit la population sauvage à moins de 200 individus.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des wadis rocheux et escarpés et un terrain montagneux à Oman, au Yémen, en Arabie saoudite et aux Émirats arabes unis, avec des proies suffisantes telles que la gazelle d'Arabie et l'ibex.
fr.actions: Soutenez le Programme du Léopard d'Arabie d'Oman et les initiatives de conservation au Yémen, plaidez pour la protection des espèces proies et appuyez les efforts d'élevage en captivité dans les établissements régionaux.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The blue-throated macaw is a strikingly beautiful parrot restricted to the Beni savanna of northern Bolivia, nesting in natural cavities in isolated Motacú palm groves.
endangerment: Trapping for the pet trade decimated populations in past decades; today habitat loss from cattle ranching and lack of suitable nest trees remain the primary threats.
ecosystemNeeds: Seasonally flooded savanna in the Llanos de Moxos, Bolivia, with scattered stands of large Motacú palms and sufficient fruit-bearing trees for foraging.
actions: Support nest box installation programmes in Beni, back Bolivian wildlife trafficking enforcement, and donate to Armonia's Blue-throated Macaw Project.

en.description: The blue-throated macaw is a strikingly beautiful parrot restricted to the Beni savanna of northern Bolivia, nesting in natural cavities in isolated Motacú palm groves.
en.endangerment: Trapping for the pet trade decimated populations in past decades; today habitat loss from cattle ranching and lack of suitable nest trees remain the primary threats.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Seasonally flooded savanna in the Llanos de Moxos, Bolivia, with scattered stands of large Motacú palms and sufficient fruit-bearing trees for foraging.
en.actions: Support nest box installation programmes in Beni, back Bolivian wildlife trafficking enforcement, and donate to Armonia's Blue-throated Macaw Project.

es.description: La guacamaya de garganta azul es un loro de belleza llamativa restringido a la sabana Beni del norte de Bolivia, que anida en cavidades naturales en grupos aislados de palmas Motacú.
es.endangerment: La captura para el comercio de mascotas diezmó las poblaciones en décadas pasadas; hoy la pérdida de hábitat por la ganadería y la falta de árboles de nidificación adecuados siguen siendo las principales amenazas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Sabana estacionalmente inundada en los Llanos de Moxos, Bolivia, con grupos dispersos de grandes palmas Motacú y árboles suficientes con frutos para forrajear.
es.actions: Apoya los programas de instalación de cajas nido en Beni, respalda la aplicación de la legislación sobre tráfico de fauna silvestre boliviana y dona al Proyecto Guacamaya de Garganta Azul de Armonía.

pt.description: A arara-de-garganta-azul é um papagaio de beleza chamativa restrito à savana Beni do norte da Bolívia, aninhando em cavidades naturais em grupos isolados de palmeiras Motacú.
pt.endangerment: A captura para o comércio de animais de estimação dizimou as populações em décadas passadas; hoje a perda de habitat pelo rancho de gado e a falta de árvores de nidificação adequadas permanecem as principais ameaças.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Savana sazonalmente inundada nos Llanos de Moxos, Bolívia, com grupos dispersos de grandes palmeiras Motacú e árvores suficientes com frutos para forrageamento.
pt.actions: Apoie os programas de instalação de caixas-ninho no Beni, respalda a aplicação da legislação boliviana de tráfico de fauna selvagem e doe ao Projeto Arara-de-garganta-azul da Armonía.

fr.description: L'ara à gorge bleue est un perroquet d'une beauté frappante cantonné à la savane du Beni dans le nord de la Bolivie, nichant dans des cavités naturelles dans des groupes isolés de palmiers Motacú.
fr.endangerment: Le piégeage pour le commerce des animaux de compagnie a décimé les populations dans les décennies passées ; aujourd'hui, la perte d'habitat due à l'élevage de bétail et le manque d'arbres à nicher adéquats restent les principales menaces.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Savane saisonnièrement inondée dans les Llanos de Moxos, en Bolivie, avec des groupes dispersés de grands palmiers Motacú et suffisamment d'arbres fruitiers pour le fourrage.
fr.actions: Soutenez les programmes d'installation de nichoirs au Beni, appuyez l'application des lois boliviennes sur le trafic d'espèces sauvages et faites des dons au Projet de l'ara à gorge bleue d'Armonía.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Bengal florican is a critically endangered bustard of tall-grass floodplain habitats, known for the dramatic aerial display of males during breeding season.
endangerment: Conversion of floodplain grasslands to agriculture, especially rice paddies, across its range in India, Nepal, and Cambodia has eliminated most habitat.
ecosystemNeeds: Tall, seasonally flooded grasslands and alluvial plains near major rivers, particularly in Kaziranga, Dudhwa, and the Cambodian Mekong floodplains.
actions: Support grassland management in Kaziranga and Dudhwa, fund Cambodia's florican grassland conservation, and back eco-friendly agricultural subsidies for landowners near habitats.

en.description: The Bengal florican is a critically endangered bustard of tall-grass floodplain habitats, known for the dramatic aerial display of males during breeding season.
en.endangerment: Conversion of floodplain grasslands to agriculture, especially rice paddies, across its range in India, Nepal, and Cambodia has eliminated most habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Tall, seasonally flooded grasslands and alluvial plains near major rivers, particularly in Kaziranga, Dudhwa, and the Cambodian Mekong floodplains.
en.actions: Support grassland management in Kaziranga and Dudhwa, fund Cambodia's florican grassland conservation, and back eco-friendly agricultural subsidies for landowners near habitats.

es.description: El florícano de Bengala es una avutarda críticamente amenazada de hábitats de llanura aluvial de pastizales altos, conocida por el dramático despliegue aéreo de los machos durante la temporada de cría.
es.endangerment: La conversión de pastizales aluviales a agricultura, especialmente arrozales, en toda su área en India, Nepal y Camboya ha eliminado la mayor parte del hábitat.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Pastizales altos, periódicamente inundados y llanuras aluviales cerca de grandes ríos, particularmente en Kaziranga, Dudhwa y las llanuras aluviales del Mekong en Camboya.
es.actions: Apoyar la gestión de pastizales en Kaziranga y Dudhwa, financiar la conservación de pastizales del florícano en Camboya y respaldar subsidios agrícolas ecológicos para propietarios de tierras cerca de hábitats.

pt.description: O florican-de-bengala é uma abetarda criticamente ameaçada de habitats de planície aluvial de pastagens altas, conhecida pelo dramático exibição aérea dos machos durante a temporada de reprodução.
pt.endangerment: A conversão de pastagens aluviais para agricultura, especialmente arrozais, em toda a sua área na Índia, Nepal e Camboja eliminou a maior parte do habitat.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pastagens altas, sazonalmente inundadas e planícies aluviais perto de grandes rios, particularmente em Kaziranga, Dudhwa e as planícies aluviais do Mekong no Camboja.
pt.actions: Apoiar o manejo de pastagens em Kaziranga e Dudhwa, financiar a conservação de pastagens do florican no Camboja e apoiar subsídios agrícolas ecológicos para proprietários de terras perto de habitats.

fr.description: L'outarde du Bengale est une outarde en danger critique de disparition des habitats de prairies hautes des plaines alluviales, connue pour les spectaculaires parades aériennes des mâles pendant la saison de reproduction.
fr.endangerment: La conversion des prairies alluviales en terres agricoles, notamment en rizières, dans toute son aire en Inde, au Népal et au Cambodge a éliminé la majeure partie de l'habitat.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Hautes prairies inondées saisonnièrement et plaines alluviales proches des grands fleuves, notamment à Kaziranga, Dudhwa et dans les plaines alluviales du Mékong au Cambodge.
fr.actions: Soutenir la gestion des prairies à Kaziranga et Dudhwa, financer la conservation des prairies à outardes au Cambodge et promouvoir des subventions agricoles écologiques pour les propriétaires terriens près des habitats.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The sociable lapwing is a migratory wading bird that breeds on Kazakhstan's dry steppes and winters in the Middle East, South Asia, and Northeast Africa.
endangerment: Rapid conversion of semi-arid steppe to cropland has reduced breeding habitat; hunting pressure on wintering grounds in Sudan and Syria adds further mortality.
ecosystemNeeds: Short-grass semi-arid steppe with bare patches for breeding in Kazakhstan, and open short-grass plains and fallow fields on wintering grounds.
actions: Support steppe protection in Kazakhstan, campaign against hunting of migratory birds in Sudan and Syria, and back satellite tracking programmes to identify critical sites.

en.description: The sociable lapwing is a migratory wading bird that breeds on Kazakhstan's dry steppes and winters in the Middle East, South Asia, and Northeast Africa.
en.endangerment: Rapid conversion of semi-arid steppe to cropland has reduced breeding habitat; hunting pressure on wintering grounds in Sudan and Syria adds further mortality.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Short-grass semi-arid steppe with bare patches for breeding in Kazakhstan, and open short-grass plains and fallow fields on wintering grounds.
en.actions: Support steppe protection in Kazakhstan, campaign against hunting of migratory birds in Sudan and Syria, and back satellite tracking programmes to identify critical sites.

es.description: El avefría sociable es un ave zancuda migratoria que cría en las estepas secas de Kazajistán e inverna en Oriente Medio, Asia del Sur y el noreste de África.
es.endangerment: La rápida conversión de estepa semiárida a tierras de cultivo ha reducido el hábitat de cría; la presión de caza en las zonas de invernada en Sudán y Siria añade mortalidad adicional.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Estepa semiárida de hierba corta con parches descubiertos para la cría en Kazajistán, y llanuras abiertas de hierba corta y campos en barbecho en las zonas de invernada.
es.actions: Apoyar la protección de la estepa en Kazajistán, hacer campaña contra la caza de aves migratorias en Sudán y Siria y respaldar programas de seguimiento por satélite para identificar sitios críticos.

pt.description: O abibe-sociável é uma ave limícola migratória que se reproduz nas estepes secas do Cazaquistão e inverna no Oriente Médio, Sul da Ásia e Nordeste da África.
pt.endangerment: A rápida conversão de estepe semiárida em terras agrícolas reduziu o habitat de reprodução; a pressão de caça nas áreas de invernada no Sudão e na Síria acrescenta mortalidade adicional.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Estepe semiárida de ervas curtas com manchas descobertas para reprodução no Cazaquistão, e planícies abertas de ervas curtas e campos em pousio nas áreas de invernada.
pt.actions: Apoiar a proteção das estepes no Cazaquistão, fazer campanha contra a caça de aves migratórias no Sudão e na Síria e apoiar programas de rastreamento por satélite para identificar locais críticos.

fr.description: Le vanneau sociable est un limicole migrateur qui se reproduit dans les steppes sèches du Kazakhstan et hiverne au Moyen-Orient, en Asie du Sud et en Afrique du Nord-Est.
fr.endangerment: La conversion rapide des steppes semi-arides en terres agricoles a réduit l'habitat de reproduction ; la pression de chasse sur les zones d'hivernage au Soudan et en Syrie ajoute une mortalité supplémentaire.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Steppe semi-aride à herbes courtes avec des zones dénudées pour la reproduction au Kazakhstan, et plaines ouvertes à herbes courtes et champs en jachère sur les zones d'hivernage.
fr.actions: Soutenir la protection des steppes au Kazakhstan, lutter contre la chasse des oiseaux migrateurs au Soudan et en Syrie et soutenir les programmes de suivi par satellite pour identifier les sites critiques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The hooded grebe is a stunning black-and-white diving bird breeding only on the remote basalt plateau lakes of Patagonia, Argentina, and wintering on coastal channels of Tierra del Fuego.
endangerment: Introduced trout, mink, and kelp gulls prey on eggs and chicks. American mink have spread to breeding lakes, causing catastrophic breeding failures since the 1980s.
ecosystemNeeds: Remote, shallow, alkaline lakes on the Santa Cruz basalt plateau with emergent aquatic vegetation for nest building and abundant small aquatic invertebrates for feeding.
actions: Support mink eradication at key breeding lakes, fund nesting raft programmes to protect nests from gulls, and back WCS Argentina's hooded grebe monitoring.

en.description: The hooded grebe is a stunning black-and-white diving bird breeding only on the remote basalt plateau lakes of Patagonia, Argentina, and wintering on coastal channels of Tierra del Fuego.
en.endangerment: Introduced trout, mink, and kelp gulls prey on eggs and chicks. American mink have spread to breeding lakes, causing catastrophic breeding failures since the 1980s.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Remote, shallow, alkaline lakes on the Santa Cruz basalt plateau with emergent aquatic vegetation for nest building and abundant small aquatic invertebrates for feeding.
en.actions: Support mink eradication at key breeding lakes, fund nesting raft programmes to protect nests from gulls, and back WCS Argentina's hooded grebe monitoring.

es.description: El macá tobiano es una impresionante ave zambullidora en blanco y negro que cría solo en los remotos lagos del plateau basáltico de la Patagonia, Argentina, e inverna en los canales costeros de Tierra del Fuego.
