fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt montagnarde et côtière humide à la Dominique et à Montserrat avec des ruisseaux d'eau douce et des terriers dans le sol pour la couvaison, sans sources d'eau contaminées par le chytride.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme en captivité du Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust, financer la surveillance du chytride et les essais de biocontrôle et soutenir la protection de l'habitat sur les deux îles.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Hochstetter's frog is one of the world's most primitive frogs, a small amphibian found in seeps and wet forest in the North Island of New Zealand, with ancient lineage unchanged for 150 million years.
endangerment: Introduced rats, stoats, and cats prey on frogs and eggs; land clearing has reduced wetland and stream habitat across the North Island.
ecosystemNeeds: Cold, clear seeps and streams within moist native forest in the North Island of New Zealand, with moss-covered rocks for shelter and breeding.
actions: Support predator control in New Zealand's native forest reserves, fund DOC's leiopelmatid frog monitoring, and back wetland and stream habitat restoration.

en.description: Hochstetter's frog is one of the world's most primitive frogs, a small amphibian found in seeps and wet forest in the North Island of New Zealand, with ancient lineage unchanged for 150 million years.
en.endangerment: Introduced rats, stoats, and cats prey on frogs and eggs; land clearing has reduced wetland and stream habitat across the North Island.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Cold, clear seeps and streams within moist native forest in the North Island of New Zealand, with moss-covered rocks for shelter and breeding.
en.actions: Support predator control in New Zealand's native forest reserves, fund DOC's leiopelmatid frog monitoring, and back wetland and stream habitat restoration.

es.description: La rana de Hochstetter es una de las ranas más primitivas del mundo, un pequeño anfibio encontrado en manantiales y bosques húmedos en la Isla Norte de Nueva Zelanda, con un linaje ancestral sin cambios durante 150 millones de años.
es.endangerment: Las ratas, armiños y gatos introducidos depredan ranas y huevos; la tala de tierras ha reducido el hábitat de humedales y arroyos en toda la Isla Norte.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Manantiales fríos y claros y arroyos dentro del bosque nativo húmedo en la Isla Norte de Nueva Zelanda, con rocas cubiertas de musgo para refugio y reproducción.
es.actions: Apoyar el control de depredadores en las reservas forestales nativas de Nueva Zelanda, financiar el monitoreo de ranas leiopelmatidas del DOC y respaldar la restauración del hábitat de humedales y arroyos.

pt.description: A rã-de-Hochstetter é uma das rãs mais primitivas do mundo, um pequeno anfíbio encontrado em nascentes e florestas úmidas na Ilha Norte da Nova Zelândia, com linhagem ancestral inalterada por 150 milhões de anos.
pt.endangerment: Ratos, arminhos e gatos introduzidos predam rãs e ovos; o desmatamento reduziu o habitat de zonas úmidas e córregos em toda a Ilha Norte.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Nascentes frias e claras e riachos dentro de florestas nativas úmidas na Ilha Norte da Nova Zelândia, com rochas cobertas de musgo para abrigo e reprodução.
pt.actions: Apoiar o controle de predadores nas reservas florestais nativas da Nova Zelândia, financiar o monitoramento de rãs leiopelmatidas do DOC e apoiar a restauração do habitat de zonas úmidas e córregos.

fr.description: La grenouille de Hochstetter est l'une des grenouilles les plus primitives du monde, un petit amphibien trouvé dans les suintements et les forêts humides de l'île du Nord de la Nouvelle-Zélande, avec une lignée ancestrale inchangée depuis 150 millions d'années.
fr.endangerment: Les rats, hermines et chats introduits prédatent les grenouilles et les œufs ; le défrichement des terres a réduit l'habitat des zones humides et des ruisseaux dans toute l'île du Nord.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Suintements froids et clairs et ruisseaux au sein de forêts indigènes humides de l'île du Nord de la Nouvelle-Zélande, avec des roches couvertes de mousse pour l'abri et la reproduction.
fr.actions: Soutenir le contrôle des prédateurs dans les réserves forestières indigènes de Nouvelle-Zélande, financer le suivi des grenouilles léiopelmatidées du DOC et soutenir la restauration de l'habitat des zones humides et des ruisseaux.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The phantasmal poison frog is a small, vividly striped Ecuadorian frog whose skin toxins led to the development of a powerful pain-relieving compound — epibatidine — by pharmaceutical researchers.
endangerment: Deforestation for agriculture in the Andean foothills of Bolívar province, Ecuador, combined with chytrid disease, has fragmented and reduced populations significantly.
ecosystemNeeds: Humid premontane and montane forest along the Andean foothills of west-central Ecuador, with rocky stream banks and leaf litter for shelter and breeding.
actions: Support cloud forest conservation in Bolívar province, fund population monitoring, and advocate for Ecuador's protected area expansion in Andean foothills.

en.description: The phantasmal poison frog is a small, vividly striped Ecuadorian frog whose skin toxins led to the development of a powerful pain-relieving compound — epibatidine — by pharmaceutical researchers.
en.endangerment: Deforestation for agriculture in the Andean foothills of Bolívar province, Ecuador, combined with chytrid disease, has fragmented and reduced populations significantly.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Humid premontane and montane forest along the Andean foothills of west-central Ecuador, with rocky stream banks and leaf litter for shelter and breeding.
en.actions: Support cloud forest conservation in Bolívar province, fund population monitoring, and advocate for Ecuador's protected area expansion in Andean foothills.

es.description: La rana venenosa fantasmal es una pequeña rana ecuatoriana vivamente rayada cuyas toxinas en la piel llevaron al desarrollo de un potente compuesto analgésico — epibatidina — por investigadores farmacéuticos.
es.endangerment: La deforestación para agricultura en las estribaciones andinas de la provincia de Bolívar, Ecuador, combinada con la enfermedad del quitridio, ha fragmentado y reducido significativamente las poblaciones.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque húmedo premontano y montano a lo largo de las estribaciones andinas del centro-oeste de Ecuador, con orillas de arroyos rocosos y hojarasca para refugio y reproducción.
es.actions: Apoyar la conservación del bosque nuboso en la provincia de Bolívar, financiar el monitoreo de poblaciones y abogar por la expansión de áreas protegidas en las estribaciones andinas de Ecuador.

pt.description: A rã-venenosa-fantasmal é uma pequena rã equatoriana vividamente listrada cujas toxinas na pele levaram ao desenvolvimento de um potente composto analgésico — epibaticina — por pesquisadores farmacêuticos.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento para agricultura nas encostas andinas da província de Bolívar, Equador, combinado com a doença do quitrídio, fragmentou e reduziu significativamente as populações.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta úmida pré-montana e montana ao longo das encostas andinas do centro-oeste do Equador, com margens de córregos rochosos e serapilheira para abrigo e reprodução.
pt.actions: Apoiar a conservação da floresta nebular na província de Bolívar, financiar o monitoramento de populações e defender a expansão de áreas protegidas nas encostas andinas do Equador.

fr.description: La grenouille venimeuse fantôme est une petite grenouille équatorienne vivement rayée dont les toxines cutanées ont conduit au développement d'un puissant analgésique — l'épibatidine — par des chercheurs pharmaceutiques.
fr.endangerment: La déforestation pour l'agriculture dans les contreforts andins de la province de Bolívar, en Équateur, combinée à la maladie chytride, a fragmenté et réduit significativement les populations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt humide pré-montagnarde et montagnarde le long des contreforts andins du centre-ouest de l'Équateur, avec des berges de ruisseaux rocailleuses et de la litière de feuilles pour l'abri et la reproduction.
fr.actions: Soutenir la conservation de la forêt nuageuse dans la province de Bolívar, financer le suivi des populations et plaider pour l'expansion des zones protégées dans les contreforts andins de l'Équateur.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Baw Baw frog is a small, secretive frog found only on the Baw Baw plateau in Victoria, Australia, where it breeds in sphagnum bogs and cool seeps under forest litter.
endangerment: Chytrid fungus has caused catastrophic population collapse; climate change is warming and drying the montane habitat; introduced deer and pigs trample sphagnum bog habitat.
ecosystemNeeds: Cool, moist sphagnum bogs, seeps, and wet forest floor on the Baw Baw plateau above 1,000 metres, with stable cool temperatures and intact native vegetation.
actions: Support Parks Victoria's captive breeding and release programme, fund chytrid management trials, and back climate adaptation measures for montane ecosystems in Victoria.

en.description: The Baw Baw frog is a small, secretive frog found only on the Baw Baw plateau in Victoria, Australia, where it breeds in sphagnum bogs and cool seeps under forest litter.
en.endangerment: Chytrid fungus has caused catastrophic population collapse; climate change is warming and drying the montane habitat; introduced deer and pigs trample sphagnum bog habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Cool, moist sphagnum bogs, seeps, and wet forest floor on the Baw Baw plateau above 1,000 metres, with stable cool temperatures and intact native vegetation.
en.actions: Support Parks Victoria's captive breeding and release programme, fund chytrid management trials, and back climate adaptation measures for montane ecosystems in Victoria.

es.description: La rana Baw Baw es una pequeña rana sigilosa que solo se encuentra en el plateau Baw Baw en Victoria, Australia, donde se reproduce en turberas de sfagno y rezumaderos frescos bajo la hojarasca del bosque.
es.endangerment: El hongo quitridio ha causado un colapso catastrófico de la población; el cambio climático está calentando y secando el hábitat montano; los ciervos y cerdos introducidos pisotean el hábitat de la turbera de sfagno.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Turberas de sfagno frescas y húmedas, rezumaderos y suelo húmedo de bosque en el plateau Baw Baw por encima de 1.000 metros, con temperaturas frescas estables y vegetación nativa intacta.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría en cautiverio y liberación de Parks Victoria, financiar ensayos de gestión del quitridio y respaldar medidas de adaptación climática para ecosistemas montanos en Victoria.

pt.description: A rã-de-baw-baw é uma pequena rã discreta encontrada apenas no platô Baw Baw em Victoria, Austrália, onde se reproduz em turfeiras de esfagno e nascentes frescas sob a serapilheira da floresta.
pt.endangerment: O fungo quitrídio causou um colapso catastrófico da população; as mudanças climáticas estão aquecendo e secando o habitat montano; veados e porcos introduzidos pisoteiam o habitat da turfeira de esfagno.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Turfeiras de esfagno frescas e úmidas, nascentes e solo de floresta úmido no platô Baw Baw acima de 1.000 metros, com temperaturas frescas estáveis e vegetação nativa intacta.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro e soltura do Parks Victoria, financiar ensaios de manejo do quitrídio e apoiar medidas de adaptação climática para ecossistemas montanos em Victoria.

fr.description: La grenouille de Baw Baw est une petite grenouille discrète trouvée uniquement sur le plateau de Baw Baw à Victoria, en Australie, où elle se reproduit dans des tourbières à sphaignes et des suintements frais sous la litière forestière.
fr.endangerment: Le champignon chytride a provoqué un effondrement catastrophique de la population ; le changement climatique réchauffe et assèche l'habitat montagnard ; les cerfs et sangliers introduits piétinent l'habitat des tourbières à sphaignes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Tourbières à sphaignes fraîches et humides, suintements et sol forestier humide sur le plateau de Baw Baw au-dessus de 1 000 mètres, avec des températures fraîches stables et une végétation indigène intacte.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage en captivité et de relâcher de Parks Victoria, financer des essais de gestion du chytride et soutenir les mesures d'adaptation climatique pour les écosystèmes montagnards de Victoria.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The armoured mistfrog is a small rock-dwelling frog from fast-flowing rainforest streams in the Wet Tropics of Queensland, Australia, not seen since 1991 and possibly extinct in the wild.
endangerment: The chytrid fungus swept through Wet Tropics stream frogs in the late 1980s with devastating speed, eliminating numerous species; no confirmed wild sightings have been made since.
ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear rocky streams in intact upland tropical rainforest in the Wet Tropics of northeastern Queensland, above 400 metres elevation.
actions: Support the James Cook University amphibian rescue programme, fund environmental DNA surveys to detect any surviving populations, and back Wet Tropics catchment protection.

en.description: The armoured mistfrog is a small rock-dwelling frog from fast-flowing rainforest streams in the Wet Tropics of Queensland, Australia, not seen since 1991 and possibly extinct in the wild.
en.endangerment: The chytrid fungus swept through Wet Tropics stream frogs in the late 1980s with devastating speed, eliminating numerous species; no confirmed wild sightings have been made since.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear rocky streams in intact upland tropical rainforest in the Wet Tropics of northeastern Queensland, above 400 metres elevation.
en.actions: Support the James Cook University amphibian rescue programme, fund environmental DNA surveys to detect any surviving populations, and back Wet Tropics catchment protection.

es.description: La rana de la niebla acorazada es una pequeña rana rupícola de los arroyos de selva tropical de rápido flujo en los Trópicos Húmedos de Queensland, Australia, no vista desde 1991 y posiblemente extinta en la naturaleza.
es.endangerment: El hongo quitridio se propagó por las ranas de los arroyos de los Trópicos Húmedos a fines de los años 80 con devastadora velocidad, eliminando numerosas especies; no se han realizado avistamientos silvestres confirmados desde entonces.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arroyos rocosos de flujo rápido y claros en selva tropical de tierras altas intacta en los Trópicos Húmedos del noreste de Queensland, por encima de 400 metros de altitud.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de rescate de anfibios de la Universidad James Cook, financiar encuestas de ADN ambiental para detectar poblaciones supervivientes y respaldar la protección de la cuenca de los Trópicos Húmedos.

pt.description: A rã-da-névoa-encouraçada é uma pequena rã rupícola de riachos de floresta tropical de fluxo rápido nos Trópicos Úmidos de Queensland, Austrália, não vista desde 1991 e possivelmente extinta na natureza.
pt.endangerment: O fungo quitrídio varreu as rãs de córregos dos Trópicos Úmidos no final dos anos 1980 com velocidade devastadora, eliminando numerosas espécies; nenhum avistamento selvagem confirmado foi feito desde então.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Riachos rochosos de fluxo rápido e claros em floresta tropical de terras altas intacta nos Trópicos Úmidos do nordeste de Queensland, acima de 400 metros de altitude.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de resgate de anfíbios da Universidade James Cook, financiar levantamentos de DNA ambiental para detectar populações sobreviventes e apoiar a proteção da bacia dos Trópicos Úmidos.

fr.description: La grenouille de brume cuirassée est une petite grenouille rupicole des ruisseaux de forêt tropicale à courant rapide dans les Tropiques Humides du Queensland, en Australie, non observée depuis 1991 et peut-être éteinte à l'état sauvage.
fr.endangerment: Le champignon chytride a balayé les grenouilles des ruisseaux des Tropiques Humides à la fin des années 1980 avec une vitesse dévastatrice, éliminant de nombreuses espèces ; aucune observation sauvage confirmée n'a été faite depuis.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Ruisseaux rocheux à courant rapide et clairs dans une forêt tropicale de haute altitude intacte dans les Tropiques Humides du nord-est du Queensland, au-dessus de 400 mètres d'altitude.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de sauvetage des amphibiens de l'Université James Cook, financer des enquêtes d'ADN environnemental pour détecter les populations survivantes et soutenir la protection du bassin versant des Tropiques Humides.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Betic midwife toad is a small, terrestrial toad found only in the Betic mountain ranges of southeastern Spain, where males famously carry the egg strings wrapped around their legs.
endangerment: Chytrid fungus, drought associated with climate change, and habitat modification have caused severe declines across the mountain ranges of Andalusia.
ecosystemNeeds: Cool, rocky habitats near small streams or water bodies in pine and oak forests above 1,000 metres in the Betic Cordillera of southeastern Spain.
actions: Support Spanish national park management in the Sierra Nevada and Cazorla ranges, fund disease treatment protocols in captive programmes, and back climate impact monitoring.

en.description: The Betic midwife toad is a small, terrestrial toad found only in the Betic mountain ranges of southeastern Spain, where males famously carry the egg strings wrapped around their legs.
en.endangerment: Chytrid fungus, drought associated with climate change, and habitat modification have caused severe declines across the mountain ranges of Andalusia.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Cool, rocky habitats near small streams or water bodies in pine and oak forests above 1,000 metres in the Betic Cordillera of southeastern Spain.
en.actions: Support Spanish national park management in the Sierra Nevada and Cazorla ranges, fund disease treatment protocols in captive programmes, and back climate impact monitoring.

es.description: El sapo partero bético es un pequeño sapo terrestre encontrado solo en las sierras béticas del sureste de España, donde los machos cargan famosamente los cordones de huevos envueltos alrededor de sus patas.
es.endangerment: El hongo quitridio, la sequía asociada al cambio climático y la modificación del hábitat han causado graves declives en todas las sierras de Andalucía.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Hábitats frescos y rocosos cerca de pequeños arroyos o masas de agua en pinares y robledales por encima de 1.000 metros en la Cordillera Bética del sureste de España.
es.actions: Apoyar la gestión del parque nacional español en las sierras Nevada y Cazorla, financiar protocolos de tratamiento de enfermedades en programas en cautiverio y respaldar el monitoreo de impactos climáticos.

pt.description: O sapo-parteiro-bético é um pequeno sapo terrestre encontrado apenas nas serras béticas do sudeste da Espanha, onde os machos carregam famosamente os cordões de ovos enrolados ao redor de suas patas.
pt.endangerment: O fungo quitrídio, a seca associada às mudanças climáticas e a modificação do habitat causaram declínios graves em todas as serras da Andaluzia.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Habitats frescos e rochosos perto de pequenos córregos ou corpos d'água em pinhais e matos de carvalho acima de 1.000 metros na Cordilheira Bética do sudeste da Espanha.
pt.actions: Apoiar o manejo do parque nacional espanhol nas serras Nevada e Cazorla, financiar protocolos de tratamento de doenças em programas em cativeiro e apoiar o monitoramento dos impactos climáticos.

fr.description: Le crapaud accoucheur bétique est un petit crapaud terrestre trouvé uniquement dans les chaînes Bétiques du sud-est de l'Espagne, où les mâles portent célèbrement les cordons d'œufs enroulés autour de leurs pattes.
fr.endangerment: Le champignon chytride, la sécheresse liée au changement climatique et la modification de l'habitat ont provoqué de graves déclins dans toutes les chaînes montagneuses d'Andalousie.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Habitats frais et rocheux à proximité de petits ruisseaux ou plans d'eau dans des forêts de pins et de chênes au-dessus de 1 000 mètres dans la cordillère Bétique du sud-est de l'Espagne.
fr.actions: Soutenir la gestion des parcs nationaux espagnols dans les sierras Nevada et Cazorla, financer des protocoles de traitement des maladies dans les programmes en captivité et soutenir le suivi des impacts climatiques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The yellow-spotted tree frog is a medium-sized frog once widespread in the Southern Tablelands of New South Wales, now believed to survive in only a handful of sites in the Snowy Mountains.
endangerment: Chytrid disease caused catastrophic declines in the late 1980s; drought from climate change, grazing impacts on wetlands, and introduced trout preying on tadpoles also contribute.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, vegetated temporary wetlands and floodplains in sub-alpine regions of the Snowy Mountains, New South Wales, with native grass tussock for shelter.
actions: Support the Zoos Victoria captive breeding programme, fund wetland restoration and feral livestock exclusion in the Southern Tablelands, and back climate change adaptation planning.

en.description: The yellow-spotted tree frog is a medium-sized frog once widespread in the Southern Tablelands of New South Wales, now believed to survive in only a handful of sites in the Snowy Mountains.
en.endangerment: Chytrid disease caused catastrophic declines in the late 1980s; drought from climate change, grazing impacts on wetlands, and introduced trout preying on tadpoles also contribute.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, vegetated temporary wetlands and floodplains in sub-alpine regions of the Snowy Mountains, New South Wales, with native grass tussock for shelter.
en.actions: Support the Zoos Victoria captive breeding programme, fund wetland restoration and feral livestock exclusion in the Southern Tablelands, and back climate change adaptation planning.

es.description: La rana arbórea de manchas amarillas es una rana de tamaño mediano que alguna vez fue común en las Mesetas del Sur de Nueva Gales del Sur, ahora se cree que solo sobrevive en unos pocos sitios en las Montañas de la Nieve.
es.endangerment: La enfermedad del quitridio causó declives catastróficos a fines de los años 80; la sequía por el cambio climático, el impacto del pastoreo en los humedales y la trucha introducida que depredó los renacuajos también contribuyen.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Humedales temporales poco profundos y vegetados y llanuras de inundación en regiones subalpinas de las Montañas de la Nieve, Nueva Gales del Sur, con matas de hierba nativa para refugio.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría en cautiverio de Zoos Victoria, financiar la restauración de humedales y la exclusión de ganado feral en las Mesetas del Sur y respaldar la planificación de adaptación al cambio climático.

pt.description: A rã-arbórea-de-manchas-amarelas é uma rã de tamanho médio antes amplamente distribuída nos Planaltos do Sul de Nova Gales do Sul, agora acredita-se que sobreviva em apenas alguns locais nas Montanhas da Neve.
pt.endangerment: A doença do quitrídio causou declínios catastróficos no final dos anos 1980; a seca causada pelas mudanças climáticas, o impacto do pastejo nas áreas úmidas e a truta introduzida predando girinos também contribuem.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Zonas úmidas temporárias rasas e vegetadas e planícies de inundação em regiões subalpinas das Montanhas da Neve, Nova Gales do Sul, com touceiras de gramíneas nativas para abrigo.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro do Zoos Victoria, financiar a restauração de áreas úmidas e a exclusão de gado feral nos Planaltos do Sul e apoiar o planejamento de adaptação às mudanças climáticas.

fr.description: La grenouille arboricole à taches jaunes est une grenouille de taille moyenne autrefois répandue dans les Southern Tablelands de Nouvelle-Galles du Sud, que l'on croit maintenant ne survivre que dans quelques sites dans les Snowy Mountains.
fr.endangerment: La maladie à chytride a provoqué des déclins catastrophiques à la fin des années 1980 ; la sécheresse due au changement climatique, l'impact du pâturage sur les zones humides et la truite introduite prédatant les têtards contribuent également.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Zones humides temporaires peu profondes et végétalisées et plaines inondables dans les régions subalpines des Snowy Mountains, en Nouvelle-Galles du Sud, avec des touffes d'herbes indigènes pour l'abri.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage en captivité de Zoos Victoria, financer la restauration des zones humides et l'exclusion du bétail sauvage dans les Southern Tablelands et soutenir la planification de l'adaptation au changement climatique.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Cuban crocodile is the most terrestrial crocodilian in the world, endemic to Cuba's Zapata Swamp and Lanier Swamp, known for hunting in groups and leaping from water to catch prey.
endangerment: Historical overhunting for skin, now hybridisation with the American crocodile — which dilutes the genetic integrity of the tiny pure population — and habitat loss are ongoing threats.
ecosystemNeeds: Freshwater marshes, swamps, and lagoons in Cuba's Ciénaga de Zapata and Lanier wetlands, with dense aquatic vegetation and adjacent terrestrial hunting areas.
actions: Support Cuba's national crocodile farming and release programme, fund genetic monitoring to detect hybridisation, and back Zapata Biosphere Reserve management.

en.description: The Cuban crocodile is the most terrestrial crocodilian in the world, endemic to Cuba's Zapata Swamp and Lanier Swamp, known for hunting in groups and leaping from water to catch prey.
en.endangerment: Historical overhunting for skin, now hybridisation with the American crocodile — which dilutes the genetic integrity of the tiny pure population — and habitat loss are ongoing threats.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Freshwater marshes, swamps, and lagoons in Cuba's Ciénaga de Zapata and Lanier wetlands, with dense aquatic vegetation and adjacent terrestrial hunting areas.
en.actions: Support Cuba's national crocodile farming and release programme, fund genetic monitoring to detect hybridisation, and back Zapata Biosphere Reserve management.

es.description: El cocodrilo cubano es el cocodrilo más terrestre del mundo, endémico de la Ciénaga de Zapata y la Ciénaga de Lanier en Cuba, conocido por cazar en grupos y saltar del agua para atrapar presas.
es.endangerment: La caza excesiva histórica por la piel, ahora la hibridación con el cocodrilo americano —que diluye la integridad genética de la pequeña población pura— y la pérdida de hábitat son amenazas actuales.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Marismas de agua dulce, pantanos y lagunas en la Ciénaga de Zapata y los humedales de Lanier en Cuba, con densa vegetación acuática y áreas terrestres adyacentes para caza.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa nacional cubano de cría y liberación de cocodrilos, financiar el monitoreo genético para detectar hibridación y respaldar la gestión de la Reserva de la Biosfera de Zapata.

pt.description: O crocodilo-cubano é o crocodiliano mais terrestre do mundo, endêmico do Pântano de Zapata e do Pântano de Lanier em Cuba, conhecido por caçar em grupos e saltar da água para capturar presas.
pt.endangerment: A caça excessiva histórica pelo couro, agora a hibridização com o crocodilo americano — que dilui a integridade genética da pequena população pura — e a perda de habitat são ameaças contínuas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pântanos de água doce, brejos e lagoas na Ciénaga de Zapata e nos pântanos de Lanier em Cuba, com densa vegetação aquática e áreas terrestres adjacentes para caça.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa nacional cubano de criação e soltura de crocodilos, financiar o monitoramento genético para detectar hibridização e apoiar o manejo da Reserva da Biosfera de Zapata.

fr.description: Le crocodile de Cuba est le crocodilien le plus terrestre du monde, endémique du Marais de Zapata et du Marais de Lanier à Cuba, connu pour chasser en groupes et sauter hors de l'eau pour attraper ses proies.
fr.endangerment: La chasse intensive historique pour la peau, désormais l'hybridation avec le crocodile américain — qui dilue l'intégrité génétique de la petite population pure — et la perte d'habitat sont des menaces permanentes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Marais d'eau douce, marécages et lagunes dans la Ciénaga de Zapata et les zones humides de Lanier à Cuba, avec une végétation aquatique dense et des zones terrestres adjacentes pour la chasse.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme national cubain d'élevage et de relâcher de crocodiles, financer le suivi génétique pour détecter l'hybridation et soutenir la gestion de la réserve de biosphère de Zapata.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Siamese crocodile is a medium-sized freshwater crocodile once widespread across Southeast Asia, now reduced to tiny fragmented wild populations, mostly in Cambodia.
endangerment: Hunting for skins and meat, collection of eggs and juveniles for crocodile farms, and loss of wetland habitat have left only an estimated few hundred wild individuals.
ecosystemNeeds: Slow-moving lowland rivers, streams, lakes, and seasonal wetlands within Cambodia's Cardamom Mountains and Mekong floodplains, with undisturbed sandy banks for nesting.
actions: Support Cambodia's Siamese Crocodile Working Group, fund nest protection in the Cardamom Mountains, and back release of farm-bred crocodiles of known genetic purity.

en.description: The Siamese crocodile is a medium-sized freshwater crocodile once widespread across Southeast Asia, now reduced to tiny fragmented wild populations, mostly in Cambodia.
en.endangerment: Hunting for skins and meat, collection of eggs and juveniles for crocodile farms, and loss of wetland habitat have left only an estimated few hundred wild individuals.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Slow-moving lowland rivers, streams, lakes, and seasonal wetlands within Cambodia's Cardamom Mountains and Mekong floodplains, with undisturbed sandy banks for nesting.
en.actions: Support Cambodia's Siamese Crocodile Working Group, fund nest protection in the Cardamom Mountains, and back release of farm-bred crocodiles of known genetic purity.

es.description: El cocodrilo siamés es un cocodrilo de agua dulce de tamaño mediano que alguna vez fue amplio en el sureste de Asia, ahora reducido a pequeñas poblaciones silvestres fragmentadas, principalmente en Camboya.
es.endangerment: La caza por pieles y carne, la recolección de huevos y juveniles para granjas de cocodrilos, y la pérdida de hábitat de humedales han dejado solo unos pocos cientos de individuos silvestres estimados.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Ríos de tierras bajas de movimiento lento, arroyos, lagos y humedales estacionales dentro de las Montañas Cardamomo de Camboya y las llanuras aluviales del Mekong, con orillas arenosas sin perturbaciones para anidar.
es.actions: Apoyar el Grupo de Trabajo del Cocodrilo Siamés de Camboya, financiar la protección de nidos en las Montañas Cardamomo y respaldar la liberación de cocodrilos criados en granjas de pureza genética conocida.

pt.description: O crocodilo-siamês é um crocodilo de água doce de tamanho médio antes amplamente distribuído no Sudeste Asiático, agora reduzido a pequenas populações selvagens fragmentadas, principalmente no Camboja.
pt.endangerment: A caça por couros e carne, a coleta de ovos e jovens para fazendas de crocodilos e a perda de habitat úmido deixaram apenas algumas centenas de indivíduos selvagens estimados.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Rios de baixadas de movimento lento, córregos, lagos e áreas úmidas sazonais dentro das Montanhas Cardamomo do Camboja e das planícies aluviais do Mekong, com margens arenosas sem perturbações para nidificação.
pt.actions: Apoiar o Grupo de Trabalho do Crocodilo Siamês do Camboja, financiar a proteção de ninhos nas Montanhas Cardamomo e apoiar a soltura de crocodilos criados em fazendas de pureza genética conhecida.

fr.description: Le crocodile siamois est un crocodile d'eau douce de taille moyenne autrefois répandu en Asie du Sud-Est, désormais réduit à de petites populations sauvages fragmentées, principalement au Cambodge.
fr.endangerment: La chasse pour les peaux et la viande, la collecte d'œufs et de juvéniles pour les fermes de crocodiles, et la perte d'habitat humide ont laissé seulement quelques centaines d'individus sauvages estimés.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Rivières de plaine à cours lent, ruisseaux, lacs et zones humides saisonnières dans les Montagnes des Cardamomes du Cambodge et les plaines alluviales du Mékong, avec des berges sablonneuses non perturbées pour la nidification.
fr.actions: Soutenir le groupe de travail sur le crocodile siamois au Cambodge, financer la protection des nids dans les Montagnes des Cardamomes et soutenir le relâcher de crocodiles d'élevage de pureté génétique connue.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The leatherback sea turtle is the largest reptile alive, a deep-diving, warm-blooded marvel that can reach 900 kg and migrate thousands of kilometres between tropical nesting beaches and cold polar feeding grounds.
endangerment: Bycatch in longlines and trawls, egg harvesting from nesting beaches, and ingestion of plastic bags mistaken for jellyfish have caused dramatic Pacific population declines.
ecosystemNeeds: Open ocean for feeding on jellyfish and sandy tropical beaches for nesting, particularly in Trinidad, Gabon, and Papua New Guinea, with minimal light pollution.
actions: Support nesting beach patrol programmes, advocate for longline bycatch circle hooks and TEDs, reduce plastic bag use, and back ocean plastic pollution cleanup initiatives.

en.description: The leatherback sea turtle is the largest reptile alive, a deep-diving, warm-blooded marvel that can reach 900 kg and migrate thousands of kilometres between tropical nesting beaches and cold polar feeding grounds.
en.endangerment: Bycatch in longlines and trawls, egg harvesting from nesting beaches, and ingestion of plastic bags mistaken for jellyfish have caused dramatic Pacific population declines.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open ocean for feeding on jellyfish and sandy tropical beaches for nesting, particularly in Trinidad, Gabon, and Papua New Guinea, with minimal light pollution.
en.actions: Support nesting beach patrol programmes, advocate for longline bycatch circle hooks and TEDs, reduce plastic bag use, and back ocean plastic pollution cleanup initiatives.

es.description: La tortuga laúd es el reptil más grande vivo, un nadador de profundidad y de sangre caliente que puede alcanzar 900 kg y migrar miles de kilómetros entre las playas de anidación tropicales y las frías aguas polares de alimentación.
es.endangerment: La captura incidental en palangres y arrastreras, la recolección de huevos en playas de anidación y la ingestión de bolsas de plástico confundidas con medusas han causado dramáticos declives en las poblaciones del Pacífico.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Océano abierto para alimentarse de medusas y playas arenosas tropicales para anidar, particularmente en Trinidad, Gabón y Papua Nueva Guinea, con mínima contaminación lumínica.
es.actions: Apoya los programas de patrullaje de playas de anidación, aboga por anzuelos circulares para palangres y dispositivos de exclusión de tortugas, reduce el uso de bolsas de plástico y respalda las iniciativas de limpieza de plásticos oceánicos.

pt.description: A tartaruga-de-couro é o maior réptil vivo, um mergulhador profundo e de sangue quente que pode atingir 900 kg e migrar milhares de quilômetros entre as praias de desova tropicais e as frias águas polares de alimentação.
pt.endangerment: A captura acidental em espinhéis e redes de arrasto, a colheita de ovos nas praias de desova e a ingestão de sacolas plásticas confundidas com águas-vivas causaram declínios dramáticos nas populações do Pacífico.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Oceano aberto para alimentação de águas-vivas e praias arenosas tropicais para desova, particularmente em Trinidad, Gabão e Papua Nova Guiné, com mínima poluição luminosa.
pt.actions: Apoie os programas de patrulha das praias de desova, defenda anzóis circulares para espinhéis e dispositivos de exclusão de tartarugas, reduza o uso de sacolas plásticas e respalda as iniciativas de limpeza de plásticos oceânicos.

fr.description: La tortue luth est le plus grand reptile vivant, un plongeur en eaux profondes et à sang chaud pouvant atteindre 900 kg, et migrant sur des milliers de kilomètres entre les plages de ponte tropicales et les eaux polaires froides où elle se nourrit.
fr.endangerment: Les prises accessoires dans les palangres et chaluts, la récolte d'œufs sur les plages de ponte et l'ingestion de sacs plastiques confondus avec des méduses ont provoqué des déclins dramatiques des populations du Pacifique.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: L'océan ouvert pour se nourrir de méduses et des plages de sable tropicales pour nidifier, notamment à Trinidad, au Gabon et en Papouasie-Nouvelle-Guinée, avec un minimum de pollution lumineuse.
fr.actions: Soutenez les programmes de patrouille des plages de ponte, plaidez pour des hameçons circulaires pour palangres et des dispositifs d'exclusion des tortues, réduisez l'utilisation des sacs plastiques et appuyez les initiatives de nettoyage des plastiques océaniques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Kemp's ridley is the world's smallest and most endangered sea turtle, famous for its mass nesting events called 'arribadas' almost entirely concentrated on a single beach in Mexico.
endangerment: Drowning in shrimp trawl nets caused catastrophic population collapse in the 20th century. Egg poaching, oil spills, and marine debris continue to impact recovering populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow coastal waters of the Gulf of Mexico for foraging on crabs and molluscs, and Rancho Nuevo beach, Tamaulipas, Mexico, for mass nesting.
actions: Support turtle excluder device mandates for shrimp trawlers, back nest protection at Rancho Nuevo, and advocate for Gulf oil spill response planning to protect sea turtle habitat.

en.description: Kemp's ridley is the world's smallest and most endangered sea turtle, famous for its mass nesting events called 'arribadas' almost entirely concentrated on a single beach in Mexico.
en.endangerment: Drowning in shrimp trawl nets caused catastrophic population collapse in the 20th century. Egg poaching, oil spills, and marine debris continue to impact recovering populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow coastal waters of the Gulf of Mexico for foraging on crabs and molluscs, and Rancho Nuevo beach, Tamaulipas, Mexico, for mass nesting.
en.actions: Support turtle excluder device mandates for shrimp trawlers, back nest protection at Rancho Nuevo, and advocate for Gulf oil spill response planning to protect sea turtle habitat.

es.description: La tortuga lora es la tortuga marina más pequeña y amenazada del mundo, famosa por sus eventos de anidación masiva llamados 'arribadas' concentrados casi en su totalidad en una sola playa de México.
es.endangerment: El ahogamiento en redes de camarón arrastrero causó un colapso catastrófico de la población en el siglo XX. La recolección de huevos, los derrames de petróleo y los desechos marinos continúan afectando a las poblaciones en recuperación.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas costeras poco profundas del Golfo de México para alimentarse de cangrejos y moluscos, y la playa Rancho Nuevo, Tamaulipas, México, para la anidación masiva.
es.actions: Apoya los mandatos de dispositivos de exclusión de tortugas para arrastreros de camarón, respalda la protección de nidos en Rancho Nuevo y aboga por la planificación de respuesta a derrames de petróleo en el Golfo para proteger el hábitat de las tortugas marinas.

pt.description: A tartaruga-de-Kemp é a menor e mais ameaçada tartaruga marinha do mundo, famosa por seus eventos de nidificação em massa chamados 'arribadas', quase inteiramente concentrados em uma única praia no México.
pt.endangerment: O afogamento em redes de arrasto de camarão causou o colapso catastrófico da população no século XX. A colheita de ovos, os derramamentos de petróleo e os detritos marinhos continuam a afetar as populações em recuperação.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas costeiras rasas do Golfo do México para alimentação de caranguejos e moluscos, e a praia de Rancho Nuevo, Tamaulipas, México, para a nidificação em massa.
pt.actions: Apoie os mandatos de dispositivos de exclusão de tartarugas para arrasteiros de camarão, respalda a proteção de ninhos em Rancho Nuevo e defenda o planejamento de resposta a derramamentos de petróleo no Golfo para proteger o habitat das tartarugas marinhas.

fr.description: La tortue de Kemp est la plus petite et la plus menacée des tortues marines, célèbre pour ses événements de ponte de masse appelés 'arribadas', presque entièrement concentrés sur une seule plage au Mexique.
fr.endangerment: La noyade dans les chaluts à crevettes a provoqué un effondrement catastrophique de la population au XXe siècle. La récolte d'œufs, les marées noires et les débris marins continuent d'affecter les populations en cours de reprise.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des eaux côtières peu profondes du golfe du Mexique pour se nourrir de crabes et de mollusques, et la plage de Rancho Nuevo, Tamaulipas, Mexique, pour la ponte en masse.
fr.actions: Soutenez les mandats d'exclusion des tortues pour les chaluts à crevettes, appuyez la protection des nids à Rancho Nuevo et plaidez pour la planification des interventions en cas de marée noire dans le golfe pour protéger l'habitat des tortues marines.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Chinese alligator is one of only two living alligator species, a small, nocturnal reptile that once ranged across eastern China but is now confined to a tiny area of Anhui province.
endangerment: Conversion of wetlands to rice paddies and aquaculture ponds has destroyed most of its habitat; historically killed as a pest by farmers, now fewer than 200 wild individuals remain.
ecosystemNeeds: Slow-moving freshwater rivers, ponds, and marshes in the lower Yangtze River valley, with soft banks for burrowing and hibernating through cold winters.
actions: Support China's Changxing Yinjiabian Chinese Alligator National Nature Reserve, fund captive breeding and release programmes, and back wetland restoration in Anhui.

en.description: The Chinese alligator is one of only two living alligator species, a small, nocturnal reptile that once ranged across eastern China but is now confined to a tiny area of Anhui province.
en.endangerment: Conversion of wetlands to rice paddies and aquaculture ponds has destroyed most of its habitat; historically killed as a pest by farmers, now fewer than 200 wild individuals remain.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Slow-moving freshwater rivers, ponds, and marshes in the lower Yangtze River valley, with soft banks for burrowing and hibernating through cold winters.
en.actions: Support China's Changxing Yinjiabian Chinese Alligator National Nature Reserve, fund captive breeding and release programmes, and back wetland restoration in Anhui.

es.description: El caimán chino es una de las dos especies de caimán vivas, un pequeño reptil nocturno que alguna vez se extendió por el este de China pero ahora está confinado a una pequeña área de la provincia de Anhui.
es.endangerment: La conversión de humedales en arrozales y acuicultura ha destruido la mayor parte de su hábitat; históricamente matado como plaga por los agricultores, ahora quedan menos de 200 individuos silvestres.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Ríos de movimiento lento, estanques y marismas en el bajo valle del río Yangtsé, con orillas blandas para excavar y hibernar durante los fríos inviernos.
es.actions: Apoya la Reserva Natural Nacional del Caimán Chino Changxing Yinjiabian de China, financia programas de cría en cautividad y liberación y respalda la restauración de humedales en Anhui.

pt.description: O aligátor-chinês é uma das duas espécies de aligátor vivas, um pequeno réptil noturno que já se estendeu pelo leste da China, mas agora está confinado a uma pequena área da província de Anhui.
pt.endangerment: A conversão de zonas úmidas em arrozais e aquicultura destruiu a maior parte de seu habitat; historicamente morto como praga pelos agricultores, agora restam menos de 200 indivíduos selvagens.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Rios de movimento lento, lagoas e pântanos no baixo vale do Rio Yangtzé, com margens macias para escavar e hibernar durante os frios invernos.
pt.actions: Apoie a Reserva Natural Nacional do Aligátor Chinês Changxing Yinjiabian da China, finance programas de criação em cativeiro e liberação e respalda a restauração de zonas úmidas em Anhui.

fr.description: L'alligator de Chine est l'une des deux espèces d'alligators vivantes, un petit reptile nocturne qui s'étendait autrefois à l'est de la Chine, mais qui est maintenant confiné à une petite zone de la province d'Anhui.
fr.endangerment: La conversion des zones humides en rizières et en aquaculture a détruit la majeure partie de son habitat ; historiquement tué comme nuisible par les agriculteurs, il ne reste plus que 200 individus à l'état sauvage.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Des rivières à débit lent, des étangs et des marais dans la basse vallée du Yangtsé, avec des berges molles pour s'enfouir et hiverner pendant les hivers rigoureux.
fr.actions: Soutenez la Réserve naturelle nationale de l'alligator de Chine de Changxing Yinjiabian, financez les programmes d'élevage en captivité et de réintroduction et appuyez la restauration des zones humides en Anhui.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Round Island boa is a primitive snake found only on the tiny Round Island off Mauritius, remarkable for its unique jaw structure that allows it to swallow lizards sideways.
endangerment: Introduced goats and rabbits devastated the island's vegetation, destroying soil stability and the habitat of the geckos the boa depends on; both were eradicated in the 2000s.
ecosystemNeeds: Regenerating palm savanna and scrub on Round Island, a volcanic islet 22 km north of Mauritius, with sufficient geckos and skinks as prey.
actions: Support ongoing habitat restoration on Round Island, back the Jersey Zoo captive breeding programme, and fund reintroduction feasibility studies on additional Mauritian islands.

en.description: The Round Island boa is a primitive snake found only on the tiny Round Island off Mauritius, remarkable for its unique jaw structure that allows it to swallow lizards sideways.
en.endangerment: Introduced goats and rabbits devastated the island's vegetation, destroying soil stability and the habitat of the geckos the boa depends on; both were eradicated in the 2000s.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Regenerating palm savanna and scrub on Round Island, a volcanic islet 22 km north of Mauritius, with sufficient geckos and skinks as prey.
en.actions: Support ongoing habitat restoration on Round Island, back the Jersey Zoo captive breeding programme, and fund reintroduction feasibility studies on additional Mauritian islands.

es.description: La boa de la Isla Redonda es una serpiente primitiva encontrada solo en la diminuta Isla Redonda frente a Mauricio, notable por su estructura de mandíbula única que le permite tragar lagartos de costado.
es.endangerment: Las cabras y conejos introducidos devastaron la vegetación de la isla, destruyendo la estabilidad del suelo y el hábitat de los gecos de los que depende la boa; ambos fueron erradicados en los años 2000.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Sabana de palmas en regeneración y matorral en la Isla Redonda, un islote volcánico a 22 km al norte de Mauricio, con suficientes gecos y eslizones como presa.
es.actions: Apoyar la restauración continua del hábitat en la Isla Redonda, respaldar el programa de cría en cautiverio del Zoo de Jersey y financiar estudios de viabilidad de reintroducción en islas adicionales de Mauricio.

pt.description: A jiboia-da-ilha-redonda é uma cobra primitiva encontrada apenas na minúscula Ilha Redonda ao largo de Maurício, notável por sua estrutura de mandíbula única que lhe permite engolir lagartos de lado.
pt.endangerment: Cabras e coelhos introduzidos devastaram a vegetação da ilha, destruindo a estabilidade do solo e o habitat dos geckos dos quais a jiboia depende; ambos foram erradicados nos anos 2000.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Savana de palmeiras em regeneração e mato na Ilha Redonda, um ilhote vulcânico a 22 km ao norte de Maurício, com geckos e lagartixas suficientes como presas.
pt.actions: Apoiar a restauração contínua do habitat na Ilha Redonda, apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro do Zoológico de Jersey e financiar estudos de viabilidade de reintrodução em ilhas adicionais de Maurício.

fr.description: Le boa de l'île Ronde est un serpent primitif trouvé uniquement sur la minuscule île Ronde au large de Maurice, remarquable pour sa structure de mâchoire unique qui lui permet d'avaler des lézards de côté.
fr.endangerment: Les chèvres et lapins introduits ont dévasté la végétation de l'île, détruisant la stabilité du sol et l'habitat des geckos dont dépend le boa ; les deux ont été éradiqués dans les années 2000.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Savane de palmiers en régénération et broussailles sur l'île Ronde, un îlot volcanique à 22 km au nord de Maurice, avec suffisamment de geckos et de scinques comme proies.
fr.actions: Soutenir la restauration continue de l'habitat sur l'île Ronde, soutenir le programme d'élevage en captivité du zoo de Jersey et financer des études de faisabilité de réintroduction sur d'autres îles mauriciennes.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Madagascan big-headed turtle is the sole surviving member of its ancient family, a large freshwater turtle endemic to western Madagascar distinguished by its massive head.
endangerment: Intensive hunting for food and collection for the international pet trade have decimated populations; wetland habitat destruction adds further pressure.
ecosystemNeeds: Large rivers, lakes, and seasonal floodplains in western Madagascar with adequate aquatic vegetation, basking sites, and freedom from intensive fishing pressure.
actions: Support local community conservation agreements around key wetlands in western Madagascar, fund trade enforcement to combat pet trade exports, and back captive breeding programmes.

en.description: The Madagascan big-headed turtle is the sole surviving member of its ancient family, a large freshwater turtle endemic to western Madagascar distinguished by its massive head.
en.endangerment: Intensive hunting for food and collection for the international pet trade have decimated populations; wetland habitat destruction adds further pressure.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large rivers, lakes, and seasonal floodplains in western Madagascar with adequate aquatic vegetation, basking sites, and freedom from intensive fishing pressure.
en.actions: Support local community conservation agreements around key wetlands in western Madagascar, fund trade enforcement to combat pet trade exports, and back captive breeding programmes.

es.description: La tortuga de cabeza grande de Madagascar es el único miembro superviviente de su antigua familia, una gran tortuga de agua dulce endémica del oeste de Madagascar distinguida por su cabeza masiva.
es.endangerment: La caza intensiva para alimento y la recolección para el comercio internacional de mascotas han diezmado las poblaciones; la destrucción del hábitat de humedales añade más presión.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes ríos, lagos y llanuras de inundación estacionales en el oeste de Madagascar con vegetación acuática adecuada, sitios para tomar el sol y libertad de la presión de pesca intensiva.
es.actions: Apoyar acuerdos de conservación comunitaria local en torno a humedales clave en el oeste de Madagascar, financiar la aplicación del comercio para combatir las exportaciones de mascotas y respaldar programas de cría en cautiverio.

pt.description: A tartaruga-de-cabeça-grande-de-madagascar é o único membro sobrevivente de sua família ancestral, uma grande tartaruga de água doce endêmica do oeste de Madagascar distinguida por sua cabeça massiva.
pt.endangerment: A caça intensiva para alimentação e a coleta para o comércio internacional de animais de estimação dizimaram as populações; a destruição do habitat úmido adiciona mais pressão.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rios, lagos e planícies de inundação sazonais no oeste de Madagascar com vegetação aquática adequada, locais de aquecimento solar e ausência de pressão de pesca intensiva.
pt.actions: Apoiar acordos de conservação comunitária local em torno de zonas úmidas-chave no oeste de Madagascar, financiar a fiscalização do comércio para combater as exportações de animais de estimação e apoiar programas de criação em cativeiro.

fr.description: La tortue à grosse tête de Madagascar est le seul membre survivant de son ancienne famille, une grande tortue d'eau douce endémique de l'ouest de Madagascar distinguée par sa tête massive.
fr.endangerment: La chasse intensive pour la nourriture et la collecte pour le commerce international d'animaux de compagnie ont décimé les populations ; la destruction de l'habitat humide ajoute une pression supplémentaire.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Grands fleuves, lacs et plaines inondables saisonnières dans l'ouest de Madagascar avec une végétation aquatique adéquate, des sites de bain de soleil et une absence de pression de pêche intensive.
fr.actions: Soutenir les accords de conservation communautaire locaux autour des zones humides clés dans l'ouest de Madagascar, financer l'application des lois commerciales pour lutter contre les exportations d'animaux de compagnie et soutenir les programmes d'élevage en captivité.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Roti Island snake-necked turtle is one of the world's most endangered turtles, endemic to a small number of lakes on tiny Roti Island near Timor, Indonesia.
endangerment: Massive overcollection for the international pet trade in the 1990s and 2000s removed enormous numbers from the wild; remaining animals face lake habitat degradation.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, vegetated freshwater lakes and seasonal marshes on Roti Island, Indonesia, with soft substrates for burrowing and aquatic invertebrate prey.
actions: Support CITES trade ban enforcement, fund habitat restoration on Roti Island, and back the Toledo Zoo's captive programme and reintroduction planning.

en.description: The Roti Island snake-necked turtle is one of the world's most endangered turtles, endemic to a small number of lakes on tiny Roti Island near Timor, Indonesia.
en.endangerment: Massive overcollection for the international pet trade in the 1990s and 2000s removed enormous numbers from the wild; remaining animals face lake habitat degradation.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, vegetated freshwater lakes and seasonal marshes on Roti Island, Indonesia, with soft substrates for burrowing and aquatic invertebrate prey.
en.actions: Support CITES trade ban enforcement, fund habitat restoration on Roti Island, and back the Toledo Zoo's captive programme and reintroduction planning.

es.description: La tortuga cuello de serpiente de la Isla Roti es una de las tortugas más amenazadas del mundo, endémica de un pequeño número de lagos en la diminuta Isla Roti cerca de Timor, Indonesia.
es.endangerment: La sobrecolección masiva para el comercio internacional de mascotas en los años 90 y 2000 eliminó un enorme número de la naturaleza; los animales restantes enfrentan degradación del hábitat lacustre.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Lagos de agua dulce poco profundos y vegetados y marismas estacionales en la Isla Roti, Indonesia, con sustratos blandos para excavar e invertebrados acuáticos como presa.
es.actions: Apoyar la aplicación de la prohibición comercial de la CITES, financiar la restauración del hábitat en la Isla Roti y respaldar el programa en cautiverio del Toledo Zoo y la planificación de reintroducción.

pt.description: A tartaruga-pescoço-de-cobra-da-ilha-roti é uma das tartarugas mais ameaçadas do mundo, endêmica de um pequeno número de lagos na minúscula Ilha Roti perto de Timor, Indonésia.
pt.endangerment: A supercoleta massiva para o comércio internacional de animais de estimação nos anos 1990 e 2000 removeu um enorme número da natureza; os animais restantes enfrentam a degradação do habitat lacustre.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Lagos de água doce rasos e vegetados e brejos sazonais na Ilha Roti, Indonésia, com substratos macios para escavar e invertebrados aquáticos como presas.
pt.actions: Apoiar a fiscalização da proibição comercial da CITES, financiar a restauração do habitat na Ilha Roti e apoiar o programa em cativeiro do Toledo Zoo e o planejamento de reintrodução.

fr.description: La tortue à long cou de l'île Roti est l'une des tortues les plus menacées au monde, endémique d'un petit nombre de lacs sur la minuscule île Roti près de Timor, en Indonésie.
fr.endangerment: La surexploitation massive pour le commerce international d'animaux de compagnie dans les années 1990 et 2000 a retiré d'énormes quantités de la nature ; les animaux restants font face à la dégradation de l'habitat lacustre.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Lacs d'eau douce peu profonds et végétalisés et marais saisonniers sur l'île Roti, en Indonésie, avec des substrats meubles pour s'enfouir et des invertébrés aquatiques comme proies.
fr.actions: Soutenir l'application de l'interdiction commerciale de la CITES, financer la restauration de l'habitat sur l'île Roti et soutenir le programme en captivité du zoo de Toledo et la planification de la réintroduction.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The spiny turtle is a distinctive forest turtle of Southeast Asia, named for the sharp spines along the edge of its shell which help it blend into leaf litter on the forest floor.
endangerment: Massive collection for the wildlife trade — food markets in China and the international pet trade — combined with forest loss has caused severe population declines across its range.
ecosystemNeeds: Humid tropical forest with leaf litter, small streams, and moist conditions in Sumatra, Borneo, and the Malay Peninsula, where it forages for fruits and fungi.
actions: Support wildlife trade enforcement in Southeast Asian markets, fund forest protection in Sumatra and Borneo, and back sustainable community forestry that benefits forest species.

en.description: The spiny turtle is a distinctive forest turtle of Southeast Asia, named for the sharp spines along the edge of its shell which help it blend into leaf litter on the forest floor.
en.endangerment: Massive collection for the wildlife trade — food markets in China and the international pet trade — combined with forest loss has caused severe population declines across its range.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Humid tropical forest with leaf litter, small streams, and moist conditions in Sumatra, Borneo, and the Malay Peninsula, where it forages for fruits and fungi.
en.actions: Support wildlife trade enforcement in Southeast Asian markets, fund forest protection in Sumatra and Borneo, and back sustainable community forestry that benefits forest species.

es.description: La tortuga espinosa es una tortuga forestal distintiva del sureste de Asia, llamada así por las espinas afiladas a lo largo del borde de su caparazón que la ayudan a mimetizarse con la hojarasca del suelo del bosque.
es.endangerment: La recolección masiva para el comercio de vida silvestre —mercados de alimentos en China y el comercio internacional de mascotas— combinada con la pérdida forestal, ha causado graves declives de población en toda su área.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque tropical húmedo con hojarasca, pequeños arroyos y condiciones húmedas en Sumatra, Borneo y la Península Malaya, donde forrajea frutas y hongos.
es.actions: Apoyar la aplicación del comercio de vida silvestre en los mercados del sureste asiático, financiar la protección forestal en Sumatra y Borneo y respaldar la silvicultura comunitaria sostenible que beneficia a las especies forestales.

pt.description: A tartaruga-espinhosa é uma tartaruga florestal distintiva do Sudeste Asiático, chamada assim pelas espinhas pontiagudas ao longo da borda de sua carapaça que a ajudam a se camuflar na serapilheira do chão da floresta.
pt.endangerment: A coleta massiva para o comércio de animais silvestres — mercados de alimentos na China e o comércio internacional de animais de estimação — combinada com a perda florestal, causou graves declínios populacionais em toda a sua área.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical úmida com serapilheira, pequenos córregos e condições úmidas em Sumatra, Bornéu e na Península Malaia, onde forrageia frutas e fungos.
pt.actions: Apoiar a fiscalização do comércio de animais silvestres nos mercados do Sudeste Asiático, financiar a proteção florestal em Sumatra e Bornéu e apoiar o manejo florestal comunitário sustentável que beneficia as espécies florestais.

fr.description: La tortue épineuse est une tortue forestière distinctive d'Asie du Sud-Est, nommée pour les épines acérées le long du bord de sa carapace qui l'aident à se fondre dans la litière de feuilles du sol forestier.
fr.endangerment: La collecte massive pour le commerce de la faune — marchés alimentaires en Chine et commerce international d'animaux de compagnie — combinée à la perte de forêts, a provoqué de graves déclins de population dans toute son aire.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale humide avec litière de feuilles, petits ruisseaux et conditions humides à Sumatra, Bornéo et dans la péninsule malaise, où elle se nourrit de fruits et de champignons.
fr.actions: Soutenir l'application des lois sur le commerce de la faune dans les marchés d'Asie du Sud-Est, financer la protection des forêts à Sumatra et à Bornéo et soutenir la foresterie communautaire durable qui profite aux espèces forestières.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Armenian viper is a stout, heavily-built viper found in mountain steppes of Armenia, eastern Turkey, and western Iran, sheltering under rocks on steep, south-facing slopes.
endangerment: Collection for the pet trade and persecution by local people who fear its venomous bite, combined with overgrazing that degrades its rocky grassland habitat, have reduced populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Rocky, dry mountain steppes and shrublands between 1,500 and 2,700 metres elevation in the Caucasus and northeastern Anatolia, with abundant small rodent prey.
actions: Support Armenia's reptile protection legislation, fund population surveys across its fragmented range, and raise local awareness to reduce persecution of this medically important species.

en.description: The Armenian viper is a stout, heavily-built viper found in mountain steppes of Armenia, eastern Turkey, and western Iran, sheltering under rocks on steep, south-facing slopes.
en.endangerment: Collection for the pet trade and persecution by local people who fear its venomous bite, combined with overgrazing that degrades its rocky grassland habitat, have reduced populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Rocky, dry mountain steppes and shrublands between 1,500 and 2,700 metres elevation in the Caucasus and northeastern Anatolia, with abundant small rodent prey.
en.actions: Support Armenia's reptile protection legislation, fund population surveys across its fragmented range, and raise local awareness to reduce persecution of this medically important species.

es.description: La víbora armenia es una víbora robusta y de cuerpo pesado encontrada en las estepas montañosas de Armenia, el este de Turquía y el oeste de Irán, refugiándose bajo rocas en laderas pronunciadas orientadas al sur.
es.endangerment: La recolección para el comercio de mascotas y la persecución por parte de los lugareños que temen su mordedura venenosa, combinadas con el sobrepastoreo que degrada su hábitat de pastizales rocosos, han reducido las poblaciones.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Estepas montañosas rocosas y secas y matorrales entre 1.500 y 2.700 metros de altitud en el Cáucaso y el noreste de Anatolia, con abundante presa de pequeños roedores.
es.actions: Apoyar la legislación de protección de reptiles de Armenia, financiar encuestas de población en toda su área fragmentada y sensibilizar a la población local para reducir la persecución de esta especie de importancia médica.

pt.description: A víbora-armênia é uma víbora robusta e de corpo pesado encontrada nas estepes montanhosas da Armênia, leste da Turquia e oeste do Irã, abrigando-se sob rochas em encostas íngremes voltadas para o sul.
pt.endangerment: A coleta para o comércio de animais de estimação e a perseguição pelos moradores locais que temem sua mordida venenosa, combinadas com o superpastejo que degrada seu habitat de pastagens rochosas, reduziram as populações.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Estepes montanhosas rochosas e secas e arbustos entre 1.500 e 2.700 metros de altitude no Cáucaso e no nordeste da Anatólia, com abundante presa de pequenos roedores.
pt.actions: Apoiar a legislação de proteção de répteis da Armênia, financiar levantamentos populacionais em toda a sua área fragmentada e aumentar a conscientização local para reduzir a perseguição a esta espécie de importância médica.

fr.description: La vipère d'Arménie est une vipère trapue et au corps lourd trouvée dans les steppes montagnardes d'Arménie, de l'est de la Turquie et de l'ouest de l'Iran, s'abritant sous les rochers sur des pentes raides exposées au sud.
fr.endangerment: La collecte pour le commerce d'animaux de compagnie et la persécution par les habitants qui craignent sa morsure venimeuse, combinées au surpâturage qui dégrade son habitat de prairies rocheuses, ont réduit les populations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Steppes montagnardes rocheuses et sèches et garrigues entre 1 500 et 2 700 mètres d'altitude dans le Caucase et le nord-est de l'Anatolie, avec des petits rongeurs en abondance.
fr.actions: Soutenir la législation de protection des reptiles en Arménie, financer des relevés de population dans toute son aire fragmentée et sensibiliser la population locale pour réduire la persécution de cette espèce d'importance médicale.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The flat-tailed horned lizard is a camouflage master of the Sonoran and Colorado deserts, nearly indistinguishable from the sand it lives on, and famous for squirting blood from its eyes as a defence.
endangerment: Off-road vehicle use that compacts sand and destroys harvester ant colonies — its primary food — combined with urban sprawl in the Imperial Valley, has severely reduced populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Fine-grained wind-blown sand flats in the Sonoran and Colorado deserts of southern California, Arizona, and Baja California, with abundant native harvester ants.
actions: Support OHV restriction zones in flat-tailed horned lizard habitat, fund sand dune restoration, and advocate for long-term population monitoring through the FTHL Interagency Coordinating Committee.

en.description: The flat-tailed horned lizard is a camouflage master of the Sonoran and Colorado deserts, nearly indistinguishable from the sand it lives on, and famous for squirting blood from its eyes as a defence.
en.endangerment: Off-road vehicle use that compacts sand and destroys harvester ant colonies — its primary food — combined with urban sprawl in the Imperial Valley, has severely reduced populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Fine-grained wind-blown sand flats in the Sonoran and Colorado deserts of southern California, Arizona, and Baja California, with abundant native harvester ants.
en.actions: Support OHV restriction zones in flat-tailed horned lizard habitat, fund sand dune restoration, and advocate for long-term population monitoring through the FTHL Interagency Coordinating Committee.

es.description: El lagarto cornudo de cola plana es un maestro del camuflaje del desierto de Sonora y Colorado, casi indistinguible de la arena en la que vive, y famoso por rociar sangre de sus ojos como defensa.
es.endangerment: El uso de vehículos todoterreno que compacta la arena y destruye las colonias de hormigas cosechadoras —su alimento principal— combinado con la expansión urbana en el Valle Imperial, ha reducido severamente las poblaciones.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Planicies de arena fina arrastrada por el viento en los desiertos de Sonora y Colorado del sur de California, Arizona y Baja California, con abundantes hormigas cosechadoras nativas.
es.actions: Apoyar zonas de restricción de vehículos todoterreno en el hábitat del lagarto cornudo de cola plana, financiar la restauración de dunas de arena y abogar por el monitoreo a largo plazo de la población a través del Comité de Coordinación Interagencial FTHL.

pt.description: O lagarto-cornudo-de-cauda-plana é um mestre do camuflagem dos desertos de Sonora e Colorado, quase indistinguível da areia em que vive, e famoso por jorrar sangue de seus olhos como defesa.
pt.endangerment: O uso de veículos off-road que compacta a areia e destrói colônias de formigas colhedoras — seu principal alimento — combinado com a expansão urbana no Vale Imperial, reduziu severamente as populações.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Planícies de areia fina transportada pelo vento nos desertos de Sonora e Colorado do sul da Califórnia, Arizona e Baja California, com abundantes formigas colhedoras nativas.
pt.actions: Apoiar zonas de restrição de veículos off-road no habitat do lagarto-cornudo-de-cauda-plana, financiar a restauração de dunas de areia e defender o monitoramento populacional de longo prazo através do Comitê de Coordenação Interagencial FTHL.

fr.description: Le lézard cornu à queue plate est un maître du camouflage des déserts de Sonora et du Colorado, presque impossible à distinguer du sable sur lequel il vit, et célèbre pour projeter du sang par les yeux en guise de défense.
fr.endangerment: L'utilisation de véhicules tout-terrain qui compacte le sable et détruit les colonies de fourmis moissonneuses — sa principale nourriture — combinée à l'étalement urbain dans la vallée Impériale, a sévèrement réduit les populations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Plaines de sable fin éolien dans les déserts de Sonora et du Colorado du sud de la Californie, de l'Arizona et de la Basse-Californie, avec des fourmis moissonneuses indigènes en abondance.
fr.actions: Soutenir les zones de restriction des véhicules tout-terrain dans l'habitat du lézard cornu à queue plate, financer la restauration des dunes de sable et promouvoir le suivi à long terme de la population via le Comité de coordination interagences FTHL.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The beluga sturgeon is the world's largest freshwater fish and the source of the most prized caviar, an ancient species that can live over 100 years and grow to 7 metres.
endangerment: Over a century of intensive caviar harvesting has devastated populations. Dams blocking spawning migrations and pollution of the Caspian and Black Sea basins compound the threat.
ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep rivers flowing into the Caspian and Black Seas — particularly the Volga, Ural, Danube, and Dnieper — with clean gravel beds for spawning and unobstructed migration routes.
actions: Support caviar trade restrictions and aquaculture certification, advocate for fish passage on key dams, and fund stocking programmes with wild-origin fish from verified populations.

en.description: The beluga sturgeon is the world's largest freshwater fish and the source of the most prized caviar, an ancient species that can live over 100 years and grow to 7 metres.
en.endangerment: Over a century of intensive caviar harvesting has devastated populations. Dams blocking spawning migrations and pollution of the Caspian and Black Sea basins compound the threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep rivers flowing into the Caspian and Black Seas — particularly the Volga, Ural, Danube, and Dnieper — with clean gravel beds for spawning and unobstructed migration routes.
en.actions: Support caviar trade restrictions and aquaculture certification, advocate for fish passage on key dams, and fund stocking programmes with wild-origin fish from verified populations.

es.description: El esturión beluga es el pez de agua dulce más grande del mundo y la fuente del caviar más preciado, una especie antigua que puede vivir más de 100 años y crecer hasta 7 metros.
es.endangerment: Más de un siglo de intensiva cosecha de caviar ha devastado las poblaciones. Las presas que bloquean las migraciones de desove y la contaminación de los mares Caspio y Negro agravan la amenaza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes ríos profundos que desembocan en los mares Caspio y Negro —particularmente el Volga, Ural, Danubio y Dniéper— con lechos de grava limpios para el desove y rutas de migración sin obstáculos.
es.actions: Apoya las restricciones al comercio de caviar y la certificación de acuicultura, aboga por pasos de peces en presas clave y financia programas de repoblación con peces de poblaciones silvestres verificadas.

pt.description: O esturjão-beluga é o maior peixe de água doce do mundo e a fonte do caviar mais valorizado, uma espécie antiga que pode viver mais de 100 anos e crescer até 7 metros.
pt.endangerment: Mais de um século de colheita intensiva de caviar devastou as populações. Barragens bloqueando as migrações de desova e a poluição dos Mares Cáspio e Negro agravam a ameaça.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rios profundos que deságuam nos Mares Cáspio e Negro — particularmente o Volga, Ural, Danúbio e Dniepre — com leitos de cascalho limpos para a desova e rotas de migração desobstruídas.
pt.actions: Apoie as restrições ao comércio de caviar e a certificação de aquicultura, defenda passagens de peixes em barragens-chave e finance programas de repovoamento com peixes de populações selvagens verificadas.

fr.description: L'esturgeon béluga est le plus grand poisson d'eau douce au monde et la source du caviar le plus précieux, une espèce ancienne qui peut vivre plus de 100 ans et atteindre 7 mètres.
fr.endangerment: Plus d'un siècle de récolte intensive de caviar a dévasté les populations. Les barrages bloquant les migrations de frai et la pollution des mers Caspienne et Noire aggravent la menace.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: De grands fleuves profonds se jetant dans les mers Caspienne et Noire — notamment la Volga, l'Oural, le Danube et le Dniepr — avec des lits de gravier propres pour le frai et des routes migratoires dégagées.
fr.actions: Soutenez les restrictions au commerce du caviar et la certification de l'aquaculture, plaidez pour des passes à poissons sur les barrages clés et financez des programmes de repeuplement avec des poissons d'origine sauvage vérifiée.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The European sturgeon once ranged across coastal waters from Norway to the Black Sea, but today a single surviving wild population breeds in the Gironde estuary of France.
endangerment: Historical overfishing, bycatch in coastal nets, river damming, and pollution have reduced the wild population to fewer than 750 adults concentrated in the Gironde–Dordogne river system.
ecosystemNeeds: Coastal Atlantic waters for juvenile and adult feeding, and large clean rivers — principally the Gironde and Dordogne — with gravel beds for spawning.
actions: Support France's hatchery and stocking programme, advocate for bycatch reduction in Atlantic trawl fisheries, and back river restoration to improve water quality in the Gironde catchment.

en.description: The European sturgeon once ranged across coastal waters from Norway to the Black Sea, but today a single surviving wild population breeds in the Gironde estuary of France.
en.endangerment: Historical overfishing, bycatch in coastal nets, river damming, and pollution have reduced the wild population to fewer than 750 adults concentrated in the Gironde–Dordogne river system.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Coastal Atlantic waters for juvenile and adult feeding, and large clean rivers — principally the Gironde and Dordogne — with gravel beds for spawning.
en.actions: Support France's hatchery and stocking programme, advocate for bycatch reduction in Atlantic trawl fisheries, and back river restoration to improve water quality in the Gironde catchment.

es.description: El esturión europeo alguna vez se extendió por las aguas costeras desde Noruega hasta el Mar Negro, pero hoy una única población silvestre superviviente se reproduce en el estuario del Girona de Francia.
es.endangerment: La pesca excesiva histórica, la captura accidental en redes costeras, la construcción de presas fluviales y la contaminación han reducido la población silvestre a menos de 750 adultos concentrados en el sistema fluvial Gironde–Dordoña.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas costeras atlánticas para la alimentación de juveniles y adultos, y grandes ríos limpios —principalmente el Gironde y el Dordoña— con lechos de grava para el desove.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría en piscifactoría y repoblación de Francia, abogar por la reducción de capturas accesorias en la pesca de arrastre atlántica y respaldar la restauración fluvial para mejorar la calidad del agua en la cuenca del Gironde.

pt.description: O esturjão-europeu já se distribuía pelas águas costeiras da Noruega ao Mar Negro, mas hoje uma única população selvagem sobrevivente se reproduz no estuário do Gironde na França.
pt.endangerment: A pesca excessiva histórica, a captura acidental em redes costeiras, a construção de barragens e a poluição reduziram a população selvagem para menos de 750 adultos concentrados no sistema fluvial Gironde–Dordogne.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas costeiras atlânticas para a alimentação de juvenis e adultos, e grandes rios limpos — principalmente o Gironde e o Dordogne — com leitos de cascalho para a desova.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de hatchery e repovoamento da França, defender a redução de captura acidental na pesca de arrasto atlântica e apoiar a restauração fluvial para melhorar a qualidade da água na bacia do Gironde.

fr.description: L'esturgeon européen s'étendait autrefois dans les eaux côtières de la Norvège à la mer Noire, mais aujourd'hui une seule population sauvage survivante se reproduit dans l'estuaire de la Gironde en France.
fr.endangerment: La surpêche historique, les captures accessoires dans les filets côtiers, la construction de barrages fluviaux et la pollution ont réduit la population sauvage à moins de 750 adultes concentrés dans le système fluvial Gironde–Dordogne.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Eaux côtières atlantiques pour l'alimentation des juvéniles et des adultes, et grandes rivières propres — principalement la Gironde et la Dordogne — avec des lits de gravier pour le frai.
