es.description: La orquídea fantasma es una orquídea pálida y sin hojas sin clorofila que vive completamente bajo tierra excepto cuando florece, obteniendo todos sus nutrientes a través de hongos micorrícicos, famosamente impredecible en sus apariciones.
es.endangerment: Considerada extinta en Gran Bretaña durante 23 años antes de su redescubrimiento en 2009, sigue siendo una de las plantas más raras de Europa. La deforestación y los cambios en el manejo del bosque agotan los hongos de los que depende.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosques antiguos de hayas, robles y bosques mixtos con profunda hojarasca sin perturbaciones y redes de hongos micorrícicos intactas, particularmente en bosques de Europa Central desde Gales hasta Rusia.
es.actions: Proteger los bosques antiguos con mínima perturbación del suelo, evitar la tala rasa que destruye las redes micorrícicas y apoyar programas de encuestas botánicas para localizar y monitorear poblaciones raras.

pt.description: A orquídea-fantasma é uma orquídea pálida e sem folhas sem clorofila que vive completamente underground exceto quando floresce, obtendo todos os seus nutrientes através de fungos micorrízicos, famosamente imprevisível em seus aparecimentos.
pt.endangerment: Considerada extinta na Grã-Bretanha por 23 anos antes da redescoberta em 2009, continua sendo uma das plantas mais raras da Europa. O desmatamento e as mudanças no manejo florestal esgotam os fungos dos quais depende.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Florestas antigas de faia, carvalho e mistas com serapilheira profunda sem perturbações e redes de fungos micorrízicos intactas, particularmente em florestas da Europa Central do País de Gales à Rússia.
pt.actions: Proteger florestas antigas com mínima perturbação do solo, evitar o corte raso que destrói as redes micorrízicas e apoiar programas de levantamento botânico para localizar e monitorar populações raras.

fr.description: L'orchidée fantôme est une orchidée pâle et sans feuilles dépourvue de chlorophylle qui vit entièrement sous terre sauf lors de la floraison, obtenant tous ses nutriments via des champignons mycorhiziens, célèbre pour l'imprévisibilité de ses apparitions.
fr.endangerment: Considérée comme éteinte en Grande-Bretagne pendant 23 ans avant sa redécouverte en 2009, elle reste l'une des plantes les plus rares d'Europe. La déforestation et les changements dans la gestion des boisements épuisent les champignons dont elle dépend.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêts anciennes de hêtres, chênes et forêts mixtes avec une litière de feuilles profonde non perturbée et des réseaux de champignons mycorhiziens intacts, notamment dans les forêts d'Europe centrale du Pays de Galles à la Russie.
fr.actions: Protéger les forêts anciennes avec une perturbation minimale du sol, éviter les coupes rases qui détruisent les réseaux mycorhiziens et soutenir des programmes d'inventaire botanique pour localiser et surveiller les populations rares.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Franklin tree is a small flowering tree last seen growing wild in 1803 along the Altamaha River, Georgia — all surviving specimens worldwide are descendants of seeds collected by John and William Bartram.
endangerment: The species was extinct in the wild by the early 19th century, possibly due to a soil-borne pathogen combined with its naturally tiny and restricted range.
ecosystemNeeds: Moist, well-drained, sandy, acidic soils near stream banks in the coastal plain of Georgia — its original habitat now entirely converted or disturbed.
actions: Support botanical garden and arboretum cultivation to maintain the captive population, fund genetic diversity studies, and investigate historical habitat for reintroduction feasibility.

en.description: The Franklin tree is a small flowering tree last seen growing wild in 1803 along the Altamaha River, Georgia — all surviving specimens worldwide are descendants of seeds collected by John and William Bartram.
en.endangerment: The species was extinct in the wild by the early 19th century, possibly due to a soil-borne pathogen combined with its naturally tiny and restricted range.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Moist, well-drained, sandy, acidic soils near stream banks in the coastal plain of Georgia — its original habitat now entirely converted or disturbed.
en.actions: Support botanical garden and arboretum cultivation to maintain the captive population, fund genetic diversity studies, and investigate historical habitat for reintroduction feasibility.

es.description: El árbol Franklin es un pequeño árbol con flores visto por última vez creciendo en la naturaleza en 1803 a lo largo del Río Altamaha, Georgia —todos los especímenes supervivientes en el mundo son descendientes de semillas recolectadas por John y William Bartram.
es.endangerment: La especie estaba extinta en la naturaleza a principios del siglo XIX, posiblemente debido a un patógeno transmitido por el suelo combinado con su rango naturalmente pequeño y restringido.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Suelos arenosos, ácidos, bien drenados y húmedos cerca de orillas de arroyos en la llanura costera de Georgia —su hábitat original ahora completamente convertido o alterado.
es.actions: Apoyar el cultivo en jardines botánicos y arboretos para mantener la población en cautiverio, financiar estudios de diversidad genética e investigar el hábitat histórico para la viabilidad de la reintroducción.

pt.description: A árvore-franklin é uma pequena árvore com flores vista pela última vez crescendo na natureza em 1803 ao longo do Rio Altamaha, Geórgia — todos os espécimes sobreviventes no mundo são descendentes de sementes coletadas por John e William Bartram.
pt.endangerment: A espécie estava extinta na natureza no início do século XIX, possivelmente devido a um patógeno transmitido pelo solo combinado com sua área naturalmente pequena e restrita.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Solos arenosos, ácidos, bem drenados e úmidos perto das margens de córregos na planície costeira da Geórgia — seu habitat original agora completamente convertido ou perturbado.
pt.actions: Apoiar o cultivo em jardins botânicos e arboretos para manter a população em cativeiro, financiar estudos de diversidade genética e investigar o habitat histórico para viabilidade de reintrodução.

fr.description: L'arbre de Franklin est un petit arbre à fleurs vu pour la dernière fois à l'état sauvage en 1803 le long de la rivière Altamaha, en Géorgie — tous les spécimens survivants dans le monde sont des descendants de graines collectées par John et William Bartram.
fr.endangerment: L'espèce était éteinte à l'état sauvage au début du XIXe siècle, peut-être en raison d'un pathogène du sol combiné à son aire naturellement petite et restreinte.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Sols sableux, acides, bien drainés et humides près des rives de ruisseaux dans la plaine côtière de Géorgie — son habitat d'origine désormais entièrement converti ou perturbé.
fr.actions: Soutenir la culture dans des jardins botaniques et des arboretums pour maintenir la population en captivité, financer des études sur la diversité génétique et étudier l'habitat historique pour la faisabilité d'une réintroduction.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The American burying beetle is North America's largest carrion beetle, once widespread but now reduced to a handful of sites; known for biparental care of larvae on buried animal carcasses.
endangerment: Loss of forest and grassland habitat, decline of large carrion from overhunting of prairie wildlife, pesticides, and light pollution from urbanisation have all contributed to catastrophic decline.
ecosystemNeeds: Forest, shrubland, and grassland habitats with accessible small to medium-sized animal carcasses for breeding and sufficient soft soil for burial at Block Island, Rhode Island, and scattered interior sites.
actions: Support USFWS reintroduction programmes, fund habitat management to maintain appropriate carrion availability, and reduce pesticide use in key recovery areas.

en.description: The American burying beetle is North America's largest carrion beetle, once widespread but now reduced to a handful of sites; known for biparental care of larvae on buried animal carcasses.
en.endangerment: Loss of forest and grassland habitat, decline of large carrion from overhunting of prairie wildlife, pesticides, and light pollution from urbanisation have all contributed to catastrophic decline.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Forest, shrubland, and grassland habitats with accessible small to medium-sized animal carcasses for breeding and sufficient soft soil for burial at Block Island, Rhode Island, and scattered interior sites.
en.actions: Support USFWS reintroduction programmes, fund habitat management to maintain appropriate carrion availability, and reduce pesticide use in key recovery areas.

es.description: El escarabajo enterrador americano es el mayor escarabajo necrófago de América del Norte, antes ampliamente distribuido pero ahora reducido a un puñado de sitios; conocido por el cuidado biparental de las larvas en carcasas de animales enterradas.
es.endangerment: La pérdida de hábitat forestal y de pastizales, el declive de carroña grande por la caza excesiva de fauna de praderas, los pesticidas y la contaminación lumínica de la urbanización han contribuido a un declive catastrófico.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Hábitats de bosque, matorral y pastizal con carcasas accesibles de animales pequeños a medianos para la reproducción y suelo blando suficiente para enterrar en Block Island, Rhode Island, y sitios interiores dispersos.
es.actions: Apoyar los programas de reintroducción del USFWS, financiar el manejo del hábitat para mantener la disponibilidad apropiada de carroña y reducir el uso de pesticidas en las áreas clave de recuperación.

pt.description: O besouro-enterrador-americano é o maior besouro necrófago da América do Norte, antes amplamente distribuído mas agora reduzido a alguns poucos locais; conhecido pelo cuidado biparental das larvas em carcaças de animais enterradas.
pt.endangerment: A perda de habitat florestal e de pastagens, o declínio de carcaças grandes pela caça excessiva da fauna das pradarias, os pesticidas e a poluição luminosa da urbanização contribuíram para um declínio catastrófico.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Habitats de floresta, arbustos e pastagens com carcaças acessíveis de animais pequenos a médios para reprodução e solo suficientemente mole para enterramento na Ilha Block, Rhode Island, e locais interiores dispersos.
pt.actions: Apoiar os programas de reintrodução do USFWS, financiar o manejo do habitat para manter a disponibilidade adequada de carcaças e reduzir o uso de pesticidas nas principais áreas de recuperação.

fr.description: Le nécrophore américain est le plus grand coléoptère charognard d'Amérique du Nord, autrefois largement répandu mais désormais réduit à quelques sites ; connu pour les soins biparentaux des larves sur des carcasses d'animaux enterrées.
fr.endangerment: La perte d'habitat forestier et de prairie, le déclin des grandes charognes dû à la surexploitation de la faune des prairies, les pesticides et la pollution lumineuse due à l'urbanisation ont tous contribué à un déclin catastrophique.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Habitats forestiers, arbustifs et de prairie avec des carcasses accessibles de petits à moyens animaux pour la reproduction et un sol suffisamment meuble pour l'enfouissement à Block Island, Rhode Island, et dans des sites intérieurs dispersés.
fr.actions: Soutenir les programmes de réintroduction de l'USFWS, financer la gestion de l'habitat pour maintenir une disponibilité appropriée en charognes et réduire l'utilisation de pesticides dans les zones de récupération clés.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The peacock parachute spider is a large, arboreal tarantula from a small area in Andhra Pradesh, India, with striking metallic-blue legs and one of the most spectacular appearances of any spider.
endangerment: Collected intensively for the exotic pet trade, it is found only in a single area of dry deciduous forest; deforestation for timber and agriculture further reduces its habitat.
ecosystemNeeds: Large, old deciduous trees with deep bark crevices and hollows for burrowing, in dry forest habitat in the Gooty area of Andhra Pradesh, India.
actions: Support CITES Appendix II listing enforcement for Poecilotheria tarantulas, fund captive breeding to supply the pet trade legally, and back local forest protection in Andhra Pradesh.

en.description: The peacock parachute spider is a large, arboreal tarantula from a small area in Andhra Pradesh, India, with striking metallic-blue legs and one of the most spectacular appearances of any spider.
en.endangerment: Collected intensively for the exotic pet trade, it is found only in a single area of dry deciduous forest; deforestation for timber and agriculture further reduces its habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, old deciduous trees with deep bark crevices and hollows for burrowing, in dry forest habitat in the Gooty area of Andhra Pradesh, India.
en.actions: Support CITES Appendix II listing enforcement for Poecilotheria tarantulas, fund captive breeding to supply the pet trade legally, and back local forest protection in Andhra Pradesh.

es.description: La araña paracaídas pavo real es una gran tarántula arborícola de una pequeña área en Andhra Pradesh, India, con llamativas patas de color azul metálico y una de las apariencias más espectaculares de cualquier araña.
es.endangerment: Recolectada intensamente para el comercio de mascotas exóticas, solo se encuentra en una sola área de bosque caducifolio seco; la deforestación para madera y agricultura reduce aún más su hábitat.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Árboles caducifolios viejos y grandes con profundas grietas de corteza y oquedades para madrigueras, en hábitat de bosque seco en el área de Gooty, Andhra Pradesh, India.
es.actions: Apoyar la aplicación del Apéndice II de la CITES para las tarántulas Poecilotheria, financiar la cría en cautiverio para suministrar legalmente el mercado de mascotas y respaldar la protección local del bosque en Andhra Pradesh.

pt.description: A aranha-paraquedas-pavão é uma grande tarântula arborícola de uma pequena área em Andhra Pradesh, Índia, com impressionantes pernas de cor azul metálico e uma das aparências mais espetaculares de qualquer aranha.
pt.endangerment: Coletada intensamente para o comércio de animais exóticos de estimação, é encontrada apenas em uma única área de floresta caducifólia seca; o desmatamento para madeira e agricultura reduz ainda mais seu habitat.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Árvores caducifólias velhas e grandes com fissuras profundas na casca e ocos para tocas, em habitat de floresta seca na área de Gooty, Andhra Pradesh, Índia.
pt.actions: Apoiar a aplicação do Apêndice II da CITES para as tarântulas Poecilotheria, financiar a criação em cativeiro para abastecer legalmente o mercado de animais de estimação e apoiar a proteção local da floresta em Andhra Pradesh.

fr.description: L'araignée-parachute paon est une grande tarentule arboricole d'une petite zone de l'Andhra Pradesh, en Inde, avec des pattes bleu métallique frappantes et l'un des aspects les plus spectaculaires de toutes les araignées.
fr.endangerment: Collectée intensément pour le commerce des animaux de compagnie exotiques, elle ne se trouve que dans une seule zone de forêt tropicale sèche ; la déforestation pour le bois et l'agriculture réduit encore son habitat.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Grands et vieux arbres caducs avec de profondes crevasses dans l'écorce et des cavités pour les terriers, dans l'habitat de forêt sèche dans la région de Gooty, Andhra Pradesh, Inde.
fr.actions: Soutenir l'application de l'Annexe II de la CITES pour les tarentules Poecilotheria, financer l'élevage en captivité pour approvisionner légalement le marché des animaux de compagnie et soutenir la protection locale des forêts en Andhra Pradesh.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The rosalia longicorn is a strikingly beautiful pale-blue beetle with long antennae banded in black and white, dependent on large, sun-exposed dead or dying beech trees for its larval development.
endangerment: Loss of old-growth and veteran beech trees across European mountain forests due to forestry practices that remove dead wood has eliminated this beetle from much of its former range.
ecosystemNeeds: Old-growth beech and other deciduous forests with abundant sun-exposed dead wood and veteran trees at warm, southerly forest edges in central and southern European mountains.
actions: Support EU Habitats Directive implementation for beech forest habitats, advocate for retention of deadwood in managed forests, and fund saproxylic beetle surveys to guide forestry policy.

en.description: The rosalia longicorn is a strikingly beautiful pale-blue beetle with long antennae banded in black and white, dependent on large, sun-exposed dead or dying beech trees for its larval development.
en.endangerment: Loss of old-growth and veteran beech trees across European mountain forests due to forestry practices that remove dead wood has eliminated this beetle from much of its former range.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Old-growth beech and other deciduous forests with abundant sun-exposed dead wood and veteran trees at warm, southerly forest edges in central and southern European mountains.
en.actions: Support EU Habitats Directive implementation for beech forest habitats, advocate for retention of deadwood in managed forests, and fund saproxylic beetle surveys to guide forestry policy.

es.description: El cerambícido de Rosalia es un escarabajo de color azul pálido y belleza llamativa con largas antenas bandeadas en blanco y negro, que depende de grandes hayas muertas o moribundas expuestas al sol para el desarrollo larval.
es.endangerment: La pérdida de hayas antiguas y veteranas en los bosques montañosos europeos debido a prácticas forestales que eliminan la madera muerta ha eliminado este escarabajo de gran parte de su área histórica.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Hayas antiguas y otros bosques caducifolios con abundante madera muerta expuesta al sol y árboles veteranos en cálidos bordes forestales meridionales en las montañas de Europa central y meridional.
es.actions: Apoyar la implementación de la Directiva de Hábitats de la UE para hábitats de bosque de hayas, abogar por la retención de madera muerta en bosques gestionados y financiar encuestas de coleópteros sapróxilos para orientar la política forestal.

pt.description: O cerambícido-de-rosalia é um besouro de cor azul-pálida e beleza notável com longas antenas bandeadas em preto e branco, dependente de grandes faias mortas ou moribundas expostas ao sol para o desenvolvimento larval.
pt.endangerment: A perda de faias antigas e veteranas nas florestas montanhosas europeias devido a práticas florestais que removem a madeira morta eliminou este besouro de grande parte de sua área histórica.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Florestas antigas de faia e outras caducifólias com abundante madeira morta exposta ao sol e árvores veteranas em bordas florestais quentes e meridionais nas montanhas da Europa central e meridional.
pt.actions: Apoiar a implementação da Diretiva Habitats da UE para habitats de florestas de faia, defender a retenção de madeira morta em florestas gerenciadas e financiar levantamentos de besouros saproxílicos para orientar a política florestal.

fr.description: Le rosalie des Alpes est un coléoptère d'un bleu pâle frappant avec de longues antennes rayées de noir et blanc, dépendant de grands hêtres morts ou mourants exposés au soleil pour son développement larvaire.
fr.endangerment: La perte de vieux hêtres et d'arbres-vétérans dans les forêts de montagne européennes due aux pratiques sylvicoles qui éliminent le bois mort a fait disparaître ce coléoptère d'une grande partie de son aire historique.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Vieilles hêtraies et autres forêts caduques avec abondamment de bois mort exposé au soleil et des arbres-vétérans aux lisières forestières chaudes et méridionales dans les montagnes d'Europe centrale et méridionale.
fr.actions: Soutenir la mise en œuvre de la directive Habitats de l'UE pour les habitats de hêtraies, plaider pour le maintien du bois mort dans les forêts gérées et financer des enquêtes sur les coléoptères saproxyliques pour orienter la politique forestière.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The giant clam is the world's largest bivalve, reaching over 1 metre across and weighing up to 300 kg, a slow-growing reef dweller that hosts symbiotic algae and can live over a century.
endangerment: Overharvesting for meat, shells, and the aquarium trade has eliminated it from large parts of its former range; bleaching events linked to climate change also kill the symbiotic algae it depends on.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow tropical coral reefs and sandy lagoons throughout the Indo-Pacific, with clear water, high light penetration for its symbiotic algae, and minimal poaching pressure.
actions: Support marine protected areas that ban clam collection, fund giant clam mariculture to supply seafood markets sustainably, and back coral reef climate protection.

en.description: The giant clam is the world's largest bivalve, reaching over 1 metre across and weighing up to 300 kg, a slow-growing reef dweller that hosts symbiotic algae and can live over a century.
en.endangerment: Overharvesting for meat, shells, and the aquarium trade has eliminated it from large parts of its former range; bleaching events linked to climate change also kill the symbiotic algae it depends on.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow tropical coral reefs and sandy lagoons throughout the Indo-Pacific, with clear water, high light penetration for its symbiotic algae, and minimal poaching pressure.
en.actions: Support marine protected areas that ban clam collection, fund giant clam mariculture to supply seafood markets sustainably, and back coral reef climate protection.

es.description: La almeja gigante es el bivalvo más grande del mundo, alcanzando más de 1 metro de ancho y pesando hasta 300 kg, un habitante de arrecifes de crecimiento lento que alberga algas simbióticas y puede vivir más de un siglo.
es.endangerment: La sobreexplotación por carne, conchas y el comercio de acuarios ha eliminado este animal de grandes partes de su área histórica; los eventos de blanqueamiento vinculados al cambio climático también matan las algas simbióticas de las que depende.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arrecifes de coral tropicales poco profundos y lagunas arenosas en todo el Indo-Pacífico, con agua clara, alta penetración de luz para sus algas simbióticas y mínima presión de caza furtiva.
es.actions: Apoyar áreas marinas protegidas que prohíban la recolección de almejas, financiar la maricultura de almeja gigante para abastecer los mercados de mariscos de forma sostenible y respaldar la protección climática de los arrecifes de coral.

pt.description: A almeijão-gigante é o maior bivalvo do mundo, atingindo mais de 1 metro de largura e pesando até 300 kg, um habitante de recifes de crescimento lento que hospeda algas simbióticas e pode viver mais de um século.
pt.endangerment: A superexploração por carne, conchas e o comércio de aquário eliminou este animal de grandes partes de sua área histórica; os eventos de branqueamento ligados às mudanças climáticas também matam as algas simbióticas das quais depende.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Recifes de coral tropicais rasos e lagoas arenosas em todo o Indo-Pacífico, com água clara, alta penetração de luz para suas algas simbióticas e mínima pressão de caça furtiva.
pt.actions: Apoiar áreas marinhas protegidas que proibam a coleta de almeijões, financiar a maricultura de almeijão-gigante para abastecer os mercados de frutos do mar de forma sustentável e apoiar a proteção climática dos recifes de coral.

fr.description: Le bénitier géant est le plus grand bivalve du monde, atteignant plus d'un mètre de diamètre et pesant jusqu'à 300 kg, un habitant de récif à croissance lente qui héberge des algues symbiotiques et peut vivre plus d'un siècle.
fr.endangerment: La surexploitation pour la chair, les coquilles et le commerce d'aquarium l'a éliminé de grandes parties de son ancienne aire ; les épisodes de blanchissement liés au changement climatique tuent également les algues symbiotiques dont il dépend.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Récifs coralliens tropicaux peu profonds et lagons sableux dans tout l'Indo-Pacifique, avec une eau claire, une forte pénétration lumineuse pour ses algues symbiotiques et une pression de braconnage minimale.
fr.actions: Soutenir les zones marines protégées interdisant la collecte de bénitiers, financer la mariculture du bénitier géant pour approvisionner durablement les marchés de fruits de mer et soutenir la protection climatique des récifs coralliens.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The queen conch is a large, charismatic marine gastropod with a flared pink lip, once abundant across Caribbean seagrass beds and now overfished to near-collapse throughout its range.
endangerment: Decades of commercial overfishing for its prized flesh and shell has drastically reduced populations across the Caribbean. Habitat degradation from coastal development further limits recovery.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, warm, clear tropical waters with extensive seagrass meadows and sand flats in the Caribbean Sea and Gulf of Mexico, from Florida to Venezuela.
actions: Support fishing moratoriums and closed seasons in overfished areas, fund conch population surveys and mariculture research, and back CITES Appendix II trade controls.

en.description: The queen conch is a large, charismatic marine gastropod with a flared pink lip, once abundant across Caribbean seagrass beds and now overfished to near-collapse throughout its range.
en.endangerment: Decades of commercial overfishing for its prized flesh and shell has drastically reduced populations across the Caribbean. Habitat degradation from coastal development further limits recovery.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, warm, clear tropical waters with extensive seagrass meadows and sand flats in the Caribbean Sea and Gulf of Mexico, from Florida to Venezuela.
en.actions: Support fishing moratoriums and closed seasons in overfished areas, fund conch population surveys and mariculture research, and back CITES Appendix II trade controls.

es.description: El caracol reina es un gran gasterópodo marino carismático con un labio rosa abierto, antes abundante en los lechos de posidonia del Caribe y ahora sobreexplotado hasta casi el colapso en toda su área.
es.endangerment: Décadas de pesca comercial excesiva por su carne apreciada y su concha han reducido drásticamente las poblaciones en todo el Caribe. La degradación del hábitat por el desarrollo costero limita aún más la recuperación.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas tropicales cálidas y claras poco profundas con extensas praderas de posidonia y fondos de arena en el Mar Caribe y el Golfo de México, desde Florida hasta Venezuela.
es.actions: Apoyar moratorias de pesca y temporadas cerradas en áreas sobreexplotadas, financiar encuestas de población de caracol y investigación de maricultura y respaldar los controles de comercio del Apéndice II de CITES.

pt.description: O strombus-gigante é um grande gastrópode marinho carismático com um lábio rosa aberto, antes abundante nos leitos de ervas marinhas do Caribe e agora superexplorado quase ao colapso em toda a sua área.
pt.endangerment: Décadas de pesca comercial excessiva por sua carne apreciada e concha reduziram drasticamente as populações em todo o Caribe. A degradação do habitat pelo desenvolvimento costeiro limita ainda mais a recuperação.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas tropicais quentes e claras rasas com extensas pradarias de ervas marinhas e fundos arenosos no Mar do Caribe e no Golfo do México, desde a Flórida até a Venezuela.
pt.actions: Apoiar moratorias de pesca e temporadas fechadas em áreas superexploradas, financiar levantamentos populacionais de strombus e pesquisas de maricultura e apoiar os controles de comércio do Apêndice II da CITES.

fr.description: Le strombus géant est un grand gastéropode marin charismatique au bord rose évasé, autrefois abondant dans les herbiers marins des Caraïbes et désormais surpêché jusqu'au quasi-effondrement dans toute son aire.
fr.endangerment: Des décennies de surpêche commerciale pour sa chair prisée et sa coquille ont considérablement réduit les populations dans toutes les Caraïbes. La dégradation de l'habitat due au développement côtier limite encore le rétablissement.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Eaux tropicales chaudes, claires et peu profondes avec d'importants herbiers marins et des fonds sableux dans la mer des Caraïbes et le golfe du Mexique, de la Floride au Venezuela.
fr.actions: Soutenir des moratoires de pêche et des saisons fermées dans les zones surpêchées, financer des enquêtes sur les populations de strombes et la recherche en mariculture et soutenir les contrôles commerciaux de l'Annexe II de la CITES.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The white-clawed crayfish is Europe's only native crayfish in most of its range, a slow-growing, sensitive indicator of clean water quality that has disappeared from much of its former habitat.
endangerment: Introduction of the North American signal crayfish brought crayfish plague, a disease that rapidly kills native crayfish; competition and habitat degradation compound the threat.
ecosystemNeeds: Clean, cool, well-oxygenated rivers and streams with rocky substrates and undercut banks for shelter in western and central Europe, free from invasive signal crayfish.
actions: Support arksite creation — isolated catchments or ponds where native crayfish are protected from plague — fund crayfish plague research, and back strict regulation of signal crayfish.

en.description: The white-clawed crayfish is Europe's only native crayfish in most of its range, a slow-growing, sensitive indicator of clean water quality that has disappeared from much of its former habitat.
en.endangerment: Introduction of the North American signal crayfish brought crayfish plague, a disease that rapidly kills native crayfish; competition and habitat degradation compound the threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Clean, cool, well-oxygenated rivers and streams with rocky substrates and undercut banks for shelter in western and central Europe, free from invasive signal crayfish.
en.actions: Support arksite creation — isolated catchments or ponds where native crayfish are protected from plague — fund crayfish plague research, and back strict regulation of signal crayfish.

es.description: El cangrejo de río de patas blancas es el único cangrejo de río nativo de Europa en la mayor parte de su área, un indicador sensible y de crecimiento lento de la calidad del agua limpia que ha desaparecido de gran parte de su hábitat histórico.
es.endangerment: La introducción del cangrejo de río señal norteamericano trajo la plaga del cangrejo de río, una enfermedad que mata rápidamente a los cangrejos de río nativos; la competencia y la degradación del hábitat agravan la amenaza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Ríos y arroyos limpios, frescos y bien oxigenados con sustratos rocosos y orillas excavadas para refugio en Europa occidental y central, libres del cangrejo señal invasor.
es.actions: Apoyar la creación de arksites —cuencas o estanques aislados donde los cangrejos nativos están protegidos de la plaga— financiar la investigación de la plaga del cangrejo y respaldar la regulación estricta del cangrejo señal.

pt.description: O lagostim-de-patas-brancas é o único lagostim nativo da Europa na maior parte de sua área, um indicador sensível e de crescimento lento da qualidade da água limpa que desapareceu de grande parte de seu habitat histórico.
pt.endangerment: A introdução do lagostim-sinal norte-americano trouxe a praga do lagostim, uma doença que mata rapidamente os lagostins nativos; a competição e a degradação do habitat agravam a ameaça.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Rios e córregos limpos, frios e bem oxigenados com substratos rochosos e margens escavadas para abrigo na Europa ocidental e central, livres do lagostim-sinal invasor.
pt.actions: Apoiar a criação de arksites — bacias hidrográficas ou tanques isolados onde os lagostins nativos são protegidos da praga — financiar pesquisas sobre a praga do lagostim e apoiar a regulamentação rigorosa do lagostim-sinal.

fr.description: L'écrevisse à pattes blanches est le seul écrevisse indigène d'Europe dans la majeure partie de son aire, un indicateur sensible et à croissance lente de la qualité de l'eau propre qui a disparu d'une grande partie de son habitat historique.
fr.endangerment: L'introduction de l'écrevisse signal nord-américaine a apporté la peste des écrevisses, une maladie qui tue rapidement les écrevisses indigènes ; la concurrence et la dégradation de l'habitat aggravent la menace.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Rivières et ruisseaux propres, frais et bien oxygénés avec des substrats rocheux et des berges creusées pour l'abri en Europe occidentale et centrale, sans écrevisse signal envahissante.
fr.actions: Soutenir la création d'arksites — bassins versants ou étangs isolés où les écrevisses indigènes sont protégées de la peste — financer la recherche sur la peste des écrevisses et soutenir une réglementation stricte de l'écrevisse signal.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Lange's metalmark butterfly is a small, strikingly patterned butterfly whose entire world population is confined to a single sandy ridge at Antioch Dunes National Wildlife Refuge in California.
endangerment: Industrial sand mining in the 20th century destroyed most of the Antioch Dunes habitat; the sole host plant — buckwheat — must be managed intensively to sustain the butterfly.
ecosystemNeeds: Specific inland sand dune habitat at Antioch, California, with abundant naked stemmed buckwheat, the only host plant for larvae and nectar source for adults.
actions: Support USFWS management of Antioch Dunes National Wildlife Refuge, fund buckwheat planting and dune restoration, and engage in emergency captive breeding as a hedge against extinction.

en.description: Lange's metalmark butterfly is a small, strikingly patterned butterfly whose entire world population is confined to a single sandy ridge at Antioch Dunes National Wildlife Refuge in California.
en.endangerment: Industrial sand mining in the 20th century destroyed most of the Antioch Dunes habitat; the sole host plant — buckwheat — must be managed intensively to sustain the butterfly.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Specific inland sand dune habitat at Antioch, California, with abundant naked stemmed buckwheat, the only host plant for larvae and nectar source for adults.
en.actions: Support USFWS management of Antioch Dunes National Wildlife Refuge, fund buckwheat planting and dune restoration, and engage in emergency captive breeding as a hedge against extinction.

es.description: La mariposa metalmark de Lange es una pequeña mariposa de llamativos patrones cuya población mundial completa está confinada a una sola cresta arenosa en el Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre de Antioch Dunes en California.
es.endangerment: La minería industrial de arena en el siglo XX destruyó la mayor parte del hábitat de las Dunas de Antioch; la única planta huésped —trigo sarraceno— debe gestionarse intensamente para sostener la mariposa.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Hábitat específico de dunas de arena interiores en Antioch, California, con abundante trigo sarraceno de tallo desnudo, la única planta huésped para larvas y fuente de néctar para adultos.
es.actions: Apoyar el manejo del Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre de Antioch Dunes del USFWS, financiar la plantación de trigo sarraceno y la restauración de dunas y participar en la cría de emergencia en cautiverio como salvaguarda contra la extinción.

pt.description: A borboleta-metalmark-de-lange é uma pequena borboleta de padrões impressionantes cuja população mundial inteira está confinada a uma única crista arenosa no Refúgio Nacional de Vida Silvestre das Dunas de Antioch na Califórnia.
pt.endangerment: A mineração industrial de areia no século XX destruiu a maior parte do habitat das Dunas de Antioch; a única planta hospedeira — trigo-sarraceno — deve ser gerenciada intensivamente para sustentar a borboleta.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Habitat específico de dunas de areia interiores em Antioch, Califórnia, com abundante trigo-sarraceno de caule nu, a única planta hospedeira para larvas e fonte de néctar para adultos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o manejo do Refúgio Nacional de Vida Silvestre das Dunas de Antioch do USFWS, financiar o plantio de trigo-sarraceno e a restauração de dunas e participar da criação de emergência em cativeiro como salvaguarda contra a extinção.

fr.description: Le papillon métal de Lange est un petit papillon aux motifs frappants dont toute la population mondiale est confinée à une seule crête sablonneuse du refuge national de faune sauvage d'Antioch Dunes en Californie.
fr.endangerment: L'extraction de sable industrielle au XXe siècle a détruit la majeure partie de l'habitat des dunes d'Antioch ; la seule plante hôte — le sarrasin — doit être gérée intensivement pour soutenir le papillon.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Habitat spécifique de dunes de sable intérieures à Antioch, en Californie, avec du sarrasin à tige nue en abondance, la seule plante hôte pour les larves et source de nectar pour les adultes.
fr.actions: Soutenir la gestion du refuge national de faune sauvage d'Antioch Dunes de l'USFWS, financer la plantation de sarrasin et la restauration des dunes et s'engager dans un élevage d'urgence en captivité comme protection contre l'extinction.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The noble crayfish is the largest native European crayfish, once widespread across freshwater systems from Scandinavia to Turkey and a culturally important species in Scandinavian cuisine.
endangerment: Crayfish plague, introduced via North American crayfish species, has devastated populations across Europe; water pollution, habitat loss, and climate warming compound the threat.
ecosystemNeeds: Clean, calcium-rich lakes and rivers with rocky substrates for shelter and abundant invertebrate and plant food in Scandinavia, central Europe, and the Balkans.
actions: Support plague-free refuge sites for noble crayfish, back strict regulation of North American crayfish introductions, and fund genetic banking and restocking programmes.

en.description: The noble crayfish is the largest native European crayfish, once widespread across freshwater systems from Scandinavia to Turkey and a culturally important species in Scandinavian cuisine.
en.endangerment: Crayfish plague, introduced via North American crayfish species, has devastated populations across Europe; water pollution, habitat loss, and climate warming compound the threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Clean, calcium-rich lakes and rivers with rocky substrates for shelter and abundant invertebrate and plant food in Scandinavia, central Europe, and the Balkans.
en.actions: Support plague-free refuge sites for noble crayfish, back strict regulation of North American crayfish introductions, and fund genetic banking and restocking programmes.

es.description: El cangrejo de río noble es el mayor cangrejo de río nativo europeo, antes ampliamente distribuido en sistemas de agua dulce desde Escandinavia hasta Turquía y una especie culturalmente importante en la cocina escandinava.
es.endangerment: La plaga del cangrejo de río, introducida a través de especies de cangrejo norteamericanas, ha devastado poblaciones en toda Europa; la contaminación del agua, la pérdida de hábitat y el calentamiento climático agravan la amenaza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Lagos y ríos limpios y ricos en calcio con sustratos rocosos para refugio y abundante alimento de invertebrados y plantas en Escandinavia, Europa central y los Balcanes.
es.actions: Apoyar sitios de refugio libres de plaga para el cangrejo noble, respaldar la regulación estricta de la introducción de cangrejos norteamericanos y financiar programas de bancos genéticos y repoblación.

pt.description: O lagostim-nobre é o maior lagostim nativo europeu, antes amplamente distribuído em sistemas de água doce desde a Escandinávia até a Turquia e uma espécie culturalmente importante na culinária escandinava.
pt.endangerment: A praga do lagostim, introduzida através de espécies de lagostim norte-americanas, devastou populações em toda a Europa; a poluição da água, a perda de habitat e o aquecimento climático agravam a ameaça.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Lagos e rios limpos e ricos em cálcio com substratos rochosos para abrigo e abundante alimento de invertebrados e plantas na Escandinávia, Europa central e nos Bálcãs.
pt.actions: Apoiar locais de refúgio livres de praga para o lagostim nobre, apoiar a regulamentação rigorosa da introdução de lagostins norte-americanos e financiar programas de banco genético e repovoamento.

fr.description: L'écrevisse à pattes rouges est le plus grand écrevisse indigène européen, autrefois répandu dans les systèmes d'eau douce de la Scandinavie à la Turquie et une espèce culturellement importante dans la cuisine scandinave.
fr.endangerment: La peste des écrevisses, introduite par des espèces d'écrevisses nord-américaines, a dévasté les populations dans toute l'Europe ; la pollution de l'eau, la perte d'habitat et le réchauffement climatique aggravent la menace.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Lacs et rivières propres et riches en calcium avec des substrats rocheux pour l'abri et de la nourriture abondante en invertébrés et plantes en Scandinavie, en Europe centrale et dans les Balkans.
fr.actions: Soutenir des sites refuges exempts de peste pour l'écrevisse à pattes rouges, soutenir une réglementation stricte des introductions d'écrevisses nord-américaines et financer des programmes de banques génétiques et de repeuplement.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The scarce large blue butterfly has a remarkable life cycle involving deception of red ant colonies, whose larvae it mimics to gain care inside ant nests where it completes development.
endangerment: Drainage and intensification of wet meadows has eliminated both the great burnet plants its larvae feed on and the specific ant species it parasitises, causing severe range contraction.
ecosystemNeeds: Wet, traditionally managed hay meadows with abundant great burnet and colonies of Myrmica ants in central Europe, ideally grazed or cut after July to allow plant seeding.
actions: Support traditional low-intensity hay meadow management through EU agri-environment schemes, fund butterfly monitoring, and restore wet grassland habitats in central European river valleys.

en.description: The scarce large blue butterfly has a remarkable life cycle involving deception of red ant colonies, whose larvae it mimics to gain care inside ant nests where it completes development.
en.endangerment: Drainage and intensification of wet meadows has eliminated both the great burnet plants its larvae feed on and the specific ant species it parasitises, causing severe range contraction.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Wet, traditionally managed hay meadows with abundant great burnet and colonies of Myrmica ants in central Europe, ideally grazed or cut after July to allow plant seeding.
en.actions: Support traditional low-intensity hay meadow management through EU agri-environment schemes, fund butterfly monitoring, and restore wet grassland habitats in central European river valleys.

es.description: La mariposa azul grande escasa tiene un ciclo vital notable que implica el engaño de colonias de hormiga roja, cuyas larvas imita para obtener cuidado dentro de los hormigueros donde completa su desarrollo.
es.endangerment: El drenaje e intensificación de los prados húmedos ha eliminado tanto las plantas de sanguisorba que alimentan sus larvas como la especie de hormiga específica que parasita, causando una grave contracción del área.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Prados de heno húmedos y gestionados tradicionalmente con abundante sanguisorba y colonias de hormigas Myrmica en Europa central, idealmente pastados o cortados después de julio para permitir la siembra de plantas.
es.actions: Apoyar el manejo tradicional de prados de baja intensidad a través de los esquemas agroambientales de la UE, financiar el monitoreo de mariposas y restaurar hábitats de pastizales húmedos en los valles fluviales de Europa central.

pt.description: A borboleta-azul-grande-escassa tem um ciclo de vida notável envolvendo o engano de colônias de formiga vermelha, cujas larvas imita para obter cuidado dentro dos formigueiros onde completa seu desenvolvimento.
pt.endangerment: A drenagem e intensificação dos prados úmidos eliminou tanto as plantas de pimpinela que alimentam suas larvas quanto a espécie de formiga específica que parasita, causando uma grave contração da área.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Prados de feno úmidos e gerenciados tradicionalmente com abundante pimpinela e colônias de formigas Myrmica na Europa central, idealmente pastados ou cortados após julho para permitir a semeadura das plantas.
pt.actions: Apoiar o manejo tradicional de baixa intensidade de prados de feno através dos esquemas agro-ambientais da UE, financiar o monitoramento de borboletas e restaurar habitats de pastagens úmidas nos vales fluviais da Europa central.

fr.description: Le grand bleu rare a un cycle de vie remarquable impliquant la tromperie de colonies de fourmis rousses, dont il imite les larves pour être soigné à l'intérieur des fourmilières où il complète son développement.
fr.endangerment: Le drainage et l'intensification des prairies humides ont éliminé à la fois les plantes de sanguisorbe dont se nourrissent ses larves et l'espèce de fourmi spécifique qu'il parasite, provoquant une grave contraction de son aire.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Prairies de fauche humides et gérées traditionnellement avec une sanguisorbe abondante et des colonies de fourmis Myrmica en Europe centrale, idéalement pâturées ou fauchées après juillet pour permettre la dispersion des graines.
fr.actions: Soutenir la gestion traditionnelle de basse intensité des prairies de fauche via les mesures agro-environnementales de l'UE, financer le suivi des papillons et restaurer les habitats de prairies humides dans les vallées fluviales d'Europe centrale.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Triton's trumpet is a large, beautifully patterned marine snail and a crucial predator of crown-of-thorns starfish, which devastate coral reefs when their populations explode.
endangerment: Overcollection for its prized shell — used as a musical instrument, decoration, and souvenir — has severely reduced populations across the Indo-Pacific, indirectly damaging reef health.
ecosystemNeeds: Tropical and subtropical coral reefs, rocky shores, and seagrass beds across the Indo-Pacific, from the Red Sea to Hawaii, where it preys on echinoderms.
actions: Support bans on collection and trade of triton snail shells, fund repopulation efforts on reefs suffering crown-of-thorns outbreaks, and include it in marine protected area management plans.

en.description: Triton's trumpet is a large, beautifully patterned marine snail and a crucial predator of crown-of-thorns starfish, which devastate coral reefs when their populations explode.
en.endangerment: Overcollection for its prized shell — used as a musical instrument, decoration, and souvenir — has severely reduced populations across the Indo-Pacific, indirectly damaging reef health.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Tropical and subtropical coral reefs, rocky shores, and seagrass beds across the Indo-Pacific, from the Red Sea to Hawaii, where it preys on echinoderms.
en.actions: Support bans on collection and trade of triton snail shells, fund repopulation efforts on reefs suffering crown-of-thorns outbreaks, and include it in marine protected area management plans.

es.description: La trompeta de Tritón es un gran caracol marino hermosamente patterneado y un predador crucial de la corona de espinas, una estrella de mar que devasta los arrecifes de coral cuando sus poblaciones explotan.
es.endangerment: La sobrecolección de su apreciada concha —usada como instrumento musical, decoración y souvenir— ha reducido severamente las poblaciones en el Indo-Pacífico, dañando indirectamente la salud del arrecife.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arrecifes de coral tropicales y subtropicales, costas rocosas y lechos de posidonia en todo el Indo-Pacífico, desde el Mar Rojo hasta Hawái, donde depredadores equinodermos.
es.actions: Apoyar prohibiciones de recolección y comercio de conchas de trompeta de Tritón, financiar esfuerzos de repoblación en arrecifes que sufren brotes de corona de espinas e incluirla en los planes de gestión de áreas marinas protegidas.

pt.description: A trombeta-de-tritão é um grande caramujo marinho com padrão bonito e um predador crucial da coroa-de-espinhos, uma estrela-do-mar que devasta os recifes de coral quando suas populações explodem.
pt.endangerment: A supercoleta de sua concha apreciada — usada como instrumento musical, decoração e souvenir — reduziu severamente as populações no Indo-Pacífico, danificando indiretamente a saúde do recife.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Recifes de coral tropicais e subtropicais, costas rochosas e leitos de ervas marinhas em todo o Indo-Pacífico, desde o Mar Vermelho até o Havaí, onde predadores equinodermos.
pt.actions: Apoiar proibições de coleta e comércio de conchas de trombeta-de-tritão, financiar esforços de repovoamento em recifes que sofrem surtos de coroa-de-espinhos e incluí-la nos planos de manejo de áreas marinhas protegidas.

fr.description: La trompe de Triton est un grand gastéropode marin magnifiquement dessiné et un prédateur crucial de l'étoile de mer couronne d'épines, qui dévaste les récifs coralliens lorsque ses populations explosent.
fr.endangerment: La surexploitation pour sa coquille prisée — utilisée comme instrument de musique, décoration et souvenir — a sévèrement réduit les populations dans l'Indo-Pacifique, endommageant indirectement la santé des récifs.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Récifs coralliens tropicaux et subtropicaux, rivages rocheux et herbiers marins dans tout l'Indo-Pacifique, de la mer Rouge à Hawaii, où il prédate des échinodermes.
fr.actions: Soutenir les interdictions de collecte et de commerce des coquilles de trompe de Triton, financer des efforts de repeuplement sur les récifs souffrant d'infestations d'étoiles couronne d'épines et l'inclure dans les plans de gestion des zones marines protégées.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The American horseshoe crab is a living fossil unchanged for 450 million years, critical to Delaware Bay shorebirds that depend on its eggs during spring migration and to biomedical science for its blood.
endangerment: Overharvesting for bait and biomedical bleeding, combined with coastal development destroying spawning beaches, has reduced populations in some areas by over 90%.
ecosystemNeeds: Sandy Atlantic coast beaches for spawning in May–June, and shallow subtidal habitats along the Atlantic coast from Maine to the Yucatan for year-round foraging.
actions: Support horseshoe crab harvest limits and spawning beach protections, fund recombinant limulus amebocyte lysate research to replace biomedical blood extraction, and back beach lighting control during spawning season.

en.description: The American horseshoe crab is a living fossil unchanged for 450 million years, critical to Delaware Bay shorebirds that depend on its eggs during spring migration and to biomedical science for its blood.
en.endangerment: Overharvesting for bait and biomedical bleeding, combined with coastal development destroying spawning beaches, has reduced populations in some areas by over 90%.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Sandy Atlantic coast beaches for spawning in May–June, and shallow subtidal habitats along the Atlantic coast from Maine to the Yucatan for year-round foraging.
en.actions: Support horseshoe crab harvest limits and spawning beach protections, fund recombinant limulus amebocyte lysate research to replace biomedical blood extraction, and back beach lighting control during spawning season.

es.description: El cangrejo herradura americano es un fósil viviente sin cambios durante 450 millones de años, fundamental para las aves playeras de la Bahía de Delaware que dependen de sus huevos durante la migración primaveral y para la ciencia biomédica por su sangre.
es.endangerment: La sobreexplotación para cebo y extracción biomédica de sangre, combinada con el desarrollo costero que destruye las playas de desove, ha reducido las poblaciones en algunas áreas en más del 90%.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Playas costeras arenosas del Atlántico para el desove en mayo-junio, y hábitats subtidales poco profundos a lo largo de la costa atlántica desde Maine hasta el Yucatán para el forrajeo durante todo el año.
es.actions: Apoyar límites de cosecha de cangrejo herradura y protecciones de playas de desove, financiar investigaciones de lisado de amebocitos de límulo recombinante para reemplazar la extracción biomédica de sangre y respaldar el control de iluminación de playa durante la temporada de desove.

pt.description: O caranguejo-ferradura-americano é um fóssil vivo inalterado por 450 milhões de anos, fundamental para as aves limícolas da Baía de Delaware que dependem de seus ovos durante a migração primaveril e para a ciência biomédica por seu sangue.
pt.endangerment: A superexploração para isca e extração biomédica de sangue, combinada com o desenvolvimento costeiro que destrói as praias de desova, reduziu as populações em algumas áreas em mais de 90%.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Praias arenosas da costa atlântica para desova em maio-junho, e habitats subtidais rasos ao longo da costa atlântica do Maine ao Yucatán para forrageamento durante todo o ano.
pt.actions: Apoiar limites de colheita de caranguejo-ferradura e proteções de praias de desova, financiar pesquisas de lisado de amebócitos de límulo recombinante para substituir a extração biomédica de sangue e apoiar o controle de iluminação de praia durante a temporada de desova.

fr.description: Le limule américain est un fossile vivant inchangé depuis 450 millions d'années, essentiel pour les oiseaux limicoles de la baie du Delaware qui dépendent de ses œufs lors de la migration printanière et pour la science biomédicale pour son sang.
fr.endangerment: La surexploitation pour l'appât et le prélèvement biomédical de sang, combinée au développement côtier qui détruit les plages de ponte, a réduit les populations dans certaines zones de plus de 90%.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Plages côtières sablonneuses de l'Atlantique pour la ponte en mai-juin, et habitats subtidaux peu profonds le long de la côte atlantique du Maine au Yucatán pour la recherche de nourriture tout au long de l'année.
fr.actions: Soutenir les limites de récolte des limules et la protection des plages de ponte, financer la recherche sur le lysat d'amébocytes de Limulus recombinant pour remplacer le prélèvement biomédical de sang et soutenir le contrôle de l'éclairage des plages pendant la saison de ponte.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The giant Gippsland earthworm can reach 3 metres in length and is audible underground — a gurgling sound as it moves through the soil — endemic to a small area of blue clay soil in Victoria, Australia.
endangerment: Land clearing for dairy farming has converted most of its specialised moist blue clay hillside habitat; soil compaction and drainage from livestock add further pressure.
ecosystemNeeds: Deep, undisturbed, moist blue-grey clay soils on gentle hillsides in the Bass River Valley of South Gippsland, Victoria, free from intensive grazing or soil disturbance.
actions: Support the Giant Earthworm Reserve near Loch, Victoria, fund private land conservation agreements with dairy farmers, and raise awareness of this remarkable species.

en.description: The giant Gippsland earthworm can reach 3 metres in length and is audible underground — a gurgling sound as it moves through the soil — endemic to a small area of blue clay soil in Victoria, Australia.
en.endangerment: Land clearing for dairy farming has converted most of its specialised moist blue clay hillside habitat; soil compaction and drainage from livestock add further pressure.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Deep, undisturbed, moist blue-grey clay soils on gentle hillsides in the Bass River Valley of South Gippsland, Victoria, free from intensive grazing or soil disturbance.
en.actions: Support the Giant Earthworm Reserve near Loch, Victoria, fund private land conservation agreements with dairy farmers, and raise awareness of this remarkable species.

es.description: El gusano de tierra gigante de Gippsland puede alcanzar 3 metros de longitud y se escucha bajo tierra —un sonido gorgoteante mientras se mueve por el suelo— endémico de una pequeña área de suelo de arcilla azul en Victoria, Australia.
es.endangerment: La tala de tierras para la ganadería lechera ha convertido la mayor parte de su hábitat especializado de ladera de arcilla azul húmeda; la compactación del suelo y el drenaje del ganado añaden más presión.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Suelos de arcilla gris-azul profundos, sin perturbaciones y húmedos en suaves laderas en el Valle del Río Bass de South Gippsland, Victoria, libres de pastoreo intensivo o perturbación del suelo.
es.actions: Apoyar la Reserva de Gusanos Gigantes cerca de Loch, Victoria, financiar acuerdos de conservación de tierras privadas con ganaderos lecheros y aumentar la conciencia de esta especie extraordinaria.

pt.description: A minhoca-gigante-de-gippsland pode atingir 3 metros de comprimento e pode ser ouvida underground — um som de gorgolejo enquanto se move pelo solo — endêmica de uma pequena área de solo de argila azul em Victoria, Austrália.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento para a pecuária leiteira converteu a maior parte de seu habitat especializado de encosta de argila azul úmida; a compactação do solo e a drenagem pelo gado adicionam mais pressão.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Solos de argila cinza-azulada profundos, não perturbados e úmidos em encostas suaves no Vale do Rio Bass de South Gippsland, Victoria, livres de pastejo intensivo ou perturbação do solo.
pt.actions: Apoiar a Reserva da Minhoca Gigante perto de Loch, Victoria, financiar acordos de conservação de terras privadas com fazendeiros de leite e aumentar a conscientização sobre esta espécie extraordinária.

fr.description: Le ver de terre géant de Gippsland peut atteindre 3 mètres de longueur et s'entend sous terre — un son de gargouillement lorsqu'il se déplace dans le sol — endémique d'une petite zone d'argile bleue en Victoria, Australie.
fr.endangerment: Le défrichement des terres pour l'élevage laitier a converti la majeure partie de son habitat spécialisé de versant d'argile bleue humide ; le compactage du sol et le drainage par le bétail ajoutent une pression supplémentaire.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Sols d'argile gris-bleu profonds, non perturbés et humides sur des versants doux dans la vallée de la rivière Bass, dans le sud de Gippsland, Victoria, sans pâturage intensif ni perturbation du sol.
fr.actions: Soutenir la réserve du Ver de terre géant près de Loch, Victoria, financer des accords de conservation des terres privées avec les éleveurs laitiers et sensibiliser à cette espèce remarquable.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The thick-shelled river mussel is a freshwater bivalve that was once common across clean rivers of central Europe, now declining severely due to water quality degradation and loss of host fish.
endangerment: Agricultural nutrient pollution causing eutrophication, fine sediment from erosion smothering gravel beds, and decline of the salmonid host fish its larvae require have caused catastrophic range contraction.
ecosystemNeeds: Clean, well-oxygenated rivers with gravel and sand substrates, low nutrient levels, and healthy populations of host fish — primarily perch, gudgeon, and minnow — for larval development.
actions: Support riparian buffer zones and nutrient reduction in European river catchments, fund mussel propagation programmes, and back integrated river management plans that consider invertebrate needs.

en.description: The thick-shelled river mussel is a freshwater bivalve that was once common across clean rivers of central Europe, now declining severely due to water quality degradation and loss of host fish.
en.endangerment: Agricultural nutrient pollution causing eutrophication, fine sediment from erosion smothering gravel beds, and decline of the salmonid host fish its larvae require have caused catastrophic range contraction.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Clean, well-oxygenated rivers with gravel and sand substrates, low nutrient levels, and healthy populations of host fish — primarily perch, gudgeon, and minnow — for larval development.
en.actions: Support riparian buffer zones and nutrient reduction in European river catchments, fund mussel propagation programmes, and back integrated river management plans that consider invertebrate needs.

es.description: El mejillón de río de concha gruesa es un bivalvo de agua dulce que antes era común en los ríos limpios de Europa central, ahora en grave declive debido a la degradación de la calidad del agua y la pérdida de peces huésped.
es.endangerment: La contaminación por nutrientes agrícolas que causa eutrofización, el sedimento fino por erosión que asfixia los lechos de grava y el declive del salmónido huésped que requieren sus larvas han causado una contracción catastrófica del área.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Ríos limpios y bien oxigenados con sustratos de grava y arena, bajos niveles de nutrientes y poblaciones saludables de peces huésped —principalmente perca, gobio y minnow— para el desarrollo larval.
es.actions: Apoyar zonas tampón riparias y reducción de nutrientes en cuencas fluviales europeas, financiar programas de propagación de mejillones y respaldar planes integrados de gestión fluvial que consideren las necesidades de invertebrados.

pt.description: O mexilhão-de-rio-de-concha-grossa é um bivalvo de água doce que antes era comum em rios limpos da Europa central, agora em grave declínio devido à degradação da qualidade da água e à perda de peixes hospedeiros.
pt.endangerment: A poluição por nutrientes agrícolas causando eutrofização, o sedimento fino por erosão sufocando os leitos de cascalho e o declínio do salmônido hospedeiro que suas larvas necessitam causaram uma contração catastrófica da área.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Rios limpos e bem oxigenados com substratos de cascalho e areia, baixos níveis de nutrientes e populações saudáveis de peixes hospedeiros — principalmente perca, gobão e minnow — para o desenvolvimento larval.
pt.actions: Apoiar zonas tampão ripárias e redução de nutrientes em bacias fluviais europeias, financiar programas de propagação de mexilhões e apoiar planos integrados de manejo fluvial que considerem as necessidades dos invertebrados.

fr.description: La mulette épaisse est un bivalve d'eau douce autrefois commun dans les rivières propres d'Europe centrale, désormais en grave déclin en raison de la dégradation de la qualité de l'eau et de la disparition des poissons hôtes.
fr.endangerment: La pollution par les nutriments agricoles provoquant l'eutrophisation, les sédiments fins d'érosion étouffant les lits de gravier et le déclin des salmonidés hôtes dont les larves ont besoin ont provoqué une contraction catastrophique de l'aire.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Rivières propres et bien oxygénées avec des substrats de gravier et de sable, de faibles niveaux de nutriments et des populations saines de poissons hôtes — principalement la perche, le goujon et le vairon — pour le développement larvaire.
fr.actions: Soutenir les zones tampons ripariales et la réduction des nutriments dans les bassins versants européens, financer des programmes de propagation des moules et soutenir des plans intégrés de gestion des cours d'eau tenant compte des besoins des invertébrés.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The pink mucket is a freshwater mussel once widespread across major rivers of the eastern United States, now reduced to a handful of rivers where water quality remains adequate for its survival.
endangerment: Industrial and agricultural water pollution, channelisation and impoundment of rivers, and decline of the host fish needed for larval development have caused catastrophic population loss.
ecosystemNeeds: Medium to large rivers with clean, well-oxygenated water, stable gravel and sand substrates, and healthy populations of host fish — principally bass and sunfish — for larval attachment.
actions: Support Clean Water Act enforcement in Tennessee and Cumberland river drainages, fund mussel propagation and reintroduction by USFWS, and back host fish recovery programmes.

en.description: The pink mucket is a freshwater mussel once widespread across major rivers of the eastern United States, now reduced to a handful of rivers where water quality remains adequate for its survival.
en.endangerment: Industrial and agricultural water pollution, channelisation and impoundment of rivers, and decline of the host fish needed for larval development have caused catastrophic population loss.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Medium to large rivers with clean, well-oxygenated water, stable gravel and sand substrates, and healthy populations of host fish — principally bass and sunfish — for larval attachment.
en.actions: Support Clean Water Act enforcement in Tennessee and Cumberland river drainages, fund mussel propagation and reintroduction by USFWS, and back host fish recovery programmes.

es.description: El mejillón de bolsa rosada es un mejillón de agua dulce que alguna vez fue común en los grandes ríos del este de los Estados Unidos, ahora reducido a un puñado de ríos donde la calidad del agua sigue siendo adecuada para su supervivencia.
es.endangerment: La contaminación del agua industrial y agrícola, la canalización y el embalse de ríos, y el declive del pez huésped necesario para el desarrollo larval han causado una pérdida catastrófica de población.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Ríos medianos a grandes con agua limpia y bien oxigenada, sustratos estables de grava y arena, y poblaciones saludables de peces huésped —principalmente lubina y perca sol— para la fijación larval.
es.actions: Apoyar la aplicación de la Ley de Agua Limpia en los drenajes de los ríos Tennessee y Cumberland, financiar la propagación y reintroducción de mejillones por el USFWS y respaldar programas de recuperación de peces huésped.

pt.description: O mexilhão-de-saco-rosa é um mexilhão de água doce antes amplamente distribuído nos grandes rios do leste dos Estados Unidos, agora reduzido a alguns poucos rios onde a qualidade da água ainda é adequada para sua sobrevivência.
pt.endangerment: A poluição hídrica industrial e agrícola, a canalização e represamento de rios e o declínio do peixe hospedeiro necessário para o desenvolvimento larval causaram uma perda catastrófica de população.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Rios médios a grandes com água limpa e bem oxigenada, substratos estáveis de cascalho e areia e populações saudáveis de peixes hospedeiros — principalmente robalo e peixe-sol — para a fixação larval.
pt.actions: Apoiar a aplicação da Lei de Água Limpa nas bacias dos rios Tennessee e Cumberland, financiar a propagação e reintrodução de mexilhões pelo USFWS e apoiar programas de recuperação de peixes hospedeiros.

fr.description: La mulette à pochette rose est une moule d'eau douce autrefois répandue dans les grandes rivières de l'est des États-Unis, désormais réduite à une poignée de rivières où la qualité de l'eau reste suffisante pour sa survie.
fr.endangerment: La pollution industrielle et agricole de l'eau, la canalisation et l'endiguement des rivières, et le déclin des poissons hôtes nécessaires au développement larvaire ont provoqué des pertes de population catastrophiques.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Rivières de taille moyenne à grande avec de l'eau propre et bien oxygénée, des substrats stables de gravier et de sable, et des populations saines de poissons hôtes — principalement des achigans et des crapets — pour la fixation larvaire.
fr.actions: Soutenir l'application du Clean Water Act dans les bassins versants du Tennessee et du Cumberland, financer la propagation et la réintroduction de moules par l'USFWS et soutenir les programmes de rétablissement des poissons hôtes.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The capricorn diving beetle is the largest diving beetle in Europe, a powerful predatory aquatic insect of clean, large lakes with abundant aquatic vegetation, now vanished from most of its historical range.
endangerment: Eutrophication and acidification of Scandinavian and central European lakes, combined with drainage of bog pools, has eliminated the clear, vegetation-rich water habitats it depends on.
ecosystemNeeds: Large, clear, oligotrophic or mesotrophic lakes with abundant aquatic macrophytes in Scandinavia and northern central Europe, with minimal water quality degradation.
actions: Support lake water quality restoration through nutrient reduction in Scandinavian catchments, advocate for EU Water Framework Directive implementation, and fund diving beetle survey and monitoring programmes.

en.description: The capricorn diving beetle is the largest diving beetle in Europe, a powerful predatory aquatic insect of clean, large lakes with abundant aquatic vegetation, now vanished from most of its historical range.
en.endangerment: Eutrophication and acidification of Scandinavian and central European lakes, combined with drainage of bog pools, has eliminated the clear, vegetation-rich water habitats it depends on.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, clear, oligotrophic or mesotrophic lakes with abundant aquatic macrophytes in Scandinavia and northern central Europe, with minimal water quality degradation.
en.actions: Support lake water quality restoration through nutrient reduction in Scandinavian catchments, advocate for EU Water Framework Directive implementation, and fund diving beetle survey and monitoring programmes.

es.description: El escarabajo buceador Capricornio es el mayor escarabajo buceador de Europa, un poderoso insecto acuático predador de lagos grandes y limpios con abundante vegetación acuática, ahora desaparecido de la mayor parte de su área histórica.
es.endangerment: La eutrofización y acidificación de los lagos escandinavos y de Europa central, combinada con el drenaje de pozas pantanosas, ha eliminado los hábitats de agua clara y rica en vegetación de los que depende.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Lagos grandes, claros, oligotróficos o mesotróficos con abundantes macrófitas acuáticas en Escandinavia y el norte de Europa central, con mínima degradación de la calidad del agua.
es.actions: Apoyar la restauración de la calidad del agua de los lagos mediante la reducción de nutrientes en las cuencas escandinavas, abogar por la implementación de la Directiva Marco del Agua de la UE y financiar programas de encuesta y monitoreo de escarabajos buceadores.

pt.description: O besouro-mergulhador-capricórnio é o maior besouro mergulhador da Europa, um poderoso inseto aquático predador de grandes lagos limpos com abundante vegetação aquática, agora desaparecido da maior parte de sua área histórica.
pt.endangerment: A eutrofização e acidificação dos lagos escandinavos e da Europa central, combinada com a drenagem de lagoas turfosas, eliminou os habitats de água clara e rica em vegetação dos quais depende.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes lagos claros, oligotróficos ou mesotróficos com abundantes macrófitas aquáticas na Escandinávia e no norte da Europa central, com mínima degradação da qualidade da água.
pt.actions: Apoiar a restauração da qualidade da água dos lagos através da redução de nutrientes nas bacias escandinavas, defender a implementação da Diretiva-Quadro da Água da UE e financiar programas de levantamento e monitoramento de besouros mergulhadores.

fr.description: Le dytique du capricorne est le plus grand coléoptère aquatique d'Europe, un insecte aquatique prédateur puissant des grands lacs clairs avec une végétation aquatique abondante, aujourd'hui disparu de la majeure partie de son aire historique.
fr.endangerment: L'eutrophisation et l'acidification des lacs scandinaves et d'Europe centrale, combinées au drainage des mares tourbières, ont éliminé les habitats d'eau claire et riche en végétation dont il dépend.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Grands lacs clairs, oligotrophes ou mésotrophes avec des macrophytes aquatiques abondants en Scandinavie et dans le nord de l'Europe centrale, avec une dégradation minimale de la qualité de l'eau.
fr.actions: Soutenir la restauration de la qualité de l'eau des lacs par la réduction des nutriments dans les bassins versants scandinaves, plaider pour la mise en œuvre de la directive-cadre sur l'eau de l'UE et financer des programmes d'enquête et de suivi des dytiques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The southern damselfly is a small, brilliant blue damselfly restricted to chalk and limestone streams with flowing, clean, base-rich water and abundant aquatic vegetation in southern England and western Europe.
endangerment: Agricultural intensification causing nutrient runoff, stream modification, and loss of traditional water meadow management have eliminated it from most former sites in the UK.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, fast-flowing chalk streams and spring-fed flushes with short, grazed bankside vegetation, clean water, and abundant water crowfoot and other aquatic plants for egg-laying.
actions: Support Natural England's chalk stream restoration programme, fund agri-environment schemes that maintain traditional water meadow grazing, and back EU Habitats Directive conservation management for this species.

en.description: The southern damselfly is a small, brilliant blue damselfly restricted to chalk and limestone streams with flowing, clean, base-rich water and abundant aquatic vegetation in southern England and western Europe.
en.endangerment: Agricultural intensification causing nutrient runoff, stream modification, and loss of traditional water meadow management have eliminated it from most former sites in the UK.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow, fast-flowing chalk streams and spring-fed flushes with short, grazed bankside vegetation, clean water, and abundant water crowfoot and other aquatic plants for egg-laying.
en.actions: Support Natural England's chalk stream restoration programme, fund agri-environment schemes that maintain traditional water meadow grazing, and back EU Habitats Directive conservation management for this species.

es.description: La libélula damselfly del sur es una pequeña y brillante libélula azul restringida a arroyos de tiza y piedra caliza con agua limpia, fluyente y rica en bases y abundante vegetación acuática en el sur de Inglaterra y el oeste de Europa.
es.endangerment: La intensificación agrícola que causa escorrentía de nutrientes, la modificación de arroyos y la pérdida del manejo tradicional de prados acuáticos la han eliminado de la mayoría de sus antiguas ubicaciones en el Reino Unido.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arroyos de tiza poco profundos y de flujo rápido y afloramientos alimentados por manantiales con vegetación corta y pastada en las orillas, agua limpia y abundante cres de agua y otras plantas acuáticas para la puesta de huevos.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de restauración de arroyos de tiza de Natural England, financiar esquemas agroambientales que mantengan el pastoreo tradicional de prados acuáticos y respaldar la gestión de conservación de la Directiva de Hábitats de la UE para esta especie.

pt.description: A libélula-damselfly-meridional é uma pequena e brilhante libélula azul restrita a córregos de giz e calcário com água limpa, fluente e rica em bases e vegetação aquática abundante no sul da Inglaterra e oeste da Europa.
pt.endangerment: A intensificação agrícola que causa escoamento de nutrientes, a modificação de córregos e a perda do manejo tradicional de prados aquáticos a eliminou da maioria de seus antigos locais no Reino Unido.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Córregos de giz rasos e de fluxo rápido e afloramentos alimentados por nascentes com vegetação curta e pastada nas margens, água limpa e abundante ranúnculo aquático e outras plantas aquáticas para a postura de ovos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de restauração de córregos de giz da Natural England, financiar esquemas agro-ambientais que mantenham o pastejo tradicional de prados aquáticos e apoiar o manejo de conservação da Diretiva Habitats da UE para esta espécie.

fr.description: L'agrion de Mercure est une petite demoiselle d'un bleu brillant confinée aux ruisseaux crayeux et calcaires avec de l'eau claire, courante et riche en bases et une végétation aquatique abondante dans le sud de l'Angleterre et l'ouest de l'Europe.
fr.endangerment: L'intensification agricole provoquant des ruissellements de nutriments, la modification des ruisseaux et la perte de la gestion traditionnelle des prairies humides l'ont éliminée de la plupart de ses anciens sites au Royaume-Uni.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Ruisseaux crayeux peu profonds à courant rapide et sources avec une végétation courte et pâturée en bord de berge, une eau propre et des renoncules aquatiques abondantes et autres plantes aquatiques pour la ponte.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de restauration des ruisseaux crayeux de Natural England, financer des mesures agro-environnementales maintenant le pâturage traditionnel des prairies humides et soutenir la gestion de conservation de la directive Habitats de l'UE pour cette espèce.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The pied tamarin is a small primate endemic to the urban surroundings of Manaus, Brazil. It is remarkable for persisting in a fragmented landscape of city parks, gardens and forest remnants surrounded by a sprawling city of two million people.
endangerment: Urban expansion of Manaus has eliminated most forest habitat, while road networks fragment remaining patches and vehicle collisions kill tamarins attempting to cross between fragments.
ecosystemNeeds: Fragments of terra firme rainforest, secondary growth, and large urban parks around Manaus with fruiting trees for foraging and tall trees or buildings for sleeping sites.
actions: Support INPA's urban tamarin conservation programme, advocate for wildlife bridges across key roads in Manaus, and encourage residents to plant native fruiting trees in gardens.

en.description: The pied tamarin is a small primate endemic to the urban surroundings of Manaus, Brazil. It is remarkable for persisting in a fragmented landscape of city parks, gardens and forest remnants surrounded by a sprawling city of two million people.
en.endangerment: Urban expansion of Manaus has eliminated most forest habitat, while road networks fragment remaining patches and vehicle collisions kill tamarins attempting to cross between fragments.
