en.ecosystemNeeds: Fragments of terra firme rainforest, secondary growth, and large urban parks around Manaus with fruiting trees for foraging and tall trees or buildings for sleeping sites.
en.actions: Support INPA's urban tamarin conservation programme, advocate for wildlife bridges across key roads in Manaus, and encourage residents to plant native fruiting trees in gardens.

es.description: El tamarino pío es un pequeño primate endémico de los alrededores urbanos de Manaos, Brasil. Es notable por persistir en un paisaje fragmentado de parques urbanos, jardines y restos de bosque rodeados por una ciudad en expansión de dos millones de personas.
es.endangerment: La expansión urbana de Manaos ha eliminado la mayor parte del hábitat forestal, mientras que las redes viales fragmentan los parches restantes y las colisiones vehiculares matan tamarinos que intentan cruzar entre fragmentos.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Fragmentos de selva tropical de tierra firme, vegetación secundaria y grandes parques urbanos alrededor de Manaos con árboles frutales para forrajeo y árboles altos o edificios para sitios de descanso nocturno.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de conservación urbana de tamarinos del INPA, abogar por puentes para vida silvestre en carreteras clave de Manaos y alentar a los residentes a plantar árboles frutales nativos en sus jardines.

pt.description: O mico-de-duas-cores é um pequeno primata endêmico dos arredores urbanos de Manaus, Brasil. É notável por persistir em uma paisagem fragmentada de parques urbanos, jardins e remanescentes florestais cercados por uma cidade em expansão de dois milhões de pessoas.
pt.endangerment: A expansão urbana de Manaus eliminou a maior parte do habitat florestal, enquanto as redes viárias fragmentam os fragmentos restantes e as colisões veiculares matam saguis tentando cruzar entre fragmentos.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Fragmentos de floresta tropical de terra firme, crescimento secundário e grandes parques urbanos ao redor de Manaus com árvores frutíferas para forrageamento e árvores altas ou edifícios para locais de descanso.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de conservação urbana de saguis do INPA, defender pontes para vida silvestre nas principais estradas de Manaus e encorajar residentes a plantar árvores frutíferas nativas em seus jardins.

fr.description: Le tamarin bicolore est un petit primate endémique des environs urbains de Manaus, au Brésil. Il est remarquable pour sa persistance dans un paysage fragmenté de parcs urbains, jardins et reliques forestières entourés par une ville tentaculaire de deux millions d'habitants.
fr.endangerment: L'expansion urbaine de Manaus a éliminé la majeure partie de l'habitat forestier, tandis que les réseaux routiers fragmentent les parcelles restantes et les collisions de véhicules tuent des tamarins tentant de traverser entre les fragments.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Fragments de forêt tropicale humide de terre ferme, végétation secondaire et grands parcs urbains autour de Manaus avec des arbres fruitiers pour la recherche de nourriture et de grands arbres ou bâtiments pour les sites de repos nocturne.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de conservation des tamarins urbains de l'INPA, plaider pour des passages à faune sur les routes clés de Manaus et encourager les résidents à planter des arbres fruitiers indigènes dans leurs jardins.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The black-and-white ruffed lemur is one of Madagascar's largest lemurs and a key seed disperser of large-seeded rainforest trees. Its loud, raucous calls carry far through the canopy.
endangerment: Slash-and-burn agriculture, illegal logging, and cyclone disturbance have devastated eastern Madagascar's rainforests, causing a 95% reduction in ruffed lemur populations over three generations.
ecosystemNeeds: Intact eastern Madagascar lowland and montane rainforest with large figs, palms, and other fruiting trees; requires continuous canopy and nesting tree cavities for its unusual litter of up to six young.
actions: Support the Lemur Conservation Foundation's habitat restoration work in Masoala, advocate for stronger enforcement against illegal logging in national parks, and fund community-based forest management.

en.description: The black-and-white ruffed lemur is one of Madagascar's largest lemurs and a key seed disperser of large-seeded rainforest trees. Its loud, raucous calls carry far through the canopy.
en.endangerment: Slash-and-burn agriculture, illegal logging, and cyclone disturbance have devastated eastern Madagascar's rainforests, causing a 95% reduction in ruffed lemur populations over three generations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact eastern Madagascar lowland and montane rainforest with large figs, palms, and other fruiting trees; requires continuous canopy and nesting tree cavities for its unusual litter of up to six young.
en.actions: Support the Lemur Conservation Foundation's habitat restoration work in Masoala, advocate for stronger enforcement against illegal logging in national parks, and fund community-based forest management.

es.description: El lémur rufo blanco y negro es uno de los lémures más grandes de Madagascar y un dispersor clave de semillas de árboles de selva tropical de semillas grandes. Sus llamadas ruidosas y estridentes se escuchan a lo lejos a través del dosel.
es.endangerment: La agricultura de tala y quema, la tala ilegal y los disturbios por ciclones han devastado las selvas tropicales del este de Madagascar, causando una reducción del 95% en las poblaciones de lémur rufo durante tres generaciones.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Selva tropical de tierras bajas y montanas del este de Madagascar intacta con grandes higos, palmas y otros árboles frutales; requiere dosel continuo y cavidades en árboles para anidar para su inusual camada de hasta seis crías.
es.actions: Apoyar el trabajo de restauración de hábitat de la Fundación de Conservación del Lémur en Masoala, abogar por una aplicación más estricta contra la tala ilegal en los parques nacionales y financiar el manejo forestal comunitario.

pt.description: O lêmure-rufo-preto-e-branco é um dos maiores lêmures de Madagascar e um dispersor chave de sementes de árvores de floresta tropical de sementes grandes. Suas chamadas altas e estridentes ecoam ao longe pelo dossel.
pt.endangerment: A agricultura de corte e queima, o desmatamento ilegal e os distúrbios de ciclones devastaram as florestas tropicais do leste de Madagascar, causando uma redução de 95% nas populações de lêmure-rufo ao longo de três gerações.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical de baixadas e montana do leste de Madagascar intacta com grandes figueiras, palmeiras e outras árvores frutíferas; requer dossel contínuo e cavidades em árvores para nidificação para sua incomum ninhada de até seis filhotes.
pt.actions: Apoiar o trabalho de restauração de habitat da Fundação de Conservação do Lêmure em Masoala, defender a aplicação mais rigorosa contra o desmatamento ilegal nos parques nacionais e financiar o manejo florestal comunitário.

fr.description: Le lémur vari noir et blanc est l'un des plus grands lémuriens de Madagascar et un disperseur clé des graines des grands arbres de la forêt tropicale humide. Ses cris forts et rauques portent loin à travers la canopée.
fr.endangerment: L'agriculture sur brûlis, l'exploitation forestière illégale et les perturbations des cyclones ont dévasté les forêts tropicales humides de l'est de Madagascar, entraînant une réduction de 95% des populations de lémur vari sur trois générations.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale humide intacte de basse et haute altitude de l'est de Madagascar avec de grands figuiers, palmiers et autres arbres fruitiers ; nécessite un couvert continu et des cavités d'arbres pour la nidification pour ses portées inhabituelles allant jusqu'à six jeunes.
fr.actions: Soutenir les travaux de restauration de l'habitat de la Lemur Conservation Foundation à Masoala, plaider pour un renforcement des lois contre l'exploitation forestière illégale dans les parcs nationaux et financer la gestion forestière communautaire.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The pygmy three-toed sloth lives exclusively on Isla Escudo de Veraguas, a tiny island off Panama's Caribbean coast. It is the world's smallest sloth, 40% smaller than its mainland relatives, and found nowhere else on Earth.
endangerment: The entire world population, estimated at fewer than 100 individuals, is confined to a single 4.3 km² island, making it exceptionally vulnerable to any disturbance including mangrove degradation and human visitation.
ecosystemNeeds: Red mangrove forest on Isla Escudo de Veraguas, where it feeds almost exclusively on mangrove leaves and hangs from mangrove branches in the island's shallow coastal lagoons.
actions: Support the Smithsonian Tropical Research Institute's Escudo monitoring programme, advocate for restricted-access protection of Isla Escudo, and fund population census surveys.

en.description: The pygmy three-toed sloth lives exclusively on Isla Escudo de Veraguas, a tiny island off Panama's Caribbean coast. It is the world's smallest sloth, 40% smaller than its mainland relatives, and found nowhere else on Earth.
en.endangerment: The entire world population, estimated at fewer than 100 individuals, is confined to a single 4.3 km² island, making it exceptionally vulnerable to any disturbance including mangrove degradation and human visitation.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Red mangrove forest on Isla Escudo de Veraguas, where it feeds almost exclusively on mangrove leaves and hangs from mangrove branches in the island's shallow coastal lagoons.
en.actions: Support the Smithsonian Tropical Research Institute's Escudo monitoring programme, advocate for restricted-access protection of Isla Escudo, and fund population census surveys.

es.description: El perezoso pigmeo de tres dedos vive exclusivamente en la Isla Escudo de Veraguas, una diminuta isla frente a la costa caribeña de Panamá. Es el perezoso más pequeño del mundo, un 40% más pequeño que sus parientes del continente, y no se encuentra en ningún otro lugar de la Tierra.
es.endangerment: Toda la población mundial, estimada en menos de 100 individuos, está confinada a una isla de 4,3 km², lo que la hace excepcionalmente vulnerable a cualquier perturbación incluyendo la degradación del manglar y las visitas humanas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque de mangle rojo en la Isla Escudo de Veraguas, donde se alimenta casi exclusivamente de hojas de mangle y cuelga de las ramas del mangle en las lagunas costeras poco profundas de la isla.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de monitoreo del Instituto de Investigaciones Tropicales del Smithsonian en Escudo, abogar por la protección de acceso restringido de la Isla Escudo y financiar censos de población.

pt.description: A preguiça-pigmeia-de-três-dedos vive exclusivamente na Ilha Escudo de Veraguas, uma minúscula ilha ao largo da costa caribenha do Panamá. É a menor preguiça do mundo, 40% menor que seus parentes do continente, e não é encontrada em nenhum outro lugar da Terra.
pt.endangerment: Toda a população mundial, estimada em menos de 100 indivíduos, está confinada a uma ilha de 4,3 km², tornando-a excepcionalmente vulnerável a qualquer distúrbio, incluindo a degradação do manguezal e as visitas humanas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta de mangue vermelho na Ilha Escudo de Veraguas, onde se alimenta quase exclusivamente de folhas de mangue e pende dos galhos de mangue nas lagoas costeiras rasas da ilha.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de monitoramento do Instituto de Pesquisas Tropicais Smithsonian em Escudo, defender a proteção de acesso restrito da Ilha Escudo e financiar censos populacionais.

fr.description: Le paresseux pygmée à trois doigts vit exclusivement sur l'île Escudo de Veraguas, une minuscule île au large de la côte caraïbe du Panama. C'est le plus petit paresseux du monde, 40% plus petit que ses parents continentaux, et introuvable ailleurs sur Terre.
fr.endangerment: L'ensemble de la population mondiale, estimée à moins de 100 individus, est confinée sur une île de 4,3 km², la rendant exceptionnellement vulnérable à toute perturbation, y compris la dégradation des mangroves et les visites humaines.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt de mangroves rouges sur l'île Escudo de Veraguas, où il se nourrit presque exclusivement de feuilles de mangroves et se suspend aux branches de palétuviers dans les lagunes côtières peu profondes de l'île.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de surveillance de l'Institut de recherche tropicale Smithsonian à Escudo, plaider pour une protection à accès restreint de l'île Escudo et financer des recensements de population.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The sei whale is the third largest baleen whale, capable of reaching 20 metres and speeds of 50 km/h. Once one of the most abundant great whales, it was devastated by 20th-century industrial whaling.
endangerment: Over 200,000 sei whales were killed during the whaling era of the 1960s–70s alone. Recovery is hampered by entanglement in fishing gear, ship strikes, and climate-driven changes to prey availability.
ecosystemNeeds: Cold to temperate open ocean waters in both hemispheres, following dense aggregations of small crustaceans, copepods, and small fish; migrates between high-latitude feeding grounds and warmer wintering areas.
actions: Support IWC's commercial whaling moratorium, advocate for whale-safe shipping lanes in key feeding areas, and fund satellite tagging research to understand migration and entanglement risk.

en.description: The sei whale is the third largest baleen whale, capable of reaching 20 metres and speeds of 50 km/h. Once one of the most abundant great whales, it was devastated by 20th-century industrial whaling.
en.endangerment: Over 200,000 sei whales were killed during the whaling era of the 1960s–70s alone. Recovery is hampered by entanglement in fishing gear, ship strikes, and climate-driven changes to prey availability.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Cold to temperate open ocean waters in both hemispheres, following dense aggregations of small crustaceans, copepods, and small fish; migrates between high-latitude feeding grounds and warmer wintering areas.
en.actions: Support IWC's commercial whaling moratorium, advocate for whale-safe shipping lanes in key feeding areas, and fund satellite tagging research to understand migration and entanglement risk.

es.description: La ballena sei es la tercera ballena con barbas más grande, capaz de alcanzar 20 metros y velocidades de 50 km/h. Antes una de las ballenas más abundantes, fue devastada por la caza industrial de ballenas del siglo XX.
es.endangerment: Más de 200.000 ballenas sei fueron cazadas solo durante la era ballenera de los años 60-70. La recuperación se ve obstaculizada por el enredo en artes de pesca, las colisiones con barcos y los cambios climáticos en la disponibilidad de presas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas oceánicas abiertas frías a templadas en ambos hemisferios, siguiendo densas agregaciones de pequeños crustáceos, copépodos y peces pequeños; migra entre zonas de alimentación de alta latitud y áreas de invernada más cálidas.
es.actions: Apoyar la moratoria de caza comercial de ballenas de la CBI, abogar por rutas marítimas seguras para ballenas en áreas clave de alimentación y financiar investigaciones de marcado por satélite para comprender la migración y el riesgo de enredo.

pt.description: A baleia-sei é a terceira maior baleia de barbatanas, capaz de atingir 20 metros e velocidades de 50 km/h. Antes uma das grandes baleias mais abundantes, foi devastada pela caça industrial de baleias do século XX.
pt.endangerment: Mais de 200.000 baleias-sei foram caçadas apenas durante a era baleeira dos anos 1960-70. A recuperação é dificultada pelo emaranhamento em equipamentos de pesca, colisões com navios e mudanças climáticas na disponibilidade de presas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas oceânicas abertas frias a temperadas em ambos os hemisférios, seguindo densas agregações de pequenos crustáceos, copépodos e peixes pequenos; migra entre áreas de alimentação de alta latitude e áreas de invernada mais quentes.
pt.actions: Apoiar a moratória de caça comercial de baleias da CBI, defender rotas marítimas seguras para baleias nas principais áreas de alimentação e financiar pesquisas de marcação por satélite para entender a migração e o risco de emaranhamento.

fr.description: Le rorqual de Sei est la troisième plus grande baleine à fanons, pouvant atteindre 20 mètres et des vitesses de 50 km/h. Autrefois l'une des grandes baleines les plus abondantes, il a été dévasté par la chasse industrielle à la baleine du XXe siècle.
fr.endangerment: Plus de 200 000 rorquals de Sei ont été tués rien que pendant l'ère baleinière des années 1960-70. Le rétablissement est entravé par l'enchevêtrement dans les engins de pêche, les collisions de navires et les changements climatiques affectant la disponibilité des proies.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Eaux océaniques ouvertes froides à tempérées dans les deux hémisphères, suivant des agrégations denses de petits crustacés, copépodes et petits poissons ; migre entre des zones d'alimentation à haute latitude et des zones d'hivernage plus chaudes.
fr.actions: Soutenir le moratoire sur la chasse commerciale à la baleine de la CBI, plaider pour des couloirs maritimes adaptés aux baleines dans les zones d'alimentation clés et financer des recherches de marquage par satellite pour comprendre la migration et le risque d'enchevêtrement.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Stresemann's bristlefront is a secretive, ground-dwelling bird of the Atlantic Forest in Bahia, Brazil. Known from fewer than ten confirmed sightings over 60 years, it may be the world's most elusive bird.
endangerment: The Atlantic Forest of Bahia has been reduced to less than 11% of its original extent, and the remaining patches continue to be degraded by charcoal production and cattle ranching.
ecosystemNeeds: Dense undergrowth in intact mature Atlantic Forest in southern Bahia at elevations of 600–900 metres, with leaf litter foraging habitat and undisturbed forest interior away from clearings.
actions: Support American Bird Conservancy's Mata do Passarinho Reserve in Bahia, advocate for expansion of protected areas in the Bahia Interior Atlantic Forest hotspot, and fund systematic survey efforts.

en.description: Stresemann's bristlefront is a secretive, ground-dwelling bird of the Atlantic Forest in Bahia, Brazil. Known from fewer than ten confirmed sightings over 60 years, it may be the world's most elusive bird.
en.endangerment: The Atlantic Forest of Bahia has been reduced to less than 11% of its original extent, and the remaining patches continue to be degraded by charcoal production and cattle ranching.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense undergrowth in intact mature Atlantic Forest in southern Bahia at elevations of 600–900 metres, with leaf litter foraging habitat and undisturbed forest interior away from clearings.
en.actions: Support American Bird Conservancy's Mata do Passarinho Reserve in Bahia, advocate for expansion of protected areas in the Bahia Interior Atlantic Forest hotspot, and fund systematic survey efforts.

es.description: El bristlefront de Stresemann es un ave terrestre sigilosa del Bosque Atlántico en Bahía, Brasil. Conocida por menos de diez avistamientos confirmados en 60 años, puede ser el ave más esquiva del mundo.
es.endangerment: El Bosque Atlántico de Bahía ha sido reducido a menos del 11% de su extensión original, y los parches restantes continúan siendo degradados por la producción de carbón vegetal y la ganadería.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Densa vegetación baja en el interior del Bosque Atlántico maduro e intacto del sur de Bahía a altitudes de 600-900 metros, con hábitat de forrajeo de hojarasca e interior de bosque sin perturbaciones lejos de los claros.
es.actions: Apoyar la Reserva Mata do Passarinho de la American Bird Conservancy en Bahía, abogar por la expansión de las áreas protegidas en el hotspot del Bosque Atlántico Interior de Bahía y financiar esfuerzos sistemáticos de encuesta.

pt.description: O grimpeirinho-de-stresemann é uma ave terrestre discreta da Mata Atlântica na Bahia, Brasil. Conhecida por menos de dez avistamentos confirmados em 60 anos, pode ser o pássaro mais elusivo do mundo.
pt.endangerment: A Mata Atlântica da Bahia foi reduzida a menos de 11% de sua extensão original, e os fragmentos restantes continuam sendo degradados pela produção de carvão vegetal e pela pecuária.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Sub-bosque denso em Mata Atlântica madura e intacta do sul da Bahia em altitudes de 600-900 metros, com habitat de forrageamento de serapilheira e interior de floresta sem perturbações longe das clareiras.
pt.actions: Apoiar a Reserva Mata do Passarinho da American Bird Conservancy na Bahia, defender a expansão das áreas protegidas no hotspot da Mata Atlântica Interior da Bahia e financiar esforços sistemáticos de levantamento.

fr.description: Le grimpeur à soies de Stresemann est un oiseau terrestre discret de la Forêt Atlantique dans l'État de Bahia, au Brésil. Connu de moins de dix observations confirmées en 60 ans, il pourrait être l'oiseau le plus insaisissable du monde.
fr.endangerment: La Forêt Atlantique de Bahia a été réduite à moins de 11% de son étendue originelle, et les parcelles restantes continuent d'être dégradées par la production de charbon de bois et l'élevage bovin.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Sous-bois dense dans l'intérieur de la forêt atlantique mature et intacte du sud de Bahia à des altitudes de 600-900 mètres, avec un habitat de recherche de nourriture dans la litière de feuilles et un intérieur forestier non perturbé loin des clairières.
fr.actions: Soutenir la réserve Mata do Passarinho de l'American Bird Conservancy à Bahia, plaider pour l'expansion des zones protégées dans le hotspot de la Forêt Atlantique intérieure de Bahia et financer des efforts d'inventaire systématiques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Raso lark is the world's rarest lark, endemic to the tiny uninhabited islet of Raso in the Cape Verde archipelago. The total population fluctuates between about 40 and 1,500 birds depending on rainfall.
endangerment: Extreme vulnerability to drought on a 7 km² island with no fresh water means the entire species can be devastated by a single bad dry season; climate change is intensifying drought frequency and severity.
ecosystemNeeds: The bare, arid plain of Raso islet with sparse desert vegetation, rocky substrate for nesting, and invertebrates that proliferate only when rainfall occurs; completely dependent on one tiny island.
actions: Support BirdLife International's Cape Verde programme, fund annual population monitoring on Raso, and advocate for rat-free status to prevent the accidental introduction of mammalian predators.

en.description: The Raso lark is the world's rarest lark, endemic to the tiny uninhabited islet of Raso in the Cape Verde archipelago. The total population fluctuates between about 40 and 1,500 birds depending on rainfall.
en.endangerment: Extreme vulnerability to drought on a 7 km² island with no fresh water means the entire species can be devastated by a single bad dry season; climate change is intensifying drought frequency and severity.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The bare, arid plain of Raso islet with sparse desert vegetation, rocky substrate for nesting, and invertebrates that proliferate only when rainfall occurs; completely dependent on one tiny island.
en.actions: Support BirdLife International's Cape Verde programme, fund annual population monitoring on Raso, and advocate for rat-free status to prevent the accidental introduction of mammalian predators.

es.description: La alondra de Raso es la alondra más rara del mundo, endémica del diminuto islote deshabitado de Raso en el archipiélago de Cabo Verde. La población total fluctúa entre unos 40 y 1.500 aves según las precipitaciones.
es.endangerment: La extrema vulnerabilidad a la sequía en una isla de 7 km² sin agua dulce significa que toda la especie puede ser devastada por una sola mala temporada seca; el cambio climático está intensificando la frecuencia y severidad de las sequías.
es.ecosystemNeeds: La llanura árida y desnuda del islote Raso con escasa vegetación desértica, sustrato rocoso para anidar e invertebrados que proliferan solo cuando caen precipitaciones; completamente dependiente de una diminuta isla.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de BirdLife International en Cabo Verde, financiar el monitoreo anual de población en Raso y abogar por el estatus libre de ratas para prevenir la introducción accidental de depredadores mamíferos.

pt.description: A cotovia-de-raso é a cotovia mais rara do mundo, endêmica da minúscula ilha desabitada de Raso no arquipélago de Cabo Verde. A população total flutua entre cerca de 40 e 1.500 aves dependendo das precipitações.
pt.endangerment: A extrema vulnerabilidade à seca em uma ilha de 7 km² sem água doce significa que toda a espécie pode ser devastada por uma única estação seca ruim; as mudanças climáticas estão intensificando a frequência e gravidade das secas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: A planície árida e nua do ilhote Raso com vegetação desértica esparsa, substrato rochoso para nidificação e invertebrados que proliferam apenas quando ocorrem precipitações; completamente dependente de uma minúscula ilha.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa da BirdLife International em Cabo Verde, financiar o monitoramento anual da população em Raso e defender o status livre de ratos para prevenir a introdução acidental de predadores mamíferos.

fr.description: L'alouette de Raso est l'alouette la plus rare du monde, endémique du minuscule îlot inhabité de Raso dans l'archipel du Cap-Vert. La population totale fluctue entre environ 40 et 1 500 oiseaux selon les précipitations.
fr.endangerment: La vulnérabilité extrême à la sécheresse sur une île de 7 km² sans eau douce signifie que toute l'espèce peut être dévastée par une seule mauvaise saison sèche ; le changement climatique intensifie la fréquence et la gravité des sécheresses.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: La plaine aride et dénudée de l'îlot Raso avec une végétation désertique clairsemée, un substrat rocheux pour la nidification et des invertébrés qui prolifèrent uniquement lors des précipitations ; entièrement dépendante d'une minuscule île.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de BirdLife International au Cap-Vert, financer la surveillance annuelle de la population à Raso et plaider pour un statut sans rats afin de prévenir l'introduction accidentelle de prédateurs mammifères.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Archey's frog is one of the most primitive living frogs, unchanged for 70 million years. It is tiny — less than 4 cm — lacks a tympanum, and carries eggs on its back rather than spawning in water. Found only on New Zealand's Coromandel Peninsula.
endangerment: The global population has crashed by 88% since the 1990s due to chytridiomycosis — a fungal skin disease devastating amphibians worldwide — combined with predation by introduced rats and stoats.
ecosystemNeeds: Cool, moist podocarp-broadleaf forest and shrubland on the Coromandel Peninsula in damp rocky areas under logs, mossy banks, and leaf litter within a narrow altitudinal range of 400–950 metres.
actions: Support the New Zealand Department of Conservation's biosecurity programme, fund chytrid research and immune-boosting trials, and advocate for predator-free buffer zones around remaining populations.

en.description: Archey's frog is one of the most primitive living frogs, unchanged for 70 million years. It is tiny — less than 4 cm — lacks a tympanum, and carries eggs on its back rather than spawning in water. Found only on New Zealand's Coromandel Peninsula.
en.endangerment: The global population has crashed by 88% since the 1990s due to chytridiomycosis — a fungal skin disease devastating amphibians worldwide — combined with predation by introduced rats and stoats.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Cool, moist podocarp-broadleaf forest and shrubland on the Coromandel Peninsula in damp rocky areas under logs, mossy banks, and leaf litter within a narrow altitudinal range of 400–950 metres.
en.actions: Support the New Zealand Department of Conservation's biosecurity programme, fund chytrid research and immune-boosting trials, and advocate for predator-free buffer zones around remaining populations.

es.description: La rana de Archey es una de las ranas vivas más primitivas, sin cambios durante 70 millones de años. Es diminuta —menos de 4 cm— carece de tímpano y lleva los huevos en su espalda en lugar de desovar en el agua. Solo se encuentra en la Península Coromandel de Nueva Zelanda.
es.endangerment: La población mundial ha colapsado un 88% desde la década de 1990 debido a la quitridiomicosis —una enfermedad fúngica cutánea devastadora para anfibios en todo el mundo— combinada con la depredación de ratas y armiños introducidos.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque podocarpáceo-latifoliado fresco y húmedo y matorral en la Península Coromandel en áreas húmedas y rocosas bajo troncos, orillas musgosas y hojarasca dentro de un estrecho rango altitudinal de 400-950 metros.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de bioseguridad del Departamento de Conservación de Nueva Zelanda, financiar investigaciones sobre el quitridio y ensayos de refuerzo inmunológico y abogar por zonas de amortiguación libres de depredadores alrededor de las poblaciones restantes.

pt.description: A rã-de-archey é uma das rãs vivas mais primitivas, inalterada por 70 milhões de anos. É minúscula — menos de 4 cm — carece de tímpano e carrega os ovos nas costas em vez de desovar na água. Encontrada apenas na Península de Coromandel, Nova Zelândia.
pt.endangerment: A população mundial colapsou 88% desde os anos 1990 devido à quitridiomicose — uma doença fúngica cutânea devastadora para anfíbios em todo o mundo — combinada com a predação de ratos e arminhos introduzidos.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta de podocarpo-folha-larga fresca e úmida e arbustos na Península de Coromandel em áreas úmidas e rochosas sob troncos, margens musgosas e serapilheira dentro de uma estreita faixa altitudinal de 400-950 metros.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de biossegurança do Departamento de Conservação da Nova Zelândia, financiar pesquisas sobre quitrídio e ensaios de reforço imunológico e defender zonas de amortecimento livres de predadores ao redor das populações restantes.

fr.description: La grenouille d'Archey est l'une des grenouilles vivantes les plus primitives, inchangée depuis 70 millions d'années. Elle est minuscule — moins de 4 cm — dépourvue de tympan et porte ses œufs sur le dos plutôt que de pondre dans l'eau. Trouvée uniquement sur la péninsule de Coromandel, en Nouvelle-Zélande.
fr.endangerment: La population mondiale a chuté de 88% depuis les années 1990 en raison de la chytridiomycose — une maladie fongique cutanée dévastatrice pour les amphibiens dans le monde entier — combinée à la prédation par les rats et les hermines introduits.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt fraîche et humide de podocarpacées-feuillus et garrigue sur la péninsule de Coromandel dans des zones humides et rocheuses sous des troncs, des berges moussues et de la litière de feuilles dans une étroite plage altitudinale de 400-950 mètres.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de biosécurité du Département de conservation de Nouvelle-Zélande, financer la recherche sur le chytride et les essais de stimulation immunitaire et plaider pour des zones tampons sans prédateurs autour des populations restantes.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Lehmann's poison frog is one of the most beautiful and toxic frogs in the world, with bold red and black markings warning predators of its potent skin alkaloids. Endemic to a tiny area of Pacific slope forest in Cauca, Colombia.
endangerment: Its extremely small range in the Anchicayá and Naya river valleys is under intense pressure from deforestation for coca cultivation and timber, while collection for the international pet trade has depleted wild populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Humid Pacific slope rainforest in Cauca and Valle del Cauca, Colombia, at 100–300 metres altitude, with bromeliads for egg deposition and humid leaf litter for tadpole development.
actions: Support ProAves's Pacific forest protection programme in Cauca, advocate for CITES enforcement against illegal collection, and fund in-situ monitoring and breeding site protection.

en.description: Lehmann's poison frog is one of the most beautiful and toxic frogs in the world, with bold red and black markings warning predators of its potent skin alkaloids. Endemic to a tiny area of Pacific slope forest in Cauca, Colombia.
en.endangerment: Its extremely small range in the Anchicayá and Naya river valleys is under intense pressure from deforestation for coca cultivation and timber, while collection for the international pet trade has depleted wild populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Humid Pacific slope rainforest in Cauca and Valle del Cauca, Colombia, at 100–300 metres altitude, with bromeliads for egg deposition and humid leaf litter for tadpole development.
en.actions: Support ProAves's Pacific forest protection programme in Cauca, advocate for CITES enforcement against illegal collection, and fund in-situ monitoring and breeding site protection.

es.description: La rana venenosa de Lehmann es una de las ranas más hermosas y tóxicas del mundo, con llamativas marcas rojas y negras que advierten a los depredadores de sus potentes alcaloides cutáneos. Endémica de una pequeña área de bosque en la ladera del Pacífico en Cauca, Colombia.
es.endangerment: Su área extremadamente pequeña en los valles de los ríos Anchicayá y Naya está bajo intensa presión de deforestación para el cultivo de coca y la madera, mientras que la recolección para el comercio internacional de mascotas ha agotado las poblaciones silvestres.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Selva tropical húmeda en la ladera del Pacífico en Cauca y Valle del Cauca, Colombia, a 100-300 metros de altitud, con bromelias para la deposición de huevos y hojarasca húmeda para el desarrollo de los renacuajos.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de protección del bosque del Pacífico de ProAves en Cauca, abogar por la aplicación de la CITES contra la recolección ilegal y financiar el monitoreo in situ y la protección de sitios de reproducción.

pt.description: A rã-venenosa-de-lehmann é uma das rãs mais belas e tóxicas do mundo, com marcas vermelhas e pretas chamativas que advertem os predadores sobre seus potentes alcaloides cutâneos. Endêmica de uma pequena área de floresta na encosta do Pacífico em Cauca, Colômbia.
pt.endangerment: Sua área extremamente pequena nos vales dos rios Anchicayá e Naya está sob intensa pressão do desmatamento para o cultivo de coca e extração de madeira, enquanto a coleta para o comércio internacional de animais de estimação esgotou as populações selvagens.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical úmida na encosta do Pacífico em Cauca e Valle del Cauca, Colômbia, a 100-300 metros de altitude, com bromélias para deposição de ovos e serapilheira úmida para o desenvolvimento dos girinos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de proteção florestal do Pacífico da ProAves em Cauca, defender a aplicação da CITES contra a coleta ilegal e financiar o monitoramento in situ e a proteção dos locais de reprodução.

fr.description: La grenouille venimeuse de Lehmann est l'une des grenouilles les plus belles et les plus toxiques du monde, avec des marques rouges et noires voyantes avertissant les prédateurs de ses puissants alcaloïdes cutanés. Endémique d'une petite zone de forêt sur le versant Pacifique au Cauca, en Colombie.
fr.endangerment: Son aire extrêmement réduite dans les vallées des rivières Anchicayá et Naya est sous une intense pression de déforestation pour la culture de coca et l'exploitation forestière, tandis que la collecte pour le commerce international d'animaux de compagnie a épuisé les populations sauvages.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale humide du versant Pacifique dans le Cauca et le Valle del Cauca, en Colombie, à 100-300 mètres d'altitude, avec des broméliacées pour la dépôt des œufs et de la litière humide pour le développement des têtards.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de protection des forêts du Pacifique de ProAves au Cauca, plaider pour l'application de la CITES contre la collecte illégale et financer le suivi in situ et la protection des sites de reproduction.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Table Mountain ghost frog exists nowhere else on Earth but the ravines of Table Mountain above Cape Town. Its entire global range covers just a few square kilometres on the southwestern slopes of this iconic mountain.
endangerment: Invasive pine plantations consume water from the mountain's streams, while winter tourist pressure disturbs breeding sites; climate change is reducing rainfall and altering the cold, fast-flowing streams the species requires.
ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, cold, rocky mountain streams in fynbos habitat on the Skeleton Gorge and Kasteelspoort ravines of Table Mountain, above 600 metres elevation, for tadpole development.
actions: Support Table Mountain Fund's invasive pine removal programme, advocate for strict wet-season stream buffers on Table Mountain, and fund population monitoring and climate vulnerability assessments.

en.description: The Table Mountain ghost frog exists nowhere else on Earth but the ravines of Table Mountain above Cape Town. Its entire global range covers just a few square kilometres on the southwestern slopes of this iconic mountain.
en.endangerment: Invasive pine plantations consume water from the mountain's streams, while winter tourist pressure disturbs breeding sites; climate change is reducing rainfall and altering the cold, fast-flowing streams the species requires.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, cold, rocky mountain streams in fynbos habitat on the Skeleton Gorge and Kasteelspoort ravines of Table Mountain, above 600 metres elevation, for tadpole development.
en.actions: Support Table Mountain Fund's invasive pine removal programme, advocate for strict wet-season stream buffers on Table Mountain, and fund population monitoring and climate vulnerability assessments.

es.description: La rana fantasma de la Montaña de la Mesa no existe en ningún otro lugar de la Tierra más que en las quebradas de la Montaña de la Mesa sobre Ciudad del Cabo. Su área global completa cubre solo unos pocos kilómetros cuadrados en las laderas suroeste de esta icónica montaña.
es.endangerment: Las plantaciones de pinos invasores consumen el agua de los arroyos de la montaña, mientras que la presión turística invernal perturba los sitios de cría; el cambio climático está reduciendo las precipitaciones y alterando los arroyos fríos y de rápido flujo que requiere la especie.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arroyos de montaña fríos, rocosos y de rápido flujo en hábitat de fynbos en las quebradas de Skeleton Gorge y Kasteelspoort de la Montaña de la Mesa, por encima de 600 metros de altitud, para el desarrollo de los renacuajos.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de eliminación de pinos invasores del Fondo de la Montaña de la Mesa, abogar por estrictas zonas de amortiguación de arroyos en temporada húmeda en la Montaña de la Mesa y financiar el monitoreo de población y las evaluaciones de vulnerabilidad climática.

pt.description: A rã-fantasma-da-montanha-da-mesa não existe em nenhum outro lugar da Terra além dos ravinas da Montanha da Mesa acima da Cidade do Cabo. Sua área global completa cobre apenas alguns quilômetros quadrados nas encostas sudoeste desta montanha icônica.
pt.endangerment: Plantações de pinheiros invasores consomem a água dos córregos da montanha, enquanto a pressão turística de inverno perturba os locais de reprodução; as mudanças climáticas estão reduzindo as precipitações e alterando os córregos frios e de fluxo rápido que a espécie necessita.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Córregos de montanha frios, rochosos e de fluxo rápido em habitat de fynbos nos ravinas de Skeleton Gorge e Kasteelspoort da Montanha da Mesa, acima de 600 metros de altitude, para o desenvolvimento dos girinos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de remoção de pinheiros invasores do Fundo da Montanha da Mesa, defender zonas de amortecimento rigorosas de córregos na temporada úmida na Montanha da Mesa e financiar o monitoramento populacional e as avaliações de vulnerabilidade climática.

fr.description: La grenouille fantôme de la Montagne de la Table n'existe nulle part ailleurs sur Terre que dans les ravins de la Montagne de la Table au-dessus du Cap. L'ensemble de son aire mondiale couvre à peine quelques kilomètres carrés sur les pentes sud-ouest de cette montagne emblématique.
fr.endangerment: Les plantations de pins envahissants absorbent l'eau des ruisseaux de la montagne, tandis que la pression touristique hivernale perturbe les sites de reproduction ; le changement climatique réduit les précipitations et modifie les ruisseaux froids et rapides dont l'espèce a besoin.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Ruisseaux de montagne froids, rocheux et à courant rapide dans l'habitat de fynbos dans les ravins de Skeleton Gorge et Kasteelspoort de la Montagne de la Table, au-dessus de 600 mètres d'altitude, pour le développement des têtards.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élimination des pins envahissants du Table Mountain Fund, plaider pour des zones tampons strictes des ruisseaux en saison humide sur la Montagne de la Table et financer le suivi des populations et les évaluations de vulnérabilité climatique.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Jamaican iguana is Jamaica's largest native land animal, a large, grey-green lizard once found island-wide. Thought extinct for 40 years, it was rediscovered in the Hellshire Hills in 1990, where a few hundred still survive.
endangerment: Invasive mongooses, rats, cats, and pigs eat iguana eggs and hatchlings, allowing fewer than 2% of eggs to reach adulthood; remaining dry limestone forest habitat is cleared for charcoal production.
ecosystemNeeds: Dry limestone forest in the Hellshire Hills of southwest Jamaica, with sandy soil clearings for nest sites, fruiting trees such as cactus and lignum vitae for food, and rocky outcrops for basking.
actions: Support the Jamaican iguana head-start programme at Hope Zoo, Kingston, which raises hatchlings until large enough to survive mongoose predation, and fund habitat protection in Hellshire Hills.

en.description: The Jamaican iguana is Jamaica's largest native land animal, a large, grey-green lizard once found island-wide. Thought extinct for 40 years, it was rediscovered in the Hellshire Hills in 1990, where a few hundred still survive.
en.endangerment: Invasive mongooses, rats, cats, and pigs eat iguana eggs and hatchlings, allowing fewer than 2% of eggs to reach adulthood; remaining dry limestone forest habitat is cleared for charcoal production.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dry limestone forest in the Hellshire Hills of southwest Jamaica, with sandy soil clearings for nest sites, fruiting trees such as cactus and lignum vitae for food, and rocky outcrops for basking.
en.actions: Support the Jamaican iguana head-start programme at Hope Zoo, Kingston, which raises hatchlings until large enough to survive mongoose predation, and fund habitat protection in Hellshire Hills.

es.description: El iguana jamaicano es el mayor animal terrestre nativo de Jamaica, un gran lagarto gris-verdoso que alguna vez se encontró en toda la isla. Considerado extinto durante 40 años, fue redescubierto en las Colinas Hellshire en 1990, donde aún sobreviven unos pocos cientos.
es.endangerment: Los mangostas, ratas, gatos y cerdos invasores se comen los huevos y crías del iguana, permitiendo que menos del 2% de los huevos lleguen a la adultez; el hábitat restante de bosque seco de piedra caliza se desmonta para la producción de carbón vegetal.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque seco de piedra caliza en las Colinas Hellshire del suroeste de Jamaica, con claros de suelo arenoso para sitios de anidamiento, árboles frutales como cactus y guayacán para alimentación y afloramientos rocosos para tomar el sol.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría inicial del iguana jamaicano en el Zoo Hope, Kingston, que cría crías hasta que son lo suficientemente grandes para sobrevivir a la depredación de las mangostas, y financiar la protección del hábitat en las Colinas Hellshire.

pt.description: O iguana-jamaicano é o maior animal terrestre nativo da Jamaica, um grande lagarto cinza-esverdeado que antes era encontrado em toda a ilha. Considerado extinto por 40 anos, foi redescoberto nas Colinas Hellshire em 1990, onde ainda sobrevivem algumas centenas.
pt.endangerment: Mangusto, ratos, gatos e porcos invasores comem os ovos e filhotes do iguana, permitindo que menos de 2% dos ovos cheguem à idade adulta; o habitat restante de floresta seca de calcário é desmatado para a produção de carvão vegetal.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta seca de calcário nas Colinas Hellshire do sudoeste da Jamaica, com clareiras de solo arenoso para locais de nidificação, árvores frutíferas como cacto e pau-ferro para alimentação e afloramentos rochosos para aquecimento solar.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação inicial do iguana-jamaicano no Zoológico Hope, Kingston, que cria filhotes até ficarem grandes o suficiente para sobreviver à predação do mangusto, e financiar a proteção do habitat nas Colinas Hellshire.

fr.description: L'iguane de la Jamaïque est le plus grand animal terrestre indigène de la Jamaïque, un grand lézard gris-vert autrefois présent sur toute l'île. Considéré comme éteint pendant 40 ans, il a été redécouvert dans les collines de Hellshire en 1990, où quelques centaines survivent encore.
fr.endangerment: Les mangoustes, rats, chats et cochons envahissants mangent les œufs et les jeunes iguanes, permettant à moins de 2% des œufs d'atteindre l'âge adulte ; l'habitat restant de forêt calcaire sèche est défriché pour la production de charbon de bois.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt calcaire sèche dans les collines de Hellshire du sud-ouest de la Jamaïque, avec des clairières de sol sableux pour les sites de nidification, des arbres fruitiers comme le cactus et le gaïac pour la nourriture et des affleurements rocheux pour le bain de soleil.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage précoce de l'iguane jamaïcain au zoo Hope de Kingston, qui élève les jeunes jusqu'à ce qu'ils soient assez grands pour survivre à la prédation des mangoustes, et financer la protection de l'habitat dans les collines de Hellshire.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The geometric tortoise is the rarest tortoise in southern Africa, named for the striking geometric star pattern on its shell. It is confined to the Swartland lowlands of the Western Cape, South Africa, in a landscape almost entirely converted to agriculture.
endangerment: More than 96% of its lowland fynbos habitat has been converted to wheat fields and vineyards; the remaining fragments are too small and isolated to sustain viable populations, and urban expansion continues.
ecosystemNeeds: Lowland renosterveld and strandveld fynbos in the Swartland and Agulhas Plain, Western Cape, with seasonally moist clay soils, diverse low shrubs for shelter and food, and open sandy patches for nesting.
actions: Support the Tortoise Conservation Group's fynbos restoration in the Swartland, advocate for biodiversity stewardship agreements with Western Cape farmers, and fund population surveys in remaining fragments.

en.description: The geometric tortoise is the rarest tortoise in southern Africa, named for the striking geometric star pattern on its shell. It is confined to the Swartland lowlands of the Western Cape, South Africa, in a landscape almost entirely converted to agriculture.
en.endangerment: More than 96% of its lowland fynbos habitat has been converted to wheat fields and vineyards; the remaining fragments are too small and isolated to sustain viable populations, and urban expansion continues.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Lowland renosterveld and strandveld fynbos in the Swartland and Agulhas Plain, Western Cape, with seasonally moist clay soils, diverse low shrubs for shelter and food, and open sandy patches for nesting.
en.actions: Support the Tortoise Conservation Group's fynbos restoration in the Swartland, advocate for biodiversity stewardship agreements with Western Cape farmers, and fund population surveys in remaining fragments.

es.description: La tortuga geométrica es la tortuga más rara del sur de África, llamada así por el llamativo patrón geométrico de estrella en su caparazón. Está confinada a las tierras bajas de Swartland del Cabo Occidental, Sudáfrica, en un paisaje casi completamente convertido en agricultura.
es.endangerment: Más del 96% de su hábitat de fynbos de tierras bajas ha sido convertido en campos de trigo y viñedos; los fragmentos restantes son demasiado pequeños y aislados para sostener poblaciones viables, y la expansión urbana continúa.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Renosterveld y strandveld fynbos de tierras bajas en el Swartland y la Llanura de Agulhas, Cabo Occidental, con suelos arcillosos estacionalmente húmedos, arbustos bajos diversos para refugio y alimento, y parches arenosos abiertos para anidar.
es.actions: Apoyar la restauración de fynbos del Grupo de Conservación de Tortugas en el Swartland, abogar por acuerdos de administración de biodiversidad con agricultores del Cabo Occidental y financiar encuestas de población en los fragmentos restantes.

pt.description: A tartaruga-geométrica é a tartaruga mais rara do sul da África, chamada assim pelo impressionante padrão geométrico de estrela em seu casco. Está confinada às planícies do Swartland do Cabo Ocidental, África do Sul, em uma paisagem quase completamente convertida em agricultura.
pt.endangerment: Mais de 96% de seu habitat de fynbos de planícies foi convertido em campos de trigo e vinhedos; os fragmentos restantes são pequenos demais e isolados para sustentar populações viáveis, e a expansão urbana continua.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Fynbos de renosterveld e strandveld de planícies no Swartland e na Planície de Agulhas, Cabo Ocidental, com solos argilosos sazonalmente úmidos, arbustos baixos diversos para abrigo e alimento, e manchas arenosas abertas para nidificação.
pt.actions: Apoiar a restauração de fynbos do Grupo de Conservação de Tartarugas no Swartland, defender acordos de administração de biodiversidade com agricultores do Cabo Ocidental e financiar levantamentos populacionais nos fragmentos restantes.

fr.description: La tortue géométrique est la tortue la plus rare d'Afrique australe, nommée pour le motif géométrique en étoile frappant sur sa carapace. Elle est confinée aux basses terres du Swartland du Cap-Occidental, en Afrique du Sud, dans un paysage presque entièrement converti en terres agricoles.
fr.endangerment: Plus de 96% de son habitat de fynbos de plaine a été converti en champs de blé et vignobles ; les fragments restants sont trop petits et isolés pour soutenir des populations viables, et l'expansion urbaine continue.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Fynbos de renosterveld et de strandveld de plaine dans le Swartland et la plaine d'Agulhas, Cap-Occidental, avec des sols argileux saisonnièrement humides, des arbustes bas variés pour l'abri et la nourriture, et des zones sablonneuses ouvertes pour la nidification.
fr.actions: Soutenir la restauration du fynbos par le Tortoise Conservation Group dans le Swartland, plaider pour des accords de gestion de la biodiversité avec les agriculteurs du Cap-Occidental et financer des relevés de population dans les fragments restants.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Alabama cavefish is the rarest cave fish in North America, known only from a single cave pool in Key Cave, Alabama. This completely blind, depigmented fish has never been seen in any other location.
endangerment: The species depends entirely on bat guano from cave-roosting grey bats as its base food source; declines in bat populations from white-nose syndrome are reducing the food supply, while agricultural pesticide runoff pollutes the cave pool.
ecosystemNeeds: A single cave pool in Key Cave National Wildlife Refuge, Alabama, fed by subterranean water and sustained by nutrients from grey bat guano; requires absolute water quality, stable hydrology, and bat colony health.
actions: Support USFWS Key Cave NWR management, fund white-nose syndrome research for cave-roosting grey bats, and advocate for strict agricultural buffer zones around the cave drainage watershed.

en.description: The Alabama cavefish is the rarest cave fish in North America, known only from a single cave pool in Key Cave, Alabama. This completely blind, depigmented fish has never been seen in any other location.
en.endangerment: The species depends entirely on bat guano from cave-roosting grey bats as its base food source; declines in bat populations from white-nose syndrome are reducing the food supply, while agricultural pesticide runoff pollutes the cave pool.
en.ecosystemNeeds: A single cave pool in Key Cave National Wildlife Refuge, Alabama, fed by subterranean water and sustained by nutrients from grey bat guano; requires absolute water quality, stable hydrology, and bat colony health.
en.actions: Support USFWS Key Cave NWR management, fund white-nose syndrome research for cave-roosting grey bats, and advocate for strict agricultural buffer zones around the cave drainage watershed.

es.description: El pez cavernícola de Alabama es el pez cavernícola más raro de América del Norte, conocido solo en una única charca subterránea en Key Cave, Alabama. Este pez completamente ciego y despigmentado nunca ha sido visto en ningún otro lugar.
es.endangerment: La especie depende enteramente del guano de murciélago de los murciélagos grises que viven en la cueva como su fuente de alimento base; los declives en las poblaciones de murciélagos por el síndrome de la nariz blanca están reduciendo el suministro de alimento, mientras que la escorrentía de pesticidas agrícolas contamina la charca subterránea.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Una única charca subterránea en el Refugio Nacional de Vida Silvestre de Key Cave, Alabama, alimentada por agua subterránea y sostenida por nutrientes del guano de murciélago gris; requiere calidad absoluta del agua, hidrología estable y salud de la colonia de murciélagos.
es.actions: Apoyar la gestión del USFWS Key Cave NWR, financiar investigaciones sobre el síndrome de la nariz blanca para los murciélagos grises que viven en cuevas y abogar por estrictas zonas de amortiguación agrícola alrededor de la cuenca de drenaje de la cueva.

pt.description: O peixe-cavernícola-do-alabama é o peixe cavernícola mais raro da América do Norte, conhecido apenas em uma única poça subterrânea na Caverna Key, Alabama. Este peixe completamente cego e despigmentado nunca foi visto em nenhum outro local.
pt.endangerment: A espécie depende inteiramente do guano de morcego dos morcegos cinzas que habitam a caverna como sua fonte de alimento base; os declínios nas populações de morcegos pelo síndrome do nariz branco estão reduzindo o suprimento de alimento, enquanto o escoamento de pesticidas agrícolas contamina a poça subterrânea.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Uma única poça subterrânea no Refúgio Nacional de Vida Silvestre de Key Cave, Alabama, alimentada por água subterrânea e sustentada por nutrientes do guano de morcego cinza; requer qualidade absoluta da água, hidrologia estável e saúde da colônia de morcegos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o manejo do USFWS Key Cave NWR, financiar pesquisas sobre o síndrome do nariz branco para morcegos cinzas de cavernas e defender zonas de amortecimento agrícola rigorosas ao redor da bacia de drenagem da caverna.

fr.description: Le poisson des cavernes de l'Alabama est le poisson cavernicole le plus rare d'Amérique du Nord, connu uniquement dans une seule mare souterraine dans Key Cave, Alabama. Ce poisson complètement aveugle et dépigmenté n'a jamais été observé dans aucun autre endroit.
fr.endangerment: L'espèce dépend entièrement du guano des chauves-souris grises vivant dans la grotte comme base alimentaire ; les déclins des populations de chauves-souris dus au syndrome du museau blanc réduisent l'approvisionnement alimentaire, tandis que le ruissellement de pesticides agricoles pollue la mare souterraine.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Une seule mare souterraine dans le refuge national de faune sauvage de Key Cave, Alabama, alimentée par des eaux souterraines et soutenue par les nutriments du guano des chauves-souris grises ; nécessite une qualité d'eau absolue, une hydrologie stable et la santé de la colonie de chauves-souris.
fr.actions: Soutenir la gestion du USFWS Key Cave NWR, financer la recherche sur le syndrome du museau blanc pour les chauves-souris grises cavernicoles et plaider pour des zones tampons agricoles strictes autour du bassin de drainage de la grotte.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Mangarahara cichlid is a freshwater fish from a small river system in northeastern Madagascar. The Mangarahara River is believed to have dried up completely, and the species was declared extinct in the wild around 2013.
endangerment: The Mangarahara River in Mahajanga province has been dammed for irrigation and extraction, causing the river to dry up seasonally; the last wild individuals disappeared when the river failed entirely.
ecosystemNeeds: The Mangarahara River and its tributaries in northeastern Madagascar, with clear, flowing water, sandy and rocky substrate, and riverine gallery forest providing shade and organic input.
actions: Support ZSL London Zoo and Bristol Zoo's Mangarahara cichlid captive breeding programme, fund hydrological surveys to identify any remaining wild habitat, and advocate for river flow restoration in Mahajanga.

en.description: The Mangarahara cichlid is a freshwater fish from a small river system in northeastern Madagascar. The Mangarahara River is believed to have dried up completely, and the species was declared extinct in the wild around 2013.
en.endangerment: The Mangarahara River in Mahajanga province has been dammed for irrigation and extraction, causing the river to dry up seasonally; the last wild individuals disappeared when the river failed entirely.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The Mangarahara River and its tributaries in northeastern Madagascar, with clear, flowing water, sandy and rocky substrate, and riverine gallery forest providing shade and organic input.
en.actions: Support ZSL London Zoo and Bristol Zoo's Mangarahara cichlid captive breeding programme, fund hydrological surveys to identify any remaining wild habitat, and advocate for river flow restoration in Mahajanga.

es.description: El cíclido Mangarahara es un pez de agua dulce de un pequeño sistema fluvial en el noreste de Madagascar. Se cree que el Río Mangarahara se ha secado completamente, y la especie fue declarada extinta en la naturaleza alrededor de 2013.
es.endangerment: El Río Mangarahara en la provincia de Mahajanga ha sido represado para irrigación y extracción, provocando que el río se seque estacionalmente; los últimos individuos silvestres desaparecieron cuando el río falló completamente.
es.ecosystemNeeds: El Río Mangarahara y sus afluentes en el noreste de Madagascar, con agua clara y fluyente, sustrato arenoso y rocoso, y bosque galería ribereño que proporciona sombra y aporte orgánico.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría en cautiverio del cíclido Mangarahara del ZSL London Zoo y Bristol Zoo, financiar encuestas hidrológicas para identificar cualquier hábitat silvestre restante y abogar por la restauración del flujo fluvial en Mahajanga.

pt.description: O cíclido-mangarahara é um peixe de água doce de um pequeno sistema fluvial no nordeste de Madagascar. Acredita-se que o Rio Mangarahara tenha secado completamente, e a espécie foi declarada extinta na natureza por volta de 2013.
pt.endangerment: O Rio Mangarahara na província de Mahajanga foi represado para irrigação e extração, fazendo com que o rio secasse sazonalmente; os últimos indivíduos selvagens desapareceram quando o rio falhou completamente.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: O Rio Mangarahara e seus afluentes no nordeste de Madagascar, com água clara e fluente, substrato arenoso e rochoso, e floresta galeria ribeirinha fornecendo sombra e aporte orgânico.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro do cíclido Mangarahara do ZSL London Zoo e Bristol Zoo, financiar levantamentos hidrológicos para identificar qualquer habitat selvagem restante e defender a restauração do fluxo fluvial em Mahajanga.

fr.description: Le cichlidé de Mangarahara est un poisson d'eau douce d'un petit système fluvial dans le nord-est de Madagascar. La rivière Mangarahara est réputée avoir complètement tari, et l'espèce a été déclarée éteinte à l'état sauvage vers 2013.
fr.endangerment: La rivière Mangarahara dans la province de Mahajanga a été endiguée pour l'irrigation et l'extraction, entraînant l'assèchement saisonnier de la rivière ; les derniers individus sauvages ont disparu lorsque la rivière a complètement tari.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: La rivière Mangarahara et ses affluents dans le nord-est de Madagascar, avec de l'eau claire et courante, un substrat sableux et rocheux, et une forêt galerie riveraine fournissant de l'ombre et des apports organiques.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage en captivité du cichlidé de Mangarahara du ZSL London Zoo et du Bristol Zoo, financer des enquêtes hydrologiques pour identifier tout habitat sauvage restant et plaider pour la restauration du débit fluvial à Mahajanga.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Clanwilliam cedar is a majestic conifer that once covered large swathes of the Cederberg mountains in the Western Cape. Now reduced to scattered, mostly elderly individuals, the species is failing to regenerate in a warming, fire-prone landscape.
endangerment: More frequent and intense wildfires, driven by climate change and invasive pine that fuels hotter fires, kill adult trees before seeds can mature; surviving trees are mainly old individuals with poor regeneration rates.
ecosystemNeeds: Rocky sandstone plateaux and cliff faces in the Cederberg Wilderness Area at 900–1,500 metres elevation, in the protea-dominated mountain fynbos, with fire-return intervals of at least 15 years for successful regeneration.
actions: Support Cape Nature's Cederberg cedar restoration programme, fund active replanting in fire-managed areas, and advocate for invasive pine clearance throughout the Cederberg to reduce catastrophic fire intensity.

en.description: The Clanwilliam cedar is a majestic conifer that once covered large swathes of the Cederberg mountains in the Western Cape. Now reduced to scattered, mostly elderly individuals, the species is failing to regenerate in a warming, fire-prone landscape.
en.endangerment: More frequent and intense wildfires, driven by climate change and invasive pine that fuels hotter fires, kill adult trees before seeds can mature; surviving trees are mainly old individuals with poor regeneration rates.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Rocky sandstone plateaux and cliff faces in the Cederberg Wilderness Area at 900–1,500 metres elevation, in the protea-dominated mountain fynbos, with fire-return intervals of at least 15 years for successful regeneration.
en.actions: Support Cape Nature's Cederberg cedar restoration programme, fund active replanting in fire-managed areas, and advocate for invasive pine clearance throughout the Cederberg to reduce catastrophic fire intensity.

es.description: El cedro de Clanwilliam es un majestuoso conífera que alguna vez cubrió grandes franjas de las montañas Cederberg en el Cabo Occidental. Ahora reducido a individuos dispersos, en su mayoría ancianos, la especie no está logrando regenerarse en un paisaje que se calienta y es propenso a incendios.
es.endangerment: Los incendios forestales más frecuentes e intensos, impulsados por el cambio climático y el pino invasor que alimenta incendios más calientes, matan a los árboles adultos antes de que las semillas puedan madurar; los árboles supervivientes son principalmente individuos viejos con tasas de regeneración deficientes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Mesetas de arenisca rocosa y acantilados en la Área Silvestre de Cederberg a 900-1.500 metros de altitud, en el fynbos montañoso dominado por proteas, con intervalos de retorno de incendios de al menos 15 años para una regeneración exitosa.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de restauración del cedro Cederberg de Cape Nature, financiar la replantación activa en áreas gestionadas contra incendios y abogar por la eliminación de pinos invasores en todo el Cederberg para reducir la intensidad de incendios catastróficos.

pt.description: O cedro-de-clanwilliam é uma majestosa conífera que antes cobria grandes extensões das montanhas Cederberg no Cabo Ocidental. Agora reduzido a indivíduos dispersos, principalmente idosos, a espécie não está conseguindo se regenerar em uma paisagem que aquece e é propensa a incêndios.
pt.endangerment: Incêndios florestais mais frequentes e intensos, impulsionados pelas mudanças climáticas e pelo pinheiro invasor que alimenta incêndios mais quentes, matam as árvores adultas antes que as sementes possam amadurecer; as árvores sobreviventes são principalmente indivíduos velhos com baixas taxas de regeneração.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Planaltos de arenito rochoso e falésias na Área Silvestre de Cederberg a 900-1.500 metros de altitude, no fynbos montanhoso dominado por proteas, com intervalos de retorno de incêndio de pelo menos 15 anos para regeneração bem-sucedida.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de restauração do cedro-de-cederberg da Cape Nature, financiar o replantio ativo em áreas gerenciadas contra incêndios e defender a limpeza de pinheiros invasores em todo o Cederberg para reduzir a intensidade de incêndios catastróficos.

fr.description: Le cèdre de Clanwilliam est un conifère majestueux qui couvrait autrefois de larges étendues des montagnes du Cederberg dans le Cap-Occidental. Désormais réduit à des individus épars, principalement âgés, l'espèce ne parvient pas à se régénérer dans un paysage se réchauffant et sujet aux incendies.
fr.endangerment: Des incendies de forêt plus fréquents et intenses, alimentés par le changement climatique et les pins envahissants qui nourrissent des incendies plus chauds, tuent les arbres adultes avant que les graines puissent mûrir ; les arbres survivants sont principalement de vieux individus avec de faibles taux de régénération.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Plateaux de grès rocheux et falaises dans la zone sauvage du Cederberg à 900-1 500 mètres d'altitude, dans le fynbos montagnard dominé par les protéas, avec des intervalles de retour du feu d'au moins 15 ans pour une régénération réussie.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de restauration du cèdre du Cederberg de Cape Nature, financer la replantation active dans les zones gérées contre les incendies et plaider pour l'élimination des pins envahissants dans tout le Cederberg pour réduire l'intensité des incendies catastrophiques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The St. Helena ebony is a small flowering tree endemic to the remote island of St. Helena in the South Atlantic. Once extinct in the wild, it was rescued from total extinction by cuttings grown in cultivation and subsequently replanted on the island.
endangerment: Introduced goats eliminated all native St. Helena forest over centuries; by 1998, the species was considered extinct in the wild with only a handful of cultivated individuals remaining.
ecosystemNeeds: Semi-arid rocky slopes in the central cloud forest zone of St. Helena island at 500–800 metres, with cloud moisture, sheltered aspects, and goat-free conditions that allow natural regeneration.
actions: Support the St. Helena National Trust's ebony reintroduction programme, fund goat exclusion fencing around planting sites, and advocate for native forest restoration across the island's central ridge.

en.description: The St. Helena ebony is a small flowering tree endemic to the remote island of St. Helena in the South Atlantic. Once extinct in the wild, it was rescued from total extinction by cuttings grown in cultivation and subsequently replanted on the island.
en.endangerment: Introduced goats eliminated all native St. Helena forest over centuries; by 1998, the species was considered extinct in the wild with only a handful of cultivated individuals remaining.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Semi-arid rocky slopes in the central cloud forest zone of St. Helena island at 500–800 metres, with cloud moisture, sheltered aspects, and goat-free conditions that allow natural regeneration.
en.actions: Support the St. Helena National Trust's ebony reintroduction programme, fund goat exclusion fencing around planting sites, and advocate for native forest restoration across the island's central ridge.

es.description: El ébano de Santa Elena es un pequeño árbol con flores endémico de la remota isla de Santa Elena en el Atlántico Sur. Antes extinto en la naturaleza, fue rescatado de la extinción total por esquejes cultivados en cautiverio y posteriormente replantado en la isla.
es.endangerment: Las cabras introducidas eliminaron todo el bosque nativo de Santa Elena durante siglos; para 1998, la especie se consideraba extinta en la naturaleza con solo un puñado de individuos cultivados restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Laderas rocosas semiáridas en la zona del bosque nuboso central de Santa Elena a 500-800 metros, con humedad de nubes, aspectos resguardados y condiciones libres de cabras que permitan la regeneración natural.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de reintroducción del ébano del Fideicomiso Nacional de Santa Elena, financiar cercas de exclusión de cabras alrededor de los sitios de plantación y abogar por la restauración del bosque nativo en toda la cresta central de la isla.

pt.description: O ébano-de-santa-helena é uma pequena árvore florida endêmica da remota ilha de Santa Helena no Atlântico Sul. Antes extinto na natureza, foi resgatado da extinção total por estacas cultivadas em cativeiro e posteriormente replantadas na ilha.
pt.endangerment: Cabras introduzidas eliminaram toda a floresta nativa de Santa Helena ao longo dos séculos; em 1998, a espécie era considerada extinta na natureza com apenas um punhado de indivíduos cultivados restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Encostas rochosas semiáridas na zona da floresta nebular central de Santa Helena a 500-800 metros, com umidade de nuvens, aspectos abrigados e condições livres de cabras que permitem a regeneração natural.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de reintrodução do ébano do Fideicomisso Nacional de Santa Helena, financiar cercas de exclusão de cabras ao redor dos locais de plantio e defender a restauração da floresta nativa em toda a crista central da ilha.

fr.description: L'ébène de Sainte-Hélène est un petit arbre à fleurs endémique de la lointaine île de Sainte-Hélène dans l'Atlantique Sud. Autrefois éteint à l'état sauvage, il a été sauvé de l'extinction totale par des boutures cultivées en captivité puis replantées sur l'île.
fr.endangerment: Les chèvres introduites ont éliminé toute la forêt indigène de Sainte-Hélène au fil des siècles ; en 1998, l'espèce était considérée comme éteinte à l'état sauvage avec seulement une poignée d'individus cultivés restants.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Versants rocheux semi-arides dans la zone de forêt nuageuse centrale de Sainte-Hélène à 500-800 mètres, avec humidité nuageuse, versants abrités et conditions sans chèvres permettant la régénération naturelle.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de réintroduction de l'ébène du National Trust de Sainte-Hélène, financer des clôtures d'exclusion des chèvres autour des sites de plantation et plaider pour la restauration des forêts indigènes sur toute la crête centrale de l'île.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Wallace's giant bee is the world's largest bee, with a wingspan of 6 cm and large mandibles used to gather tree resin. Known only from a few Indonesian islands in North Maluku, it was lost to science for 38 years until its rediscovery in 2019.
endangerment: Deforestation for cocoa, oil palm, and logging has destroyed most of the lowland forest habitat on Bacan, Obi, and Halmahera islands; the species' nest sites in occupied termite mounds are destroyed with the trees they inhabit.
ecosystemNeeds: Lowland tropical forest on the North Maluku islands of Indonesia, particularly Bacan and Obi, with arboreal termite nests in tall trees that the bee uses as nest sites and surrounding forest producing resin.
actions: Support Wildlife Conservation Society's North Maluku programme to protect lowland forest, advocate for CITES enforcement against wildlife collectors, and fund systematic nest site surveys to map remaining populations.

en.description: Wallace's giant bee is the world's largest bee, with a wingspan of 6 cm and large mandibles used to gather tree resin. Known only from a few Indonesian islands in North Maluku, it was lost to science for 38 years until its rediscovery in 2019.
en.endangerment: Deforestation for cocoa, oil palm, and logging has destroyed most of the lowland forest habitat on Bacan, Obi, and Halmahera islands; the species' nest sites in occupied termite mounds are destroyed with the trees they inhabit.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Lowland tropical forest on the North Maluku islands of Indonesia, particularly Bacan and Obi, with arboreal termite nests in tall trees that the bee uses as nest sites and surrounding forest producing resin.
en.actions: Support Wildlife Conservation Society's North Maluku programme to protect lowland forest, advocate for CITES enforcement against wildlife collectors, and fund systematic nest site surveys to map remaining populations.

es.description: La abeja gigante de Wallace es la abeja más grande del mundo, con una envergadura de 6 cm y grandes mandíbulas usadas para recolectar resina de árboles. Conocida solo en algunas islas indonesias en el Norte de Maluku, estuvo perdida para la ciencia durante 38 años hasta su redescubrimiento en 2019.
es.endangerment: La deforestación para cacao, palma de aceite y tala ha destruido la mayor parte del hábitat de bosque de tierras bajas en las islas de Bacan, Obi y Halmahera; los sitios de anidamiento de la especie en termiteros ocupados son destruidos con los árboles que habitan.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque tropical de tierras bajas en las islas del Norte de Maluku de Indonesia, particularmente Bacan y Obi, con nidos de termitas arbóreas en árboles altos que la abeja usa como sitios de anidamiento y el bosque circundante que produce resina.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa del Norte de Maluku de la Wildlife Conservation Society para proteger el bosque de tierras bajas, abogar por la aplicación de la CITES contra los coleccionistas de vida silvestre y financiar encuestas sistemáticas de sitios de nidificación para mapear las poblaciones restantes.

pt.description: A abelha-gigante-de-wallace é a maior abelha do mundo, com envergadura de 6 cm e grandes mandíbulas usadas para coletar resina de árvores. Conhecida apenas em algumas ilhas indonésias no Norte de Maluku, estava perdida para a ciência por 38 anos até sua redescoberta em 2019.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento para cacau, óleo de palma e extração de madeira destruiu a maior parte do habitat de floresta de baixadas nas ilhas de Bacan, Obi e Halmahera; os locais de nidificação da espécie em ninhos de cupins habitados são destruídos com as árvores que habitam.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical de baixadas nas ilhas do Norte de Maluku da Indonésia, particularmente Bacan e Obi, com ninhos de cupins arborícolas em árvores altas que a abelha usa como locais de nidificação e a floresta circundante produzindo resina.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa do Norte de Maluku da Wildlife Conservation Society para proteger a floresta de baixadas, defender a aplicação da CITES contra os coletores de vida silvestre e financiar levantamentos sistemáticos de locais de nidificação para mapear as populações restantes.

fr.description: L'abeille géante de Wallace est la plus grande abeille du monde, avec une envergure de 6 cm et de grandes mandibules utilisées pour récolter la résine des arbres. Connue seulement de quelques îles indonésiennes dans le Nord des Moluques, elle était perdue pour la science pendant 38 ans jusqu'à sa redécouverte en 2019.
fr.endangerment: La déforestation pour le cacao, l'huile de palme et l'exploitation forestière a détruit la majeure partie de l'habitat forestier de basse altitude sur les îles de Bacan, Obi et Halmahera ; les sites de nidification de l'espèce dans des termitières occupées sont détruits avec les arbres qu'ils habitent.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale de basse altitude sur les îles du Nord des Moluques d'Indonésie, notamment Bacan et Obi, avec des nids de termites arboricoles dans de grands arbres que l'abeille utilise comme sites de nidification et la forêt environnante produisant de la résine.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme Nord Moluques de la Wildlife Conservation Society pour protéger la forêt de basse altitude, plaider pour l'application de la CITES contre les collectionneurs de faune sauvage et financer des enquêtes systématiques des sites de nidification pour cartographier les populations restantes.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The monarch butterfly undertakes one of nature's most extraordinary migrations, travelling up to 4,800 km from Canada and the USA to overwintering colonies in the oyamel fir forests of central Mexico. The migratory population was added to the IUCN Red List as Critically Endangered in 2022.
endangerment: Loss of milkweed through herbicide use and agriculture in North America has removed the only plant monarch caterpillars can eat; climate extremes disrupt migration; illegal logging at overwintering sites removes refuge trees.
ecosystemNeeds: Milkweed-containing grasslands, meadows, and fields across North America for breeding; overwintering in oyamel fir and pine-oak forest in the Trans-Mexican Volcanic Belt at 2,400–3,600 m elevation in Michoacán and Mexico State.
actions: Plant milkweed in gardens and on farms along the migration corridor, support World Wildlife Fund-Mexico's Sierra Chincua reserve protection, and advocate for milkweed-positive agricultural policies across the breeding range.

en.description: The monarch butterfly undertakes one of nature's most extraordinary migrations, travelling up to 4,800 km from Canada and the USA to overwintering colonies in the oyamel fir forests of central Mexico. The migratory population was added to the IUCN Red List as Critically Endangered in 2022.
en.endangerment: Loss of milkweed through herbicide use and agriculture in North America has removed the only plant monarch caterpillars can eat; climate extremes disrupt migration; illegal logging at overwintering sites removes refuge trees.
