en.ecosystemNeeds: Milkweed-containing grasslands, meadows, and fields across North America for breeding; overwintering in oyamel fir and pine-oak forest in the Trans-Mexican Volcanic Belt at 2,400–3,600 m elevation in Michoacán and Mexico State.
en.actions: Plant milkweed in gardens and on farms along the migration corridor, support World Wildlife Fund-Mexico's Sierra Chincua reserve protection, and advocate for milkweed-positive agricultural policies across the breeding range.

es.description: La mariposa monarca realiza una de las migraciones más extraordinarias de la naturaleza, viajando hasta 4.800 km desde Canadá y EE.UU. a las colonias de invernada en los bosques de abeto oyamel del centro de México. La población migratoria fue añadida a la Lista Roja de la UICN como En Peligro Crítico en 2022.
es.endangerment: La pérdida de asclepia por el uso de herbicidas y la agricultura en América del Norte ha eliminado la única planta que las orugas monarca pueden comer; los extremos climáticos interrumpen la migración; la tala ilegal en los sitios de invernada elimina los árboles refugio.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Pastizales, prados y campos que contienen asclepia en América del Norte para la reproducción; invernada en bosques de abeto oyamel y pino-encino en el Cinturón Volcánico Trans-Mexicano a 2.400-3.600 m de altitud en Michoacán y el Estado de México.
es.actions: Plantar asclepia en jardines y granjas a lo largo del corredor de migración, apoyar la protección de la reserva Sierra Chincua de WWF-México y abogar por políticas agrícolas favorables a la asclepia en toda el área de reproducción.

pt.description: A borboleta-monarca realiza uma das mais extraordinárias migrações da natureza, viajando até 4.800 km do Canadá e dos EUA para as colônias de invernada nas florestas de abeto oyamel do centro do México. A população migratória foi adicionada à Lista Vermelha da IUCN como Criticamente Ameaçada em 2022.
pt.endangerment: A perda de asclépia pelo uso de herbicidas e pela agricultura na América do Norte eliminou a única planta que as lagartas monarca podem comer; os extremos climáticos interrompem a migração; o desmatamento ilegal nos locais de invernada remove as árvores de refúgio.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pastagens, prados e campos contendo asclépia na América do Norte para reprodução; invernada em florestas de abeto oyamel e pinheiro-carvalho no Cinturão Vulcânico Trans-Mexicano a 2.400-3.600 m de altitude em Michoacán e no Estado do México.
pt.actions: Plantar asclépia em jardins e fazendas ao longo do corredor de migração, apoiar a proteção da reserva Sierra Chincua da WWF-México e defender políticas agrícolas favoráveis à asclépia em toda a área de reprodução.

fr.description: Le papillon monarque effectue l'une des migrations les plus extraordinaires de la nature, parcourant jusqu'à 4 800 km du Canada et des États-Unis jusqu'aux colonies d'hivernage dans les forêts de sapins oyamel du centre du Mexique. La population migratrice a été ajoutée à la Liste rouge de l'UICN en tant qu'espèce en danger critique de disparition en 2022.
fr.endangerment: La perte d'asclépiade due à l'utilisation d'herbicides et à l'agriculture en Amérique du Nord a éliminé la seule plante que les chenilles monarques peuvent manger ; les extrêmes climatiques perturbent la migration ; l'exploitation forestière illégale sur les sites d'hivernage élimine les arbres refuges.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Prairies, prés et champs contenant de l'asclépiade en Amérique du Nord pour la reproduction ; hivernage dans les forêts de sapins oyamel et de pins-chênes dans la ceinture volcanique trans-mexicaine à 2 400-3 600 m d'altitude au Michoacán et dans l'État de Mexico.
fr.actions: Planter de l'asclépiade dans les jardins et les fermes le long du couloir de migration, soutenir la protection de la réserve de la Sierra Chincua du WWF-Mexique et plaider pour des politiques agricoles favorables à l'asclépiade dans toute l'aire de reproduction.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Indiana cave crayfish is a pale, eyeless freshwater crustacean adapted to the perpetual darkness of limestone cave streams in south-central Indiana. It represents an ancient lineage isolated underground since the Pleistocene.
endangerment: Agricultural runoff carrying pesticides, sediment, and fertilisers seeps into the karst groundwater system, poisoning cave streams; groundwater extraction for crop irrigation lowers water tables, drying cave pools.
ecosystemNeeds: Underground streams and cave pools in the karst limestone aquifer of south-central Indiana, with clean, cold, well-oxygenated groundwater, organic debris washed in from surface as food, and stable cave hydrology.
actions: Advocate for agricultural buffer zones along cave recharge areas in south-central Indiana, support Indiana Department of Natural Resources' cave ecosystem monitoring, and fund karst groundwater quality surveys.

en.description: The Indiana cave crayfish is a pale, eyeless freshwater crustacean adapted to the perpetual darkness of limestone cave streams in south-central Indiana. It represents an ancient lineage isolated underground since the Pleistocene.
en.endangerment: Agricultural runoff carrying pesticides, sediment, and fertilisers seeps into the karst groundwater system, poisoning cave streams; groundwater extraction for crop irrigation lowers water tables, drying cave pools.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Underground streams and cave pools in the karst limestone aquifer of south-central Indiana, with clean, cold, well-oxygenated groundwater, organic debris washed in from surface as food, and stable cave hydrology.
en.actions: Advocate for agricultural buffer zones along cave recharge areas in south-central Indiana, support Indiana Department of Natural Resources' cave ecosystem monitoring, and fund karst groundwater quality surveys.

es.description: El cangrejo de río de las cuevas de Indiana es un crustáceo de agua dulce pálido y sin ojos adaptado a la oscuridad perpetua de los arroyos subterráneos de piedra caliza en el centro-sur de Indiana. Representa un antiguo linaje aislado bajo tierra desde el Pleistoceno.
es.endangerment: La escorrentía agrícola que transporta pesticidas, sedimentos y fertilizantes se filtra en el sistema de aguas subterráneas kársticas, envenenando los arroyos de las cuevas; la extracción de aguas subterráneas para el riego de cultivos baja los niveles freáticos, secando las charcas subterráneas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arroyos subterráneos y charcas de cuevas en el acuífero de piedra caliza kárstica del centro-sur de Indiana, con agua subterránea limpia, fría y bien oxigenada, detritos orgánicos arrastrados desde la superficie como alimento e hidrología estable de la cueva.
es.actions: Abogar por zonas de amortiguación agrícola a lo largo de las áreas de recarga de cuevas en el centro-sur de Indiana, apoyar el monitoreo del ecosistema de cuevas del Departamento de Recursos Naturales de Indiana y financiar encuestas de calidad de aguas subterráneas kársticas.

pt.description: O lagostim-de-caverna-de-indiana é um crustáceo de água doce pálido e sem olhos adaptado à escuridão perpétua dos riachos subterrâneos de calcário no centro-sul de Indiana. Representa uma linhagem antiga isolada no subsolo desde o Pleistoceno.
pt.endangerment: O escoamento agrícola que carrega pesticidas, sedimentos e fertilizantes penetra no sistema de águas subterrâneas cársticas, envenenando os riachos das cavernas; a extração de águas subterrâneas para irrigação de culturas abaixa os lençóis freáticos, secando as poças subterrâneas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Riachos subterrâneos e poças de cavernas no aquífero de calcário cárstico do centro-sul de Indiana, com água subterrânea limpa, fria e bem oxigenada, detritos orgânicos lavados da superfície como alimento e hidrologia estável da caverna.
pt.actions: Defender zonas de amortecimento agrícola ao longo das áreas de recarga de cavernas no centro-sul de Indiana, apoiar o monitoramento do ecossistema de cavernas do Departamento de Recursos Naturais de Indiana e financiar levantamentos de qualidade de águas subterrâneas cársticas.

fr.description: L'écrevisse des cavernes de l'Indiana est un crustacé d'eau douce pâle et sans yeux adapté à l'obscurité permanente des ruisseaux souterrains calcaires du centre-sud de l'Indiana. Elle représente une ancienne lignée isolée sous terre depuis le Pléistocène.
fr.endangerment: Le ruissellement agricole transportant des pesticides, des sédiments et des engrais s'infiltre dans le système d'eaux souterraines karstiques, empoisonnant les ruisseaux des grottes ; l'extraction des eaux souterraines pour l'irrigation agricole abaisse les nappes phréatiques, asséchant les mares souterraines.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Ruisseaux souterrains et mares de grottes dans l'aquifère calcaire karstique du centre-sud de l'Indiana, avec des eaux souterraines propres, froides et bien oxygénées, des débris organiques apportés de la surface comme nourriture et une hydrologie stable de la grotte.
fr.actions: Plaider pour des zones tampons agricoles le long des zones de recharge des grottes dans le centre-sud de l'Indiana, soutenir le suivi de l'écosystème des grottes du Département des ressources naturelles de l'Indiana et financer des enquêtes sur la qualité des eaux souterraines karstiques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The baiji, or Yangtze River dolphin, was a freshwater dolphin endemic to the Yangtze River in China. A 2006 survey failed to detect any individuals, and it is now considered functionally extinct — the first cetacean driven to extinction in modern times.
endangerment: Intensive river traffic, electrofishing, dam construction blocking migration, and industrial pollution destroyed the Yangtze ecosystem on which the baiji depended. A 1997 estimate counted only 13 individuals; by 2006, none could be found.
ecosystemNeeds: The main channel of the Yangtze River in central and eastern China with clear water, abundant fish, low boat traffic, and undisturbed stretches for echolocation-based navigation in murky river conditions.
actions: Support the Yangtze River Protection Law's ban on fishing until 2030, fund environmental DNA surveys for any surviving individuals, and back efforts to restore Yangtze ecosystem health as a memorial and model for river conservation.

en.description: The baiji, or Yangtze River dolphin, was a freshwater dolphin endemic to the Yangtze River in China. A 2006 survey failed to detect any individuals, and it is now considered functionally extinct — the first cetacean driven to extinction in modern times.
en.endangerment: Intensive river traffic, electrofishing, dam construction blocking migration, and industrial pollution destroyed the Yangtze ecosystem on which the baiji depended. A 1997 estimate counted only 13 individuals; by 2006, none could be found.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The main channel of the Yangtze River in central and eastern China with clear water, abundant fish, low boat traffic, and undisturbed stretches for echolocation-based navigation in murky river conditions.
en.actions: Support the Yangtze River Protection Law's ban on fishing until 2030, fund environmental DNA surveys for any surviving individuals, and back efforts to restore Yangtze ecosystem health as a memorial and model for river conservation.

es.description: El baiji, o delfín del Río Yangtze, era un delfín de río endémico del Río Yangtze en China. Una encuesta de 2006 no logró detectar ningún individuo, y ahora se considera funcionalmente extinto — el primer cetáceo conducido a la extinción en tiempos modernos.
es.endangerment: El intenso tráfico fluvial, la pesca eléctrica, la construcción de presas que bloqueaban la migración y la contaminación industrial destruyeron el ecosistema del Yangtze del que dependía el baiji. Una estimación de 1997 contó solo 13 individuos; para 2006, no se podía encontrar ninguno.
es.ecosystemNeeds: El canal principal del Río Yangtze en el centro y este de China con agua clara, abundantes peces, poco tráfico de barcos y tramos sin perturbaciones para la navegación basada en ecolocalización en condiciones de río turbio.
es.actions: Apoyar la prohibición de pesca de la Ley de Protección del Río Yangtze hasta 2030, financiar encuestas de ADN ambiental para cualquier individuo superviviente y respaldar los esfuerzos para restaurar la salud del ecosistema del Yangtze como memorial y modelo para la conservación fluvial.

pt.description: O baiji, ou golfinho-do-rio-yangtze, era um golfinho fluvial endêmico do Rio Yangtze na China. Uma pesquisa de 2006 não detectou nenhum indivíduo, e agora é considerado funcionalmente extinto — o primeiro cetáceo conduzido à extinção nos tempos modernos.
pt.endangerment: O intenso tráfego fluvial, a pesca elétrica, a construção de barragens bloqueando a migração e a poluição industrial destruíram o ecossistema do Yangtze do qual o baiji dependia. Uma estimativa de 1997 contou apenas 13 indivíduos; em 2006, nenhum pôde ser encontrado.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: O canal principal do Rio Yangtze no centro e leste da China com água clara, peixes abundantes, baixo tráfego de barcos e trechos sem perturbações para a navegação baseada em ecolocalização em condições de rio turvo.
pt.actions: Apoiar a proibição de pesca da Lei de Proteção do Rio Yangtze até 2030, financiar levantamentos de DNA ambiental para qualquer indivíduo sobrevivente e apoiar os esforços para restaurar a saúde do ecossistema do Yangtze como memorial e modelo para a conservação fluvial.

fr.description: Le baiji, ou dauphin du fleuve Yangtsé, était un dauphin fluvial endémique du fleuve Yangtsé en Chine. Une enquête menée en 2006 n'a détecté aucun individu, et il est maintenant considéré comme fonctionnellement éteint — le premier cétacé conduit à l'extinction dans les temps modernes.
fr.endangerment: L'intense trafic fluvial, la pêche électrique, la construction de barrages bloquant la migration et la pollution industrielle ont détruit l'écosystème du Yangtsé dont dépendait le baiji. Une estimation de 1997 en comptait seulement 13 ; en 2006, aucun n'a pu être trouvé.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Le chenal principal du fleuve Yangtsé dans le centre et l'est de la Chine avec de l'eau claire, des poissons abondants, un faible trafic de bateaux et des tronçons non perturbés pour la navigation par écholocation dans les eaux turbides du fleuve.
fr.actions: Soutenir l'interdiction de pêche de la loi sur la protection du fleuve Yangtsé jusqu'en 2030, financer des enquêtes d'ADN environnemental pour tout individu survivant et soutenir les efforts de restauration de la santé de l'écosystème du Yangtsé comme mémorial et modèle pour la conservation des rivières.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The North Atlantic right whale is among the world's most endangered large whales, with fewer than 370 individuals remaining. Once targeted by whalers for its high blubber content and tendency to float when killed, giving it its name as the "right" whale to hunt.
endangerment: Rope entanglement in fishing gear — particularly lobster trap lines — and vessel strikes kill several whales every year, a rate that exceeds reproduction. Climate change is shifting prey into areas with higher fishing activity.
ecosystemNeeds: North Atlantic coastal and offshore waters from Florida to the Gulf of St. Lawrence, feeding on dense aggregations of copepods in cold, productive shelf waters and using protected bays for calving.
actions: Support NOAA's large-whale entanglement response, advocate for ropeless fishing gear adoption in the US northeast, and back mandatory speed restrictions for vessels in right whale habitat.

en.description: The North Atlantic right whale is among the world's most endangered large whales, with fewer than 370 individuals remaining. Once targeted by whalers for its high blubber content and tendency to float when killed, giving it its name as the "right" whale to hunt.
en.endangerment: Rope entanglement in fishing gear — particularly lobster trap lines — and vessel strikes kill several whales every year, a rate that exceeds reproduction. Climate change is shifting prey into areas with higher fishing activity.
en.ecosystemNeeds: North Atlantic coastal and offshore waters from Florida to the Gulf of St. Lawrence, feeding on dense aggregations of copepods in cold, productive shelf waters and using protected bays for calving.
en.actions: Support NOAA's large-whale entanglement response, advocate for ropeless fishing gear adoption in the US northeast, and back mandatory speed restrictions for vessels in right whale habitat.

es.description: La ballena franca del Atlántico Norte está entre las ballenas grandes más amenazadas del mundo, con menos de 370 individuos restantes. Antes cazada por los balleneros por su alto contenido de grasa y su tendencia a flotar cuando era matada, dándole su nombre como la ballena 'correcta' para cazar.
es.endangerment: El enredo en cuerdas de equipos de pesca —particularmente las líneas de trampas de langosta— y las colisiones con barcos matan varias ballenas cada año, una tasa que supera la reproducción. El cambio climático está desplazando las presas hacia áreas con mayor actividad pesquera.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas costeras y de alta mar del Atlántico Norte desde Florida hasta el Golfo de San Lorenzo, alimentándose de densas agregaciones de copépodos en aguas frías y productivas de la plataforma continental y usando bahías protegidas para el parto.
es.actions: Apoyar la respuesta de enredo de ballenas grandes de la NOAA, abogar por la adopción de equipos de pesca sin cuerdas en el noreste de EE.UU. y respaldar restricciones de velocidad obligatorias para los barcos en el hábitat de la ballena franca.

pt.description: A baleia-franca-do-atlântico-norte está entre as maiores baleias mais ameaçadas do mundo, com menos de 370 indivíduos restantes. Antes alvo dos baleeiros por seu alto teor de gordura e tendência a flutuar quando morta, dando-lhe o nome de baleia 'certa' para caçar.
pt.endangerment: O emaranhamento em cordas de equipamentos de pesca — particularmente linhas de armadilhas de lagosta — e colisões com navios matam várias baleias a cada ano, uma taxa que supera a reprodução. As mudanças climáticas estão deslocando as presas para áreas com maior atividade pesqueira.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas costeiras e ao largo do Atlântico Norte da Flórida ao Golfo de São Lourenço, alimentando-se de densas agregações de copépodos em águas frias e produtivas da plataforma continental e usando baías protegidas para o parto.
pt.actions: Apoiar a resposta de emaranhamento de grandes baleias da NOAA, defender a adoção de equipamentos de pesca sem cordas no nordeste dos EUA e apoiar restrições de velocidade obrigatórias para navios no habitat da baleia-franca.

fr.description: La baleine noire de l'Atlantique Nord figure parmi les grandes baleines les plus menacées du monde, avec moins de 370 individus restants. Autrefois ciblée par les baleiniers pour sa forte teneur en graisse et sa tendance à flotter lorsqu'elle était tuée, lui donnant son nom de baleine « juste » à chasser.
fr.endangerment: L'enchevêtrement dans les cordages des engins de pêche — notamment les lignes des casiers à homards — et les collisions de navires tuent plusieurs baleines chaque année, à un rythme qui dépasse la reproduction. Le changement climatique déplace les proies vers des zones à plus forte activité de pêche.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Eaux côtières et hauturières de l'Atlantique Nord de la Floride au golfe du Saint-Laurent, se nourrissant de denses agrégations de copépodes dans les eaux froides et productives du plateau continental et utilisant des baies abritées pour les mises bas.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de réponse aux enchevêtrements de grandes baleines de la NOAA, plaider pour l'adoption d'engins de pêche sans cordages dans le nord-est des États-Unis et soutenir des restrictions de vitesse obligatoires pour les navires dans l'habitat de la baleine noire.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The roloway monkey is a striking primate with a distinctive white beard and dark face, found in the Upper Guinea forests of Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana. Surveys suggest it may be functionally extinct in Ghana with only a few remaining populations in Côte d'Ivoire.
endangerment: Bushmeat hunting, with roloway monkeys considered a delicacy in local markets, has devastated populations across both countries; simultaneous deforestation for cocoa farming has eliminated remaining habitat corridors.
ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane tropical rainforest in the Upper Guinea biodiversity hotspot across southern Côte d'Ivoire, with access to fruiting trees, a closed canopy, and protection from hunting disturbance.
actions: Support WAPCA's roloway monkey rescue centre and anti-poaching efforts in the Tanoé-Ehy forest in Côte d'Ivoire, advocate for community-based bushmeat reduction, and fund systematic range surveys.

en.description: The roloway monkey is a striking primate with a distinctive white beard and dark face, found in the Upper Guinea forests of Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana. Surveys suggest it may be functionally extinct in Ghana with only a few remaining populations in Côte d'Ivoire.
en.endangerment: Bushmeat hunting, with roloway monkeys considered a delicacy in local markets, has devastated populations across both countries; simultaneous deforestation for cocoa farming has eliminated remaining habitat corridors.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact lowland and montane tropical rainforest in the Upper Guinea biodiversity hotspot across southern Côte d'Ivoire, with access to fruiting trees, a closed canopy, and protection from hunting disturbance.
en.actions: Support WAPCA's roloway monkey rescue centre and anti-poaching efforts in the Tanoé-Ehy forest in Côte d'Ivoire, advocate for community-based bushmeat reduction, and fund systematic range surveys.

es.description: El mono roloway es un primate llamativo con una distintiva barba blanca y cara oscura, encontrado en los bosques de la Alta Guinea de Côte d'Ivoire y Ghana. Los estudios sugieren que puede estar funcionalmente extinto en Ghana con solo unas pocas poblaciones restantes en Côte d'Ivoire.
es.endangerment: La caza de carne de monte, considerando los monos roloway un manjar en los mercados locales, ha devastado las poblaciones en ambos países; la deforestación simultánea para el cultivo de cacao ha eliminado los corredores de hábitat restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Selva tropical de tierras bajas y montana intacta en el hotspot de biodiversidad de la Alta Guinea en el sur de Côte d'Ivoire, con acceso a árboles frutales, un dosel cerrado y protección contra la perturbación de la caza.
es.actions: Apoyar el centro de rescate del mono roloway de WAPCA y los esfuerzos anti-furtivismo en el bosque Tanoé-Ehy de Côte d'Ivoire, abogar por la reducción comunitaria de la carne de monte y financiar encuestas sistemáticas del área.

pt.description: O macaco-roloway é um primata impressionante com uma barba branca distintiva e rosto escuro, encontrado nas florestas da Alta Guiné de Côte d'Ivoire e Gana. Os levantamentos sugerem que pode estar funcionalmente extinto em Gana, com apenas algumas populações restantes em Côte d'Ivoire.
pt.endangerment: A caça de carne de caça, considerando os macacos roloway uma iguaria nos mercados locais, devastou as populações em ambos os países; o desmatamento simultâneo para o cultivo de cacau eliminou os corredores de habitat restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical de baixadas e montana intacta no hotspot de biodiversidade da Alta Guiné no sul de Côte d'Ivoire, com acesso a árvores frutíferas, um dossel fechado e proteção contra perturbações de caça.
pt.actions: Apoiar o centro de resgate do macaco roloway da WAPCA e os esforços antipirataria na floresta Tanoé-Ehy de Côte d'Ivoire, defender a redução comunitária da carne de caça e financiar levantamentos sistemáticos da área.

fr.description: Le colobe de Roloway est un primate remarquable à la barbe blanche distinctive et au visage sombre, trouvé dans les forêts de Haute Guinée de Côte d'Ivoire et du Ghana. Les études suggèrent qu'il pourrait être fonctionnellement éteint au Ghana, avec seulement quelques populations restantes en Côte d'Ivoire.
fr.endangerment: La chasse à la viande de brousse, les singes roloway étant considérés comme un mets délicat sur les marchés locaux, a dévasté les populations dans les deux pays ; la déforestation simultanée pour la culture du cacao a éliminé les corridors d'habitat restants.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale humide de plaine et de montagne intacte dans le hotspot de biodiversité de Haute Guinée dans le sud de la Côte d'Ivoire, avec accès à des arbres fruitiers, une canopée fermée et une protection contre les perturbations liées à la chasse.
fr.actions: Soutenir le centre de sauvetage du colobe de Roloway de WAPCA et les efforts anti-braconnage dans la forêt de Tanoé-Ehy en Côte d'Ivoire, plaider pour la réduction de la viande de brousse au niveau communautaire et financer des enquêtes systématiques sur l'aire de répartition.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The pygmy hog is the world's smallest wild pig, barely 25 cm tall and weighing around 8 kg. Once distributed across the Terai grasslands from Nepal through Bhutan to Assam, it now survives only in Manas National Park and a handful of adjacent reserves in Assam, India.
endangerment: The conversion of tall elephant grass and terai swamp habitat to agriculture and human settlement eliminated the species from all but a fraction of its range; annual burning destroys remaining grassland patches.
ecosystemNeeds: Dense stands of tall elephant grass (Saccharum spontaneum and Narenga porphyrocoma) in the terai floodplain landscape of Assam, with undisturbed nesting habitat and seasonal wetland areas.
actions: Support the Pygmy Hog Conservation Programme's captive breeding and reintroduction work in Assam, advocate for controlled burning regimes that preserve tall grass patches in Manas and Orang, and fund habitat corridor restoration.

en.description: The pygmy hog is the world's smallest wild pig, barely 25 cm tall and weighing around 8 kg. Once distributed across the Terai grasslands from Nepal through Bhutan to Assam, it now survives only in Manas National Park and a handful of adjacent reserves in Assam, India.
en.endangerment: The conversion of tall elephant grass and terai swamp habitat to agriculture and human settlement eliminated the species from all but a fraction of its range; annual burning destroys remaining grassland patches.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dense stands of tall elephant grass (Saccharum spontaneum and Narenga porphyrocoma) in the terai floodplain landscape of Assam, with undisturbed nesting habitat and seasonal wetland areas.
en.actions: Support the Pygmy Hog Conservation Programme's captive breeding and reintroduction work in Assam, advocate for controlled burning regimes that preserve tall grass patches in Manas and Orang, and fund habitat corridor restoration.

es.description: El cerdo pigmeo es el cerdo salvaje más pequeño del mundo, con apenas 25 cm de altura y un peso de unos 8 kg. Antes distribuido en los pastizales de Terai desde Nepal a través de Bután hasta Assam, ahora sobrevive solo en el Parque Nacional Manas y un puñado de reservas adyacentes en Assam, India.
es.endangerment: La conversión del hábitat de hierba elefante alta y pantano terai en agricultura y asentamientos humanos eliminó la especie de todo menos una fracción de su área; las quemas anuales destruyen los parches de pastizal restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Densos rodales de hierba elefante alta (Saccharum spontaneum y Narenga porphyrocoma) en el paisaje de llanura aluvial terai de Assam, con hábitat de anidamiento sin perturbaciones y áreas de humedales estacionales.
es.actions: Apoyar el trabajo de cría en cautiverio y reintroducción del Programa de Conservación del Cerdo Pigmeo en Assam, abogar por regímenes de quema controlada que preserven parches de hierba alta en Manas y Orang y financiar la restauración de corredores de hábitat.

pt.description: O porco-pigmeu é o menor porco selvagem do mundo, com apenas 25 cm de altura e pesando cerca de 8 kg. Antes distribuído nas pastagens de Terai do Nepal ao Butão e Assam, agora sobrevive apenas no Parque Nacional Manas e em algumas reservas adjacentes em Assam, Índia.
pt.endangerment: A conversão do habitat de capim-elefante alto e pântano terai em agricultura e assentamentos humanos eliminou a espécie de tudo menos uma fração de sua área; as queimadas anuais destroem os fragmentos de pastagem restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Densos agrupamentos de capim-elefante alto (Saccharum spontaneum e Narenga porphyrocoma) na paisagem de planície aluvial terai de Assam, com habitat de nidificação sem perturbações e áreas de zonas úmidas sazonais.
pt.actions: Apoiar o trabalho de criação em cativeiro e reintrodução do Programa de Conservação do Porco-Pigmeu em Assam, defender regimes de queima controlada que preservem fragmentos de capim alto em Manas e Orang e financiar a restauração de corredores de habitat.

fr.description: Le cochon pygmée est le plus petit cochon sauvage du monde, à peine 25 cm de haut et pesant environ 8 kg. Autrefois réparti dans les prairies du Teraï du Népal au Bhoutan et jusqu'en Assam, il ne survit aujourd'hui que dans le parc national de Manas et une poignée de réserves adjacentes en Assam, en Inde.
fr.endangerment: La conversion de l'habitat de hautes herbes éléphantes et de marais du Teraï en terres agricoles et en colonies humaines a éliminé l'espèce de presque toute son aire ; les brûlis annuels détruisent les parcelles de prairie restantes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Denses peuplements de hautes herbes éléphantes (Saccharum spontaneum et Narenga porphyrocoma) dans le paysage de plaine alluviale du Teraï en Assam, avec un habitat de nidification non perturbé et des zones humides saisonnières.
fr.actions: Soutenir les travaux d'élevage en captivité et de réintroduction du Programme de conservation du cochon pygmée en Assam, plaider pour des régimes de brûlage contrôlé préservant les parcelles de hautes herbes à Manas et Orang et financer la restauration des corridors d'habitat.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Brazilian merganser is a large, serrated-billed diving duck and one of South America's rarest birds. Estimated at fewer than 250 mature individuals, it occupies fast-flowing, clear rivers in the Brazilian Cerrado.
endangerment: Siltation from agriculture, hydroelectric dam construction, and destruction of riparian gallery forest have devastated the clear, fast-flowing rivers this specialist species depends on for feeding and nesting.
ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear, pristine rivers with rocky channels and well-preserved gallery forest in the Brazilian Cerrado and adjacent Atlantic Forest, particularly the São Francisco and Tocantins tributaries in Goiás.
actions: Support ICMBio's Brazilian merganser action plan in Serra da Canastra and Chapada dos Veadeiros, advocate for strict riparian forest protection along Cerrado rivers, and fund dam impact assessments.

en.description: The Brazilian merganser is a large, serrated-billed diving duck and one of South America's rarest birds. Estimated at fewer than 250 mature individuals, it occupies fast-flowing, clear rivers in the Brazilian Cerrado.
en.endangerment: Siltation from agriculture, hydroelectric dam construction, and destruction of riparian gallery forest have devastated the clear, fast-flowing rivers this specialist species depends on for feeding and nesting.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Fast-flowing, clear, pristine rivers with rocky channels and well-preserved gallery forest in the Brazilian Cerrado and adjacent Atlantic Forest, particularly the São Francisco and Tocantins tributaries in Goiás.
en.actions: Support ICMBio's Brazilian merganser action plan in Serra da Canastra and Chapada dos Veadeiros, advocate for strict riparian forest protection along Cerrado rivers, and fund dam impact assessments.

es.description: El mergo brasileño es un gran pato zambullidor de pico serrado y una de las aves más raras de América del Sur. Estimado en menos de 250 individuos maduros, ocupa ríos de rápido flujo y claros en el Cerrado brasileño.
es.endangerment: La sedimentación de la agricultura, la construcción de presas hidroeléctricas y la destrucción del bosque galería ripario han devastado los ríos claros y de rápido flujo de los que depende esta especie especialista para alimentarse y anidar.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Ríos rápidos, claros y prístinos con canales rocosos y bosque galería bien conservado en el Cerrado brasileño y el adyacente Bosque Atlántico, particularmente los afluentes del São Francisco y Tocantins en Goiás.
es.actions: Apoyar el plan de acción del mergo brasileño del ICMBio en Serra da Canastra y Chapada dos Veadeiros, abogar por una estricta protección del bosque ripario a lo largo de los ríos del Cerrado y financiar evaluaciones de impacto de presas.

pt.description: O mergão-brasileiro é um grande pato mergulhador de bico serrilhado e uma das aves mais raras da América do Sul. Estimado em menos de 250 indivíduos maduros, ocupa rios de fluxo rápido e claros no Cerrado brasileiro.
pt.endangerment: O assoreamento pela agricultura, a construção de barragens hidrelétricas e a destruição da floresta galeria ripária devastaram os rios claros e de fluxo rápido dos quais esta espécie especialista depende para se alimentar e nidificar.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Rios rápidos, claros e pristinos com canais rochosos e floresta galeria bem conservada no Cerrado brasileiro e na Mata Atlântica adjacente, particularmente os afluentes do São Francisco e Tocantins em Goiás.
pt.actions: Apoiar o plano de ação do mergão-brasileiro do ICMBio na Serra da Canastra e na Chapada dos Veadeiros, defender a proteção rigorosa da floresta ripária ao longo dos rios do Cerrado e financiar avaliações de impacto de barragens.

fr.description: Le harle du Brésil est un grand canard plongeur à bec dentelé et l'un des oiseaux les plus rares d'Amérique du Sud. Estimé à moins de 250 individus matures, il occupe des rivières au courant rapide et clair dans le Cerrado brésilien.
fr.endangerment: L'envasement dû à l'agriculture, la construction de barrages hydroélectriques et la destruction de la forêt galerie riveraine ont dévasté les rivières claires et à courant rapide dont dépend cette espèce spécialisée pour se nourrir et nicher.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Rivières rapides, claires et vierges avec des chenaux rocheux et une forêt galerie bien préservée dans le Cerrado brésilien et la Forêt Atlantique adjacente, notamment les affluents du São Francisco et du Tocantins dans le Goiás.
fr.actions: Soutenir le plan d'action du harle du Brésil de l'ICMBio à Serra da Canastra et Chapada dos Veadeiros, plaider pour une protection stricte des forêts riveraines le long des rivières du Cerrado et financer des évaluations d'impact des barrages.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The blue-eyed ground dove is an exquisite small dove with iridescent blue eyes, endemic to the Brazilian Cerrado. Believed extinct for 75 years, it was spectacularly rediscovered in 2015 in a tiny fragment of native Cerrado in Minas Gerais.
endangerment: The Brazilian Cerrado has lost more than half its natural vegetation to agriculture; native campo limpo grassland — the specific habitat this dove needs — is among the most threatened vegetation types in South America.
ecosystemNeeds: Open campo limpo grassland with sparse native vegetation in the Brazilian Cerrado, particularly disturbed areas with mixed grass and bare ground where it forages for small seeds at ground level.
actions: Support BirdLife Brazil's campo limpo protection programme in Minas Gerais, advocate for RPPN (private reserve) establishment in the rediscovery area, and fund population monitoring and habitat mapping.

en.description: The blue-eyed ground dove is an exquisite small dove with iridescent blue eyes, endemic to the Brazilian Cerrado. Believed extinct for 75 years, it was spectacularly rediscovered in 2015 in a tiny fragment of native Cerrado in Minas Gerais.
en.endangerment: The Brazilian Cerrado has lost more than half its natural vegetation to agriculture; native campo limpo grassland — the specific habitat this dove needs — is among the most threatened vegetation types in South America.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open campo limpo grassland with sparse native vegetation in the Brazilian Cerrado, particularly disturbed areas with mixed grass and bare ground where it forages for small seeds at ground level.
en.actions: Support BirdLife Brazil's campo limpo protection programme in Minas Gerais, advocate for RPPN (private reserve) establishment in the rediscovery area, and fund population monitoring and habitat mapping.

es.description: La palomita de ojos azules es una exquisita paloma pequeña con ojos azules iridiscentes, endémica del Cerrado brasileño. Considerada extinta durante 75 años, fue espectacularmente redescubierta en 2015 en un diminuto fragmento de Cerrado nativo en Minas Gerais.
es.endangerment: El Cerrado brasileño ha perdido más de la mitad de su vegetación natural en la agricultura; el pastizal nativo de campo limpo —el hábitat específico que esta paloma necesita— es uno de los tipos de vegetación más amenazados en América del Sur.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Pastizal abierto de campo limpo con vegetación nativa escasa en el Cerrado brasileño, particularmente áreas perturbadas con mezcla de hierba y suelo descubierto donde forrajea semillas pequeñas a nivel del suelo.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de protección de campo limpo de BirdLife Brasil en Minas Gerais, abogar por el establecimiento de RPPN (reserva privada) en el área de redescubrimiento y financiar el monitoreo de población y la cartografía de hábitats.

pt.description: A rolinha-dos-olhos-azuis é uma exquisita pombinha com olhos azuis iridescentes, endêmica do Cerrado brasileiro. Considerada extinta por 75 anos, foi espetacularmente redescoberta em 2015 em um minúsculo fragmento de Cerrado nativo em Minas Gerais.
pt.endangerment: O Cerrado brasileiro perdeu mais da metade de sua vegetação natural para a agricultura; a pastagem nativa de campo limpo — o habitat específico que esta pombinha necessita — é um dos tipos de vegetação mais ameaçados da América do Sul.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Pastagem aberta de campo limpo com vegetação nativa esparsa no Cerrado brasileiro, particularmente áreas perturbadas com mistura de grama e solo descoberto onde forrageia pequenas sementes ao nível do solo.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de proteção de campo limpo da BirdLife Brasil em Minas Gerais, defender o estabelecimento de RPPN (reserva particular) na área de redescoberta e financiar o monitoramento populacional e o mapeamento de habitats.

fr.description: La colombe à yeux bleus est une exquise petite colombe aux yeux bleus iridescents, endémique du Cerrado brésilien. Considérée comme éteinte pendant 75 ans, elle a été spectaculairement redécouverte en 2015 dans un minuscule fragment de Cerrado naturel dans le Minas Gerais.
fr.endangerment: Le Cerrado brésilien a perdu plus de la moitié de sa végétation naturelle au profit de l'agriculture ; les prairies indigènes de campo limpo — l'habitat spécifique dont a besoin cette colombe — figurent parmi les types de végétation les plus menacés d'Amérique du Sud.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Prairie ouverte de campo limpo avec une végétation indigène clairsemée dans le Cerrado brésilien, notamment des zones perturbées avec un mélange d'herbes et de sol nu où elle cherche de petites graines au niveau du sol.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de protection du campo limpo de BirdLife Brésil dans le Minas Gerais, plaider pour l'établissement de RPPN (réserve privée) dans la zone de redécouverte et financer le suivi des populations et la cartographie de l'habitat.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Marquesan kingfisher is found only on the island of Tahuata in the Marquesas Islands, French Polynesia. With an estimated population of just 400–600 individuals, it represents one of the Pacific's most threatened forest birds.
endangerment: Introduced cats and rats predate eggs and chicks from nesting cavities; habitat loss from agriculture and invasive vegetation transforms the native coastal and valley forest the species depends on.
ecosystemNeeds: Dry coastal and valley forest on Tahuata island in the Marquesas, particularly areas with large-diameter dead trees containing natural cavities for nesting, and open ground for foraging invertebrates and small lizards.
actions: Support SOP Manu's Marquesan bird programme, fund invasive predator control on Tahuata using trapping and poison stations, and advocate for strict protection of remaining native forest on the island.

en.description: The Marquesan kingfisher is found only on the island of Tahuata in the Marquesas Islands, French Polynesia. With an estimated population of just 400–600 individuals, it represents one of the Pacific's most threatened forest birds.
en.endangerment: Introduced cats and rats predate eggs and chicks from nesting cavities; habitat loss from agriculture and invasive vegetation transforms the native coastal and valley forest the species depends on.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Dry coastal and valley forest on Tahuata island in the Marquesas, particularly areas with large-diameter dead trees containing natural cavities for nesting, and open ground for foraging invertebrates and small lizards.
en.actions: Support SOP Manu's Marquesan bird programme, fund invasive predator control on Tahuata using trapping and poison stations, and advocate for strict protection of remaining native forest on the island.

es.description: El martín pescador de las Marquesas solo se encuentra en la isla de Tahuata en las Islas Marquesas, Polinesia Francesa. Con una población estimada de solo 400-600 individuos, representa una de las aves forestales más amenazadas del Pacífico.
es.endangerment: Los gatos y ratas introducidos depredan huevos y polluelos de las cavidades de anidamiento; la pérdida de hábitat por la agricultura y la vegetación invasora transforma el bosque costero y de valles nativo del que depende la especie.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque costero seco y de valles en la isla Tahuata en las Marquesas, particularmente áreas con árboles muertos de gran diámetro que contienen cavidades naturales para anidar y suelo abierto para forrajear invertebrados y pequeños lagartos.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de aves de las Marquesas de SOP Manu, financiar el control de depredadores invasores en Tahuata usando trampas y estaciones de veneno y abogar por la estricta protección del bosque nativo restante en la isla.

pt.description: O martim-pescador-das-marquesas é encontrado apenas na ilha de Tahuata nas Ilhas Marquesas, Polinésia Francesa. Com uma população estimada de apenas 400-600 indivíduos, representa uma das aves florestais mais ameaçadas do Pacífico.
pt.endangerment: Gatos e ratos introduzidos predam ovos e filhotes das cavidades de nidificação; a perda de habitat pela agricultura e vegetação invasora transforma a floresta costeira e de vales nativa da qual a espécie depende.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta costeira seca e de vales na ilha de Tahuata nas Marquesas, particularmente áreas com árvores mortas de grande diâmetro contendo cavidades naturais para nidificação e terreno aberto para forragear invertebrados e pequenos lagartos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de aves das Marquesas da SOP Manu, financiar o controle de predadores invasores em Tahuata usando armadilhas e estações de veneno e defender a proteção rigorosa da floresta nativa restante na ilha.

fr.description: Le martin-pêcheur des Marquises ne se trouve que sur l'île de Tahuata dans les Îles Marquises, en Polynésie française. Avec une population estimée à seulement 400-600 individus, il représente l'un des oiseaux forestiers les plus menacés du Pacifique.
fr.endangerment: Les chats et rats introduits prédatent les œufs et les poussins dans les cavités de nidification ; la perte d'habitat due à l'agriculture et à la végétation envahissante transforme la forêt côtière et de vallée indigène dont dépend l'espèce.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt côtière sèche et de vallée sur l'île de Tahuata aux Marquises, notamment des zones avec de grands arbres morts contenant des cavités naturelles pour la nidification et des zones ouvertes pour chercher des invertébrés et de petits lézards.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme des oiseaux marquisiens de SOP Manu, financer le contrôle des prédateurs envahissants sur Tahuata en utilisant des pièges et des stations d'appâts empoisonnés et plaider pour une protection stricte de la forêt indigène restante sur l'île.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Negros bleeding-heart is a ground-dwelling pigeon named for the vivid red "blood spot" on its white breast. Restricted to the islands of Negros and Panay in the Philippines, it is among the most threatened birds in Southeast Asia.
endangerment: Deforestation for agriculture has eliminated more than 95% of lowland forest on Negros and Panay; remaining forest is heavily hunted, and the species is also captured for the cagebird trade.
ecosystemNeeds: Intact, undisturbed primary and mature secondary lowland and foothill forest on Negros and Panay, particularly forest with dense undergrowth, leaf litter for foraging, and minimal human disturbance.
actions: Support Haribon Foundation's forest protection and restoration in the North Negros Natural Park, advocate for hunting bans in remaining forest fragments, and fund surveys to locate any remaining populations.

en.description: The Negros bleeding-heart is a ground-dwelling pigeon named for the vivid red "blood spot" on its white breast. Restricted to the islands of Negros and Panay in the Philippines, it is among the most threatened birds in Southeast Asia.
en.endangerment: Deforestation for agriculture has eliminated more than 95% of lowland forest on Negros and Panay; remaining forest is heavily hunted, and the species is also captured for the cagebird trade.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Intact, undisturbed primary and mature secondary lowland and foothill forest on Negros and Panay, particularly forest with dense undergrowth, leaf litter for foraging, and minimal human disturbance.
en.actions: Support Haribon Foundation's forest protection and restoration in the North Negros Natural Park, advocate for hunting bans in remaining forest fragments, and fund surveys to locate any remaining populations.

es.description: La paloma corazón sangrante de Negros es una paloma terrestre llamada así por la vívida 'mancha de sangre' roja en su pecho blanco. Restringida a las islas de Negros y Panay en Filipinas, está entre las aves más amenazadas del sureste de Asia.
es.endangerment: La deforestación para la agricultura ha eliminado más del 95% del bosque de tierras bajas en Negros y Panay; el bosque restante es intensamente cazado, y la especie también es capturada para el comercio de aves en jaula.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque primario y secundario maduro intacto y sin perturbaciones de tierras bajas y estribaciones en Negros y Panay, particularmente bosque con denso sotobosque, hojarasca para forrajeo y mínima perturbación humana.
es.actions: Apoyar la protección y restauración forestal de la Fundación Haribon en el Parque Natural del Norte de Negros, abogar por prohibiciones de caza en los fragmentos forestales restantes y financiar encuestas para localizar las poblaciones restantes.

pt.description: A pomba-coração-sangrante-de-negros é uma pomba terrestre chamada assim pela vívida 'mancha de sangue' vermelha em seu peito branco. Restrita às ilhas de Negros e Panay nas Filipinas, está entre as aves mais ameaçadas do Sudeste Asiático.
pt.endangerment: O desmatamento para a agricultura eliminou mais de 95% da floresta de baixadas em Negros e Panay; a floresta restante é intensamente caçada, e a espécie também é capturada para o comércio de pássaros em gaiola.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta primária e secundária madura intacta e sem perturbações de baixadas e encostas em Negros e Panay, particularmente floresta com sub-bosque denso, serapilheira para forragear e perturbação humana mínima.
pt.actions: Apoiar a proteção e restauração florestal da Fundação Haribon no Parque Natural do Norte de Negros, defender proibições de caça nos fragmentos florestais restantes e financiar levantamentos para localizar as populações restantes.

fr.description: La colombine poignardée de Négros est une colombe terrestre nommée pour la vivante 'tache de sang' rouge sur sa poitrine blanche. Limitée aux îles de Negros et Panay aux Philippines, elle figure parmi les oiseaux les plus menacés d'Asie du Sud-Est.
fr.endangerment: La déforestation pour l'agriculture a éliminé plus de 95% de la forêt de basse altitude à Negros et Panay ; la forêt restante est intensément chassée, et l'espèce est également capturée pour le commerce d'oiseaux en cage.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt primaire et secondaire mature intacte et non perturbée de basse altitude et de piémont à Negros et Panay, notamment des forêts avec un sous-bois dense, de la litière de feuilles pour la recherche de nourriture et une perturbation humaine minimale.
fr.actions: Soutenir la protection et la restauration forestière de la Fondation Haribon dans le parc naturel du nord de Negros, plaider pour des interdictions de chasse dans les fragments forestiers restants et financer des enquêtes pour localiser les populations restantes.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Mississippi gopher frog is a stocky, warty frog that uses the burrows of gopher tortoises for shelter in longleaf pine forests of the US Gulf Coast. Fewer than 200 adults survive at a single natural breeding pond in Harrison County, Mississippi.
endangerment: The near-total loss of longleaf pine savanna and open-canopy forest — less than 3% of the original extent remains — combined with fire suppression that closes canopy over breeding ponds, has driven this species to the edge of extinction.
ecosystemNeeds: Open-canopy longleaf pine savanna in the US Gulf Coastal Plain, with seasonal ephemeral ponds for breeding, sandy soils where gopher tortoises dig burrows for shelter, and regular fire maintaining open ground cover.
actions: Support the US Fish and Wildlife Service's Mississippi gopher frog recovery programme, fund longleaf pine restoration and prescribed burning at Harrison County, and back the head-starting programme releasing young frogs.

en.description: The Mississippi gopher frog is a stocky, warty frog that uses the burrows of gopher tortoises for shelter in longleaf pine forests of the US Gulf Coast. Fewer than 200 adults survive at a single natural breeding pond in Harrison County, Mississippi.
en.endangerment: The near-total loss of longleaf pine savanna and open-canopy forest — less than 3% of the original extent remains — combined with fire suppression that closes canopy over breeding ponds, has driven this species to the edge of extinction.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Open-canopy longleaf pine savanna in the US Gulf Coastal Plain, with seasonal ephemeral ponds for breeding, sandy soils where gopher tortoises dig burrows for shelter, and regular fire maintaining open ground cover.
en.actions: Support the US Fish and Wildlife Service's Mississippi gopher frog recovery programme, fund longleaf pine restoration and prescribed burning at Harrison County, and back the head-starting programme releasing young frogs.

es.description: La rana topo de Mississippi es una rana rechoncha y verrugosa que usa las madrigueras de las tortugas topo para refugiarse en los bosques de pino de hoja larga de la costa del Golfo de EE.UU. Menos de 200 adultos sobreviven en un único estanque de cría natural en el Condado de Harrison, Mississippi.
es.endangerment: La pérdida casi total de la sabana de pino de hoja larga y el bosque de dosel abierto —menos del 3% de la extensión original queda— combinada con la supresión del fuego que cierra el dosel sobre los estanques de cría, ha llevado a esta especie al borde de la extinción.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Sabana de pino de hoja larga de dosel abierto en la Llanura Costera del Golfo de EE.UU., con estanques efímeros estacionales para la cría, suelos arenosos donde las tortugas topo excavan madrigueras para refugio y fuego regular que mantiene una cubierta vegetal abierta.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de recuperación de la rana topo de Mississippi del Servicio de Pesca y Vida Silvestre de EE.UU., financiar la restauración del pino de hoja larga y la quema prescrita en el Condado de Harrison y respaldar el programa de cría inicial que libera ranas jóvenes.

pt.description: A rã-toupeira-do-mississippi é uma rã rechonchuda e verrugosa que usa as tocas das tartarugas-toupeira para abrigo nas florestas de pinheiro-folha-longa da costa do Golfo dos EUA. Menos de 200 adultos sobrevivem em um único lago de reprodução natural no Condado de Harrison, Mississippi.
pt.endangerment: A perda quase total da savana de pinheiro-folha-longa e da floresta de dossel aberto — menos de 3% da extensão original permanece — combinada com a supressão do fogo que fecha o dossel sobre os lagos de reprodução, levou esta espécie à beira da extinção.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Savana de pinheiro-folha-longa de dossel aberto na Planície Costeira do Golfo dos EUA, com lagoas efêmeras sazonais para reprodução, solos arenosos onde as tartarugas-toupeira escavam tocas para abrigo e fogo regular mantendo uma cobertura vegetal aberta.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de recuperação da rã-toupeira-do-mississippi do Serviço de Pesca e Vida Silvestre dos EUA, financiar a restauração do pinheiro-folha-longa e a queima prescrita no Condado de Harrison e apoiar o programa de criação inicial que libera rãs jovens.

fr.description: La grenouille taupe du Mississippi est une grenouille trapue et verruqueuse qui utilise les terriers des tortues-taupes pour s'abriter dans les forêts de pin des marais de la côte du Golfe des États-Unis. Moins de 200 adultes survivent dans un seul étang de reproduction naturel dans le comté de Harrison, Mississippi.
fr.endangerment: La perte presque totale de la savane à pin des marais et de la forêt à canopée ouverte — moins de 3% de l'étendue originelle subsiste — combinée à la suppression des incendies qui ferment la canopée au-dessus des étangs de reproduction, a conduit cette espèce au bord de l'extinction.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Savane à canopée ouverte de pin des marais dans la plaine côtière du Golfe des États-Unis, avec des mares éphémères saisonnières pour la reproduction, des sols sableux où les tortues-taupes creusent des terriers pour l'abri et des incendies réguliers maintenant un couvert végétal ouvert.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de rétablissement de la grenouille taupe du Mississippi du Service des poissons et de la faune sauvage des États-Unis, financer la restauration du pin des marais et le brûlage dirigé dans le comté de Harrison et soutenir le programme d'élevage précoce libérant de jeunes grenouilles.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The purple frog is a blobby, dark-purple amphibian with a tiny pointed head, endemic to the Western Ghats of India. A relic lineage that separated from its closest relatives over 100 million years ago, it lives almost entirely underground, emerging only briefly to breed.
endangerment: Conversion of the Western Ghats' evergreen forests to monoculture plantations — particularly cardamom and coffee — destroys the deep, moist soil habitat where the frog spends 11 months of the year; chytridiomycosis is an emerging threat.
ecosystemNeeds: The deep laterite soil and leaf litter of semi-evergreen and moist deciduous forest in the Western Ghats, particularly at elevations of 100–1,600 metres in Tamil Nadu and Kerala, emerging only during monsoon to breed in streams.
actions: Support field surveys to map purple frog distribution across the Western Ghats, advocate for wildlife-friendly cardamom and coffee farming standards, and fund chytridiomycosis monitoring and antibiotic probiotic trials.

en.description: The purple frog is a blobby, dark-purple amphibian with a tiny pointed head, endemic to the Western Ghats of India. A relic lineage that separated from its closest relatives over 100 million years ago, it lives almost entirely underground, emerging only briefly to breed.
en.endangerment: Conversion of the Western Ghats' evergreen forests to monoculture plantations — particularly cardamom and coffee — destroys the deep, moist soil habitat where the frog spends 11 months of the year; chytridiomycosis is an emerging threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The deep laterite soil and leaf litter of semi-evergreen and moist deciduous forest in the Western Ghats, particularly at elevations of 100–1,600 metres in Tamil Nadu and Kerala, emerging only during monsoon to breed in streams.
en.actions: Support field surveys to map purple frog distribution across the Western Ghats, advocate for wildlife-friendly cardamom and coffee farming standards, and fund chytridiomycosis monitoring and antibiotic probiotic trials.

es.description: La rana púrpura es un anfibio de color púrpura oscuro y aspecto bulboso con una pequeña cabeza puntiaguda, endémico de los Ghats Occidentales de India. Un linaje relicto que se separó de sus parientes más cercanos hace más de 100 millones de años, vive casi enteramente bajo tierra, emergiendo solo brevemente para reproducirse.
es.endangerment: La conversión de los bosques siempreverdes de los Ghats Occidentales en plantaciones de monocultivo —particularmente cardamomo y café— destruye el hábitat de suelo profundo y húmedo donde la rana pasa 11 meses del año; la quitridiomicosis es una amenaza emergente.
es.ecosystemNeeds: El suelo profundo de laterita y la hojarasca del bosque semisiempreverde y caducifolio húmedo en los Ghats Occidentales, particularmente a altitudes de 100-1.600 metros en Tamil Nadu y Kerala, emergiendo solo durante el monzón para reproducirse en arroyos.
es.actions: Apoyar encuestas de campo para mapear la distribución de la rana púrpura en los Ghats Occidentales, abogar por estándares de cultivo de cardamomo y café favorables a la vida silvestre y financiar el monitoreo de la quitridiomicosis y ensayos de probióticos antibióticos.

pt.description: A rã-roxa é um anfíbio de cor roxo-escuro e aparência bulbosa com uma pequena cabeça pontiaguda, endêmico dos Ghats Ocidentais da Índia. Uma linhagem relíqua que se separou de seus parentes mais próximos há mais de 100 milhões de anos, vive quase inteiramente underground, emergindo apenas brevemente para se reproduzir.
pt.endangerment: A conversão das florestas perenes dos Ghats Ocidentais em plantações de monocultura — especialmente cardamomo e café — destrói o habitat de solo profundo e úmido onde a rã passa 11 meses do ano; a quitridiomicose é uma ameaça emergente.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: O solo profundo de laterita e a serapilheira da floresta semi-perene e caducifólia úmida nos Ghats Ocidentais, particularmente em altitudes de 100-1.600 metros em Tamil Nadu e Kerala, emergindo apenas durante a monção para se reproduzir em córregos.
pt.actions: Apoiar levantamentos de campo para mapear a distribuição da rã-roxa nos Ghats Ocidentais, defender padrões de cultivo de cardamomo e café favoráveis à vida silvestre e financiar o monitoramento da quitridiomicose e ensaios probióticos antibióticos.

fr.description: La grenouille violette est un amphibien de couleur violet foncé et d'apparence bulbeuse à la petite tête pointue, endémique des Ghats occidentaux de l'Inde. Une lignée relique qui s'est séparée de ses plus proches parents il y a plus de 100 millions d'années, elle vit presque entièrement sous terre, n'émergeant que brièvement pour se reproduire.
fr.endangerment: La conversion des forêts sempervirentes des Ghats occidentaux en plantations de monoculture — notamment de cardamome et de café — détruit l'habitat de sol profond et humide où la grenouille passe 11 mois de l'année ; la chytridiomycose est une menace émergente.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Le sol de latérite profond et la litière de feuilles de la forêt semi-sempervirente et décidue humide dans les Ghats occidentaux, notamment à des altitudes de 100-1 600 mètres au Tamil Nadu et au Kerala, n'émergeant que pendant la mousson pour se reproduire dans les ruisseaux.
fr.actions: Soutenir des enquêtes de terrain pour cartographier la distribution de la grenouille violette dans les Ghats occidentaux, plaider pour des normes de culture de cardamome et de café favorables à la faune et financer la surveillance de la chytridiomycose et les essais de probiotiques antibiotiques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Jackson's climbing salamander is a brilliantly coloured yellow-and-black amphibian that was known only from museum specimens for 42 years until its rediscovery on cloud forest trees in the Cuchumatanes Mountains of Guatemala in 2017.
endangerment: The cloud forest of the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in Huehuetenango is under intense pressure from illegal logging and slash-and-burn agriculture; chytridiomycosis threatens remaining populations.
ecosystemNeeds: Humid cloud forest at 2,600–2,850 metres in the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in northwestern Guatemala, spending its time in the leaf litter and on mossy tree trunks in undisturbed mature forest.
actions: Support FUNDAECO's Cuchumatanes cloud forest protection programme in Guatemala, fund anti-chytrid probiotic research, and advocate for legal designation of Cuchumatanes cloud forest as a protected area.

en.description: Jackson's climbing salamander is a brilliantly coloured yellow-and-black amphibian that was known only from museum specimens for 42 years until its rediscovery on cloud forest trees in the Cuchumatanes Mountains of Guatemala in 2017.
en.endangerment: The cloud forest of the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in Huehuetenango is under intense pressure from illegal logging and slash-and-burn agriculture; chytridiomycosis threatens remaining populations.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Humid cloud forest at 2,600–2,850 metres in the Sierra de los Cuchumatanes in northwestern Guatemala, spending its time in the leaf litter and on mossy tree trunks in undisturbed mature forest.
en.actions: Support FUNDAECO's Cuchumatanes cloud forest protection programme in Guatemala, fund anti-chytrid probiotic research, and advocate for legal designation of Cuchumatanes cloud forest as a protected area.

es.description: La salamandra trepadora de Jackson es un anfibio de color brillante amarillo y negro que solo era conocido por especímenes de museo durante 42 años hasta su redescubrimiento en los árboles de los bosques nubosos de las Montañas Cuchumatanes de Guatemala en 2017.
es.endangerment: El bosque nuboso de la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes en Huehuetenango está bajo intensa presión de la tala ilegal y la agricultura de tala y quema; la quitridiomicosis amenaza a las poblaciones restantes.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque nuboso húmedo a 2.600-2.850 metros en la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes en el noroeste de Guatemala, pasando su tiempo en la hojarasca y en los troncos musgosos de los árboles en bosques maduros sin perturbaciones.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de protección del bosque nuboso de los Cuchumatanes de FUNDAECO en Guatemala, financiar investigaciones de probióticos anti-quitridio y abogar por la designación legal del bosque nuboso de los Cuchumatanes como área protegida.

pt.description: A salamandra-trepadora-de-jackson é um anfíbio de cores brilhantes amarelo e preto que era conhecido apenas por espécimes de museu por 42 anos até sua redescoberta nas árvores das florestas nebulares das Montanhas Cuchumatanes da Guatemala em 2017.
pt.endangerment: A floresta nebular da Sierra de los Cuchumatanes em Huehuetenango está sob intensa pressão do desmatamento ilegal e da agricultura de corte e queima; a quitridiomicose ameaça as populações restantes.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta nebular úmida a 2.600-2.850 metros na Sierra de los Cuchumatanes no noroeste da Guatemala, passando seu tempo na serapilheira e nos troncos musgosos das árvores em florestas maduras sem perturbações.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de proteção da floresta nebular dos Cuchumatanes da FUNDAECO na Guatemala, financiar pesquisas de probióticos anti-quitrídio e defender a designação legal da floresta nebular dos Cuchumatanes como área protegida.

fr.description: La salamandre grimpante de Jackson est un amphibien brillamment coloré en jaune et noir qui n'était connu que par des spécimens de musée pendant 42 ans jusqu'à sa redécouverte dans les arbres de la forêt nuageuse des montagnes Cuchumatanes du Guatemala en 2017.
fr.endangerment: La forêt nuageuse de la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes à Huehuetenango est sous une intense pression de l'exploitation forestière illégale et de l'agriculture sur brûlis ; la chytridiomycose menace les populations restantes.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt nuageuse humide à 2 600-2 850 mètres dans la Sierra de los Cuchumatanes dans le nord-ouest du Guatemala, passant son temps dans la litière et sur les troncs d'arbres moussus dans des forêts matures non perturbées.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de protection des forêts nuageuses des Cuchumatanes de FUNDAECO au Guatemala, financer des recherches sur les probiotiques anti-chytride et plaider pour la désignation légale de la forêt nuageuse des Cuchumatanes comme zone protégée.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Yangtze giant softshell turtle is the largest freshwater turtle on Earth, with a shell over a metre long. With fewer than four confirmed living individuals — including one female artificially inseminated in 2019 — it is among the most critically endangered animals on the planet.
endangerment: Hunting for food and traditional medicine, sand dredging, pollution, and dam construction across the Yangtze and Red River systems destroyed this turtle's habitat and population over the 20th century; the last confirmed wild female died in 2019.
ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep rivers and lakes in the Yangtze Valley in China and Red River drainage in Vietnam, with sandy beaches for nesting, deep pools for feeding on fish and molluscs, and minimal boat traffic and pollution.
actions: Support the Turtle Survival Alliance's emergency captive breeding programme in China and Vietnam, advocate for environmental DNA surveys of the Red River system in Vietnam for any surviving individuals.

en.description: The Yangtze giant softshell turtle is the largest freshwater turtle on Earth, with a shell over a metre long. With fewer than four confirmed living individuals — including one female artificially inseminated in 2019 — it is among the most critically endangered animals on the planet.
en.endangerment: Hunting for food and traditional medicine, sand dredging, pollution, and dam construction across the Yangtze and Red River systems destroyed this turtle's habitat and population over the 20th century; the last confirmed wild female died in 2019.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep rivers and lakes in the Yangtze Valley in China and Red River drainage in Vietnam, with sandy beaches for nesting, deep pools for feeding on fish and molluscs, and minimal boat traffic and pollution.
en.actions: Support the Turtle Survival Alliance's emergency captive breeding programme in China and Vietnam, advocate for environmental DNA surveys of the Red River system in Vietnam for any surviving individuals.

es.description: La tortuga de caparazón blando gigante del Yangtze es la tortuga de agua dulce más grande de la Tierra, con un caparazón de más de un metro de largo. Con menos de cuatro individuos vivos confirmados —incluyendo una hembra inseminada artificialmente en 2019— está entre los animales más críticamente amenazados del planeta.
es.endangerment: La caza para alimento y medicina tradicional, el dragado de arena, la contaminación y la construcción de presas en los sistemas del Yangtze y el Río Rojo destruyeron el hábitat y la población de esta tortuga durante el siglo XX; la última hembra silvestre confirmada murió en 2019.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes ríos profundos y lagos en el Valle del Yangtze en China y la cuenca del Río Rojo en Vietnam, con playas arenosas para anidar, pozas profundas para alimentarse de peces y moluscos y mínimo tráfico de barcos y contaminación.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría de emergencia en cautiverio de la Turtle Survival Alliance en China y Vietnam, abogar por encuestas de ADN ambiental del sistema del Río Rojo en Vietnam para cualquier individuo superviviente.

pt.description: A tartaruga-de-casca-mole-gigante-do-yangtze é a maior tartaruga de água doce da Terra, com um casco de mais de um metro de comprimento. Com menos de quatro indivíduos vivos confirmados — incluindo uma fêmea inseminada artificialmente em 2019 — está entre os animais mais criticamente ameaçados do planeta.
pt.endangerment: A caça para alimento e medicina tradicional, a dragagem de areia, a poluição e a construção de barragens nos sistemas do Yangtze e do Rio Vermelho destruíram o habitat e a população desta tartaruga ao longo do século XX; a última fêmea selvagem confirmada morreu em 2019.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rios profundos e lagos no Vale do Yangtze na China e na bacia do Rio Vermelho no Vietnã, com praias arenosas para nidificação, poços profundos para se alimentar de peixes e moluscos e tráfego mínimo de barcos e poluição.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro de emergência da Turtle Survival Alliance na China e no Vietnã, defender levantamentos de DNA ambiental do sistema do Rio Vermelho no Vietnã para qualquer indivíduo sobrevivente.

fr.description: La tortue géante à carapace molle du Yangtsé est la plus grande tortue d'eau douce de la Terre, avec une carapace de plus d'un mètre de long. Avec moins de quatre individus vivants confirmés — dont une femelle inséminée artificiellement en 2019 — elle figure parmi les animaux les plus gravement menacés de la planète.
fr.endangerment: La chasse pour la nourriture et la médecine traditionnelle, le dragage du sable, la pollution et la construction de barrages sur les systèmes du Yangtsé et du fleuve Rouge ont détruit l'habitat et la population de cette tortue au cours du XXe siècle ; la dernière femelle sauvage confirmée est morte en 2019.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rivières profondes et lacs dans la vallée du Yangtsé en Chine et le bassin du fleuve Rouge au Vietnam, avec des plages sablonneuses pour la nidification, des fosses profondes pour se nourrir de poissons et de mollusques et un trafic de bateaux et une pollution minimaux.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage d'urgence en captivité de la Turtle Survival Alliance en Chine et au Vietnam, plaider pour des enquêtes d'ADN environnemental du système du fleuve Rouge au Vietnam pour tout individu survivant.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The spider tortoise is a small, attractively patterned tortoise endemic to the spiny dry forests of southwestern Madagascar. It can retract into its hinged shell and aestivate for months during drought, but its numbers have collapsed in recent decades.
endangerment: Poaching for the illegal international pet trade combined with habitat destruction from charcoal production and slash-and-burn agriculture in Madagascar's spiny forest has caused severe population declines.
ecosystemNeeds: Spiny dry deciduous forest and thicket in the Tulear and Toliara regions of southwestern Madagascar, with sandy substrate for burrowing during aestivation, dry leaf litter for foraging, and succulent plants for water.
actions: Support the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust's southern Madagascar programme, advocate for CITES enforcement and strengthened penalties for tortoise trafficking, and fund community patrol networks in spiny forest.

en.description: The spider tortoise is a small, attractively patterned tortoise endemic to the spiny dry forests of southwestern Madagascar. It can retract into its hinged shell and aestivate for months during drought, but its numbers have collapsed in recent decades.
en.endangerment: Poaching for the illegal international pet trade combined with habitat destruction from charcoal production and slash-and-burn agriculture in Madagascar's spiny forest has caused severe population declines.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Spiny dry deciduous forest and thicket in the Tulear and Toliara regions of southwestern Madagascar, with sandy substrate for burrowing during aestivation, dry leaf litter for foraging, and succulent plants for water.
en.actions: Support the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust's southern Madagascar programme, advocate for CITES enforcement and strengthened penalties for tortoise trafficking, and fund community patrol networks in spiny forest.

es.description: La tortuga araña es una pequeña tortuga de patrones atractivos endémica de los espinosos bosques secos del suroeste de Madagascar. Puede retraerse en su caparazón articulado y estivarse durante meses durante la sequía, pero sus números han colapsado en las últimas décadas.
es.endangerment: La caza furtiva para el comercio internacional ilegal de mascotas combinada con la destrucción del hábitat por la producción de carbón vegetal y la agricultura de tala y quema en el bosque espinoso de Madagascar ha causado graves declives de población.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque caducifolio seco y espinoso en las regiones de Tulear y Toliara del suroeste de Madagascar, con sustrato arenoso para excavar durante la estivación, hojarasca seca para forrajear y plantas suculentas para el agua.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa del suroeste de Madagascar del Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust, abogar por la aplicación de la CITES y el fortalecimiento de las penas por el tráfico de tortugas y financiar redes de patrullas comunitarias en el bosque espinoso.

pt.description: A tartaruga-aranha é uma pequena tartaruga de padrões atraentes endêmica das florestas espinhosas secas do sudoeste de Madagascar. Pode se recolher em seu casco articulado e estivar por meses durante a seca, mas seus números colapsaram nas últimas décadas.
pt.endangerment: A caça furtiva para o comércio internacional ilegal de animais de estimação combinada com a destruição do habitat pela produção de carvão vegetal e pela agricultura de corte e queima na floresta espinhosa de Madagascar causou graves declínios populacionais.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta caducifólia seca e espinhosa nas regiões de Tulear e Toliara do sudoeste de Madagascar, com substrato arenoso para escavar durante a estivação, serapilheira seca para forragear e plantas suculentas para água.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa do sudoeste de Madagascar do Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust, defender a aplicação da CITES e o fortalecimento das penalidades para o tráfico de tartarugas e financiar redes de patrulha comunitária na floresta espinhosa.

fr.description: La tortue araignée est une petite tortue aux motifs attrayants endémique des forêts sèches épineuses du sud-ouest de Madagascar. Elle peut se rétracter dans sa carapace articulée et s'estiver pendant des mois en période de sécheresse, mais ses effectifs se sont effondrés au cours des dernières décennies.
fr.endangerment: Le braconnage pour le commerce international illégal d'animaux de compagnie, combiné à la destruction de l'habitat par la production de charbon de bois et l'agriculture sur brûlis dans la forêt épineuse de Madagascar, a provoqué de graves déclins de population.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt sèche épineuse caduque dans les régions de Tulear et Toliara dans le sud-ouest de Madagascar, avec un substrat sableux pour s'enfouir pendant l'estivation, de la litière sèche de feuilles pour se nourrir et des plantes succulentes pour l'eau.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme du sud-ouest de Madagascar du Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust, plaider pour l'application de la CITES et le renforcement des sanctions contre le trafic de tortues et financer des réseaux de patrouilles communautaires dans la forêt épineuse.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Jamaican boa is Jamaica's largest terrestrial predator, a yellow-and-black constrictor reaching two metres. It controls rodent populations and pollinates flowers by serving as a nocturnal pollinator for certain plants. Persecution, road kills, and habitat loss have made it rare.
endangerment: Deliberate killing by humans who fear or dislike snakes, combined with predation of eggs and young by introduced mongooses and rats, and forest clearing for agriculture have severely reduced populations across Jamaica.
ecosystemNeeds: Native dry and wet limestone forest across Jamaica, particularly forest associated with limestone caves where it hunts birds and bats, large trees for roosting, and rocky areas for thermoregulation.
actions: Support Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust's boa education programme, advocate for legal protection against deliberate killing, and fund mongoose control in key boa habitat areas.

en.description: The Jamaican boa is Jamaica's largest terrestrial predator, a yellow-and-black constrictor reaching two metres. It controls rodent populations and pollinates flowers by serving as a nocturnal pollinator for certain plants. Persecution, road kills, and habitat loss have made it rare.
en.endangerment: Deliberate killing by humans who fear or dislike snakes, combined with predation of eggs and young by introduced mongooses and rats, and forest clearing for agriculture have severely reduced populations across Jamaica.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Native dry and wet limestone forest across Jamaica, particularly forest associated with limestone caves where it hunts birds and bats, large trees for roosting, and rocky areas for thermoregulation.
en.actions: Support Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust's boa education programme, advocate for legal protection against deliberate killing, and fund mongoose control in key boa habitat areas.

es.description: La boa jamaicana es el mayor depredador terrestre de Jamaica, un constrictor amarillo y negro que alcanza dos metros. Controla las poblaciones de roedores y poliniza flores sirviendo como polinizador nocturno de ciertas plantas. La persecución, las muertes por carretera y la pérdida de hábitat la han hecho escasa.
es.endangerment: La matanza deliberada por humanos que temen o no les gustan las serpientes, combinada con la depredación de huevos y crías por mangostas y ratas introducidas, y la tala forestal para la agricultura han reducido severamente las poblaciones en toda Jamaica.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosques nativos de piedra caliza seca y húmeda en toda Jamaica, particularmente bosque asociado con cuevas de piedra caliza donde caza aves y murciélagos, grandes árboles para descansar y áreas rocosas para la termorregulación.
