es.actions: Apoyar el programa educativo sobre boas del Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust, abogar por la protección legal contra la matanza deliberada y financiar el control de mangostas en las áreas clave de hábitat de boas.

pt.description: A jiboia-jamaicana é o maior predador terrestre da Jamaica, um construtor amarelo e preto que atinge dois metros. Controla as populações de roedores e poliniza flores servindo como polinizador noturno de certas plantas. A perseguição, as mortes na estrada e a perda de habitat a tornaram rara.
pt.endangerment: A matança deliberada por humanos que temem ou não gostam de cobras, combinada com a predação de ovos e filhotes por mangusto e ratos introduzidos, e o desmatamento para agricultura reduziram severamente as populações em toda a Jamaica.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Florestas nativas de calcário seco e úmido em toda a Jamaica, particularmente floresta associada a cavernas de calcário onde caça pássaros e morcegos, grandes árvores para descanso e áreas rochosas para termorregulação.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa educacional sobre jiboias do Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust, defender a proteção legal contra a matança deliberada e financiar o controle do mangusto nas principais áreas de habitat da jiboia.

fr.description: Le boa de Jamaïque est le plus grand prédateur terrestre de la Jamaïque, un constricteur jaune et noir atteignant deux mètres. Il contrôle les populations de rongeurs et pollinise les fleurs en servant de pollinisateur nocturne pour certaines plantes. La persécution, les accidents de la route et la perte d'habitat l'ont rendu rare.
fr.endangerment: Le meurtre délibéré par des humains qui craignent ou n'aiment pas les serpents, combiné à la prédation des œufs et des jeunes par des mangoustes et des rats introduits, et le défrichement des forêts pour l'agriculture ont sévèrement réduit les populations à travers la Jamaïque.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêts calcaires sèches et humides indigènes à travers la Jamaïque, notamment les forêts associées aux grottes calcaires où il chasse les oiseaux et les chauves-souris, les grands arbres pour se reposer et les zones rocheuses pour la thermorégulation.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme éducatif sur les boas du Jamaica Conservation and Development Trust, plaider pour une protection juridique contre le meurtre délibéré et financer le contrôle des mangoustes dans les zones clés d'habitat des boas.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The four-eyed turtle gets its name from the two large, eye-like ocelli on the back of its head that may confuse predators. Native to mountainous streams of southern China and northern Vietnam, it is among the region's most traded turtles.
endangerment: Intensive collection for Chinese food and traditional medicine markets has devastated populations; habitat degradation from dam construction, sand mining, and stream channelisation has further reduced available habitat.
ecosystemNeeds: Clear, fast-flowing, rocky mountain streams in Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan, and Fujian in China, and northern Vietnam, with bankside vegetation and pools deep enough for foraging on aquatic invertebrates and small fish.
actions: Advocate for China's wildlife protection law enforcement against turtle markets, support WCS's turtle protection programme in southern China, and fund captive breeding populations as insurance against wild extinction.

en.description: The four-eyed turtle gets its name from the two large, eye-like ocelli on the back of its head that may confuse predators. Native to mountainous streams of southern China and northern Vietnam, it is among the region's most traded turtles.
en.endangerment: Intensive collection for Chinese food and traditional medicine markets has devastated populations; habitat degradation from dam construction, sand mining, and stream channelisation has further reduced available habitat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Clear, fast-flowing, rocky mountain streams in Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan, and Fujian in China, and northern Vietnam, with bankside vegetation and pools deep enough for foraging on aquatic invertebrates and small fish.
en.actions: Advocate for China's wildlife protection law enforcement against turtle markets, support WCS's turtle protection programme in southern China, and fund captive breeding populations as insurance against wild extinction.

es.description: La tortuga de cuatro ojos obtiene su nombre de los dos grandes ocelos en forma de ojo en la parte posterior de su cabeza que pueden confundir a los depredadores. Nativa de los arroyos de montaña del sur de China y el norte de Vietnam, está entre las tortugas más comercializadas de la región.
es.endangerment: La recolección intensiva para los mercados chinos de alimentos y medicina tradicional ha devastado las poblaciones; la degradación del hábitat por la construcción de presas, la minería de arena y la canalización de arroyos ha reducido aún más el hábitat disponible.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Arroyos de montaña rocosos, claros y de rápido flujo en Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan y Fujian en China, y el norte de Vietnam, con vegetación en las orillas y pozas lo suficientemente profundas para forrajear invertebrados acuáticos y peces pequeños.
es.actions: Abogar por la aplicación de la ley de protección de vida silvestre de China contra los mercados de tortugas, apoyar el programa de protección de tortugas de WCS en el sur de China y financiar poblaciones de cría en cautiverio como seguro contra la extinción silvestre.

pt.description: A tartaruga-de-quatro-olhos recebe seu nome dos dois grandes ocelos em forma de olho na parte posterior de sua cabeça que podem confundir os predadores. Nativa dos córregos montanhosos do sul da China e norte do Vietnã, está entre as tartarugas mais comercializadas da região.
pt.endangerment: A coleta intensiva para os mercados chineses de alimentos e medicina tradicional devastou as populações; a degradação do habitat pela construção de barragens, mineração de areia e canalização de córregos reduziu ainda mais o habitat disponível.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Córregos de montanha rochosos, claros e de fluxo rápido em Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan e Fujian na China, e norte do Vietnã, com vegetação nas margens e poças profundas o suficiente para forragear invertebrados aquáticos e peixes pequenos.
pt.actions: Defender a aplicação da lei de proteção à vida silvestre da China contra os mercados de tartarugas, apoiar o programa de proteção de tartarugas do WCS no sul da China e financiar populações de criação em cativeiro como seguro contra a extinção selvagem.

fr.description: La tortue à quatre yeux doit son nom aux deux grands ocelles en forme d'yeux à l'arrière de sa tête qui peuvent dérouter les prédateurs. Originaire des ruisseaux de montagne du sud de la Chine et du nord du Vietnam, elle figure parmi les tortues les plus commercialisées de la région.
fr.endangerment: La collecte intensive pour les marchés alimentaires chinois et la médecine traditionnelle a dévasté les populations ; la dégradation de l'habitat due à la construction de barrages, à l'extraction de sable et à la canalisation des ruisseaux a encore réduit l'habitat disponible.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Ruisseaux de montagne rocheux, clairs et à courant rapide dans le Guangdong, le Guangxi, le Hainan et le Fujian en Chine, et dans le nord du Vietnam, avec une végétation en bord de berge et des mares suffisamment profondes pour chercher des invertébrés aquatiques et de petits poissons.
fr.actions: Plaider pour l'application de la loi chinoise sur la protection de la faune contre les marchés aux tortues, soutenir le programme de protection des tortues du WCS dans le sud de la Chine et financer des populations d'élevage en captivité comme assurance contre l'extinction sauvage.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The spotted handfish is a remarkable fish that "walks" across the seafloor on modified pectoral fins rather than swimming. Endemic to the lower Derwent Estuary in Tasmania, Australia, it is one of the world's most endangered marine fish.
endangerment: Introduction of the Pacific seastar has devastated populations by consuming the stalked ascidians the handfish uses as nest supports; siltation, pollution, and habitat degradation in the Derwent Estuary compound the threat.
ecosystemNeeds: Sandy and silty seafloor of the lower Derwent River Estuary near Hobart, Tasmania, at depths of 1–30 metres, with stalked ascidians used as nest supports and abundant invertebrate prey.
actions: Support CSIRO's spotted handfish captive breeding programme, fund removal of invasive Pacific seastars from handfish habitat in the Derwent, and advocate for strict pollution controls in the Hobart urban estuary.

en.description: The spotted handfish is a remarkable fish that "walks" across the seafloor on modified pectoral fins rather than swimming. Endemic to the lower Derwent Estuary in Tasmania, Australia, it is one of the world's most endangered marine fish.
en.endangerment: Introduction of the Pacific seastar has devastated populations by consuming the stalked ascidians the handfish uses as nest supports; siltation, pollution, and habitat degradation in the Derwent Estuary compound the threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Sandy and silty seafloor of the lower Derwent River Estuary near Hobart, Tasmania, at depths of 1–30 metres, with stalked ascidians used as nest supports and abundant invertebrate prey.
en.actions: Support CSIRO's spotted handfish captive breeding programme, fund removal of invasive Pacific seastars from handfish habitat in the Derwent, and advocate for strict pollution controls in the Hobart urban estuary.

es.description: El pez mano manchado es un pez notable que 'camina' por el fondo del mar sobre aletas pectorales modificadas en lugar de nadar. Endémico del estuario bajo del Río Derwent en Tasmania, Australia, es uno de los peces marinos más amenazados del mundo.
es.endangerment: La introducción de la estrella de mar del Pacífico ha devastado las poblaciones al consumir las ascidias pedunculadas que el pez mano usa como soportes de nido; la sedimentación, la contaminación y la degradación del hábitat en el estuario Derwent agravan la amenaza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Fondo marino arenoso y limoso del estuario bajo del Río Derwent cerca de Hobart, Tasmania, a profundidades de 1-30 metros, con ascidias pedunculadas usadas como soportes de nido y abundante presa de invertebrados.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría en cautiverio del pez mano manchado del CSIRO, financiar la eliminación de estrellas de mar del Pacífico invasoras del hábitat del pez mano en el Derwent y abogar por estrictos controles de contaminación en el estuario urbano de Hobart.

pt.description: O peixe-mão-manchado é um peixe notável que 'caminha' pelo fundo do mar sobre nadadeiras peitorais modificadas em vez de nadar. Endêmico do estuário inferior do Rio Derwent na Tasmânia, Austrália, é um dos peixes marinhos mais ameaçados do mundo.
pt.endangerment: A introdução da estrela-do-mar-do-pacífico devastou as populações ao consumir as ascídias pedunculadas que o peixe-mão usa como suportes de ninho; o assoreamento, a poluição e a degradação do habitat no estuário Derwent agravam a ameaça.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Fundo marinho arenoso e lodoso do estuário inferior do Rio Derwent perto de Hobart, Tasmânia, a profundidades de 1-30 metros, com ascídias pedunculadas usadas como suportes de ninho e abundante presa de invertebrados.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação em cativeiro do peixe-mão-manchado do CSIRO, financiar a remoção de estrelas-do-mar-do-pacífico invasoras do habitat do peixe-mão no Derwent e defender controles rigorosos de poluição no estuário urbano de Hobart.

fr.description: Le poisson-main tacheté est un poisson remarquable qui 'marche' sur le fond marin sur des nageoires pectorales modifiées plutôt que de nager. Endémique du bas estuaire de la rivière Derwent en Tasmanie, en Australie, c'est l'un des poissons marins les plus menacés au monde.
fr.endangerment: L'introduction de l'étoile de mer du Pacifique a dévasté les populations en consommant les ascidies pédonculées que le poisson-main utilise comme supports de nid ; l'envasement, la pollution et la dégradation de l'habitat dans l'estuaire Derwent aggravent la menace.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Fond marin sableux et limoneux du bas estuaire de la rivière Derwent près de Hobart, Tasmanie, à des profondeurs de 1-30 mètres, avec des ascidies pédonculées utilisées comme supports de nid et des proies invertébrées abondantes.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage en captivité du poisson-main tacheté du CSIRO, financer la suppression des étoiles de mer du Pacifique envahissantes de l'habitat du poisson-main dans le Derwent et plaider pour des contrôles stricts de la pollution dans l'estuaire urbain de Hobart.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Siamese tiger perch is a large, boldly striped freshwater fish from the Chao Phraya and Mekong river systems in Thailand. Prized by aquarium collectors and as a food fish, it has virtually vanished from its native rivers.
endangerment: Overfishing for food and the aquarium trade, dam construction blocking migration routes, and severe water pollution in Thai river systems have reduced populations to near extinction in the wild.
ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep, slow-flowing freshwater rivers with complex structure including submerged debris and rocky substrate in the Chao Phraya and Mekong basins of Thailand and Cambodia, at mid-water depth for ambush predation.
actions: Support Thai fisheries department captive breeding reintroduction work, advocate for stricter aquarium trade regulations, and fund environmental DNA surveys in Thai rivers to detect any remaining wild populations.

en.description: The Siamese tiger perch is a large, boldly striped freshwater fish from the Chao Phraya and Mekong river systems in Thailand. Prized by aquarium collectors and as a food fish, it has virtually vanished from its native rivers.
en.endangerment: Overfishing for food and the aquarium trade, dam construction blocking migration routes, and severe water pollution in Thai river systems have reduced populations to near extinction in the wild.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Large, deep, slow-flowing freshwater rivers with complex structure including submerged debris and rocky substrate in the Chao Phraya and Mekong basins of Thailand and Cambodia, at mid-water depth for ambush predation.
en.actions: Support Thai fisheries department captive breeding reintroduction work, advocate for stricter aquarium trade regulations, and fund environmental DNA surveys in Thai rivers to detect any remaining wild populations.

es.description: La perca tigre siamesa es un gran pez de agua dulce con llamativas rayas de los sistemas fluviales Chao Phraya y Mekong en Tailandia. Apreciado por los coleccionistas de acuarios y como pez alimenticio, ha desaparecido prácticamente de sus ríos nativos.
es.endangerment: La pesca excesiva para alimento y el comercio de acuarios, la construcción de presas que bloquean las rutas de migración y la severa contaminación del agua en los sistemas fluviales tailandeses han reducido las poblaciones casi a la extinción en la naturaleza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes ríos de agua dulce de movimiento lento y profundo con estructura compleja incluyendo escombros sumergidos y sustrato rocoso en las cuencas del Chao Phraya y Mekong de Tailandia y Camboya, a profundidad media del agua para la depredación de emboscada.
es.actions: Apoyar el trabajo de reintroducción de cría en cautiverio del departamento de pesca tailandés, abogar por regulaciones más estrictas del comercio de acuarios y financiar encuestas de ADN ambiental en los ríos tailandeses para detectar cualquier población silvestre restante.

pt.description: A perca-tigre-siamesa é um grande peixe de água doce com listras chamativas dos sistemas fluviais Chao Phraya e Mekong na Tailândia. Apreciado por colecionadores de aquário e como peixe alimentar, praticamente desapareceu de seus rios nativos.
pt.endangerment: A pesca excessiva para alimento e o comércio de aquário, a construção de barragens bloqueando rotas migratórias e a severa poluição da água nos sistemas fluviais tailandeses reduziram as populações quase à extinção na natureza.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rios de água doce de movimento lento e profundos com estrutura complexa incluindo detritos submersos e substrato rochoso nas bacias do Chao Phraya e Mekong da Tailândia e Camboja, a profundidade média da água para predação de emboscada.
pt.actions: Apoiar o trabalho de reintrodução de criação em cativeiro do departamento de pesca tailandês, defender regulamentações mais rigorosas do comércio de aquário e financiar levantamentos de DNA ambiental nos rios tailandeses para detectar qualquer população selvagem restante.

fr.description: La perche tigre siamoise est un grand poisson d'eau douce aux rayures frappantes des systèmes fluviaux Chao Phraya et Mékong en Thaïlande. Prisée par les collectionneurs d'aquarium et comme poisson alimentaire, elle a pratiquement disparu de ses rivières natales.
fr.endangerment: La surpêche pour la nourriture et le commerce d'aquarium, la construction de barrages bloquant les voies migratoires et la grave pollution de l'eau dans les systèmes fluviaux thaïlandais ont réduit les populations à la quasi-extinction à l'état sauvage.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Grandes rivières d'eau douce à courant lent et profondes avec une structure complexe comprenant des débris submergés et des substrats rocheux dans les bassins du Chao Phraya et du Mékong en Thaïlande et au Cambodge, à mi-profondeur pour la prédation en embuscade.
fr.actions: Soutenir les travaux de réintroduction par élevage en captivité du département des pêches thaïlandais, plaider pour des réglementations plus strictes du commerce d'aquarium et financer des enquêtes d'ADN environnemental dans les rivières thaïlandaises pour détecter les populations sauvages restantes.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Brazilian guitarfish is a large elasmobranch with a body shaped between a shark and a ray, once abundant in the warm shallow coastal waters of southern Brazil and Uruguay. Industrial fishing has reduced it by more than 80% in recent decades.
endangerment: High-value bycatch in shrimp trawls and longline fisheries, combined with targeted fishing for its large fins, has devastated populations; the shallow estuaries and nursery areas it needs for pups are heavily impacted by coastal development.
ecosystemNeeds: Shallow coastal waters, estuaries, and sandy bays along the Atlantic coast of southern Brazil and Uruguay, with seagrass beds as nursery habitat for pups and abundant benthic invertebrates and small fish for adults.
actions: Support WWF-Brazil's marine elasmobranch programme, advocate for guitarfish-specific bycatch reduction measures in Brazilian shrimp fisheries, and fund population surveys along the southern Brazilian coast.

en.description: The Brazilian guitarfish is a large elasmobranch with a body shaped between a shark and a ray, once abundant in the warm shallow coastal waters of southern Brazil and Uruguay. Industrial fishing has reduced it by more than 80% in recent decades.
en.endangerment: High-value bycatch in shrimp trawls and longline fisheries, combined with targeted fishing for its large fins, has devastated populations; the shallow estuaries and nursery areas it needs for pups are heavily impacted by coastal development.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Shallow coastal waters, estuaries, and sandy bays along the Atlantic coast of southern Brazil and Uruguay, with seagrass beds as nursery habitat for pups and abundant benthic invertebrates and small fish for adults.
en.actions: Support WWF-Brazil's marine elasmobranch programme, advocate for guitarfish-specific bycatch reduction measures in Brazilian shrimp fisheries, and fund population surveys along the southern Brazilian coast.

es.description: La guitarra brasileña es un gran elasmobranquio con un cuerpo en forma de entre un tiburón y una raya, antes abundante en las aguas costeras cálidas y poco profundas del sur de Brasil y Uruguay. La pesca industrial lo ha reducido en más del 80% en las últimas décadas.
es.endangerment: La captura accidental de alto valor en arrastres de camarón y pesquerías de palangre, combinada con la pesca dirigida por sus grandes aletas, ha devastado las poblaciones; los estuarios poco profundos y las áreas de cría que necesita para las crías están muy impactados por el desarrollo costero.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Aguas costeras poco profundas, estuarios y bahías arenosas a lo largo de la costa atlántica del sur de Brasil y Uruguay, con praderas de posidonia como hábitat de cría para las crías y abundantes invertebrados bentónicos y peces pequeños para los adultos.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de elasmobranquios marinos de WWF-Brasil, abogar por medidas específicas de reducción de captura accidental de guitarra en las pesquerías de camarón brasileñas y financiar encuestas de población a lo largo de la costa sur de Brasil.

pt.description: O violão-brasileiro é um grande elasmobrânquio com um corpo em forma de entre um tubarão e uma arraia, antes abundante nas águas costeiras quentes e rasas do sul do Brasil e Uruguai. A pesca industrial o reduziu em mais de 80% nas últimas décadas.
pt.endangerment: A captura acidental de alto valor em redes de arrasto de camarão e pesqueiras de espinhel, combinada com a pesca direcionada por suas grandes barbatanas, devastou as populações; os estuários rasos e as áreas de criação que necessita para os filhotes são intensamente impactados pelo desenvolvimento costeiro.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Águas costeiras rasas, estuários e baías arenosas ao longo da costa atlântica do sul do Brasil e Uruguai, com pradarias de ervas marinhas como habitat de criação para filhotes e abundantes invertebrados bentônicos e peixes pequenos para adultos.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de elasmobranquios marinhos do WWF-Brasil, defender medidas específicas de redução de captura acidental de violão nas pesqueiras de camarão brasileiras e financiar levantamentos populacionais ao longo da costa sul do Brasil.

fr.description: La raie-guitare brésilienne est un grand élasmobranches au corps en forme de hybride entre un requin et une raie, autrefois abondant dans les eaux côtières chaudes et peu profondes du sud du Brésil et de l'Uruguay. La pêche industrielle l'a réduit de plus de 80% au cours des dernières décennies.
fr.endangerment: Les captures accessoires de grande valeur dans les chaluts à crevettes et les palangres, combinées à la pêche ciblée pour ses grandes nageoires, ont dévasté les populations ; les estuaires peu profonds et les zones de nurserie dont il a besoin pour les jeunes sont fortement impactés par le développement côtier.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Eaux côtières peu profondes, estuaires et baies sablonneuses le long de la côte atlantique du sud du Brésil et de l'Uruguay, avec des herbiers marins comme habitat de nurserie pour les jeunes et des invertébrés benthiques abondants et de petits poissons pour les adultes.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élasmobranches marins du WWF-Brésil, plaider pour des mesures spécifiques de réduction des prises accessoires de raies-guitares dans les pêcheries brésiliennes de crevettes et financer des enquêtes sur les populations le long de la côte sud du Brésil.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The monkey puzzle tree is a living fossil conifer from the Andes and coastal mountains of Chile and Argentina, with interlocking triangular leaves that persist for up to 24 years. Sacred to the Pehuenche people, its large seeds — piñones — are a critical food source.
endangerment: Past commercial logging devastated old-growth stands; now the Patagonian fire cycle, amplified by climate change, and invasive exotic species threaten remaining populations; cattle grazing prevents regeneration in many areas.
ecosystemNeeds: Volcanic soils and rocky slopes of the Andes and Coastal Range at 900–1,800 metres in the Araucanía and Bío Bío regions of Chile and Neuquén province of Argentina, in a cold, snowy climate with high annual rainfall.
actions: Support CONAF's monkey puzzle protected area network in the Araucanía region, advocate for a complete ban on monkey puzzle felling, and fund reforestation with locally sourced seeds in degraded Andean stands.

en.description: The monkey puzzle tree is a living fossil conifer from the Andes and coastal mountains of Chile and Argentina, with interlocking triangular leaves that persist for up to 24 years. Sacred to the Pehuenche people, its large seeds — piñones — are a critical food source.
en.endangerment: Past commercial logging devastated old-growth stands; now the Patagonian fire cycle, amplified by climate change, and invasive exotic species threaten remaining populations; cattle grazing prevents regeneration in many areas.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Volcanic soils and rocky slopes of the Andes and Coastal Range at 900–1,800 metres in the Araucanía and Bío Bío regions of Chile and Neuquén province of Argentina, in a cold, snowy climate with high annual rainfall.
en.actions: Support CONAF's monkey puzzle protected area network in the Araucanía region, advocate for a complete ban on monkey puzzle felling, and fund reforestation with locally sourced seeds in degraded Andean stands.

es.description: El árbol rompecabezas de mono es un conífera fósil viviente de los Andes y las montañas costeras de Chile y Argentina, con hojas triangulares entrelazadas que persisten hasta 24 años. Sagrado para el pueblo Pehuenche, sus grandes semillas —piñones— son una fuente crítica de alimento.
es.endangerment: La tala comercial pasada devastó los rodales de bosque antiguo; ahora el ciclo de incendios patagónico, amplificado por el cambio climático, y las especies exóticas invasoras amenazan las poblaciones restantes; el pastoreo de ganado impide la regeneración en muchas áreas.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Suelos volcánicos y laderas rocosas de los Andes y la Cordillera Costera a 900-1.800 metros en las regiones de Araucanía y Bío Bío de Chile y la provincia de Neuquén de Argentina, en un clima frío y nevado con alta precipitación anual.
es.actions: Apoyar la red de áreas protegidas del árbol rompecabezas de mono de CONAF en la región de Araucanía, abogar por una prohibición total de la tala del árbol rompecabezas de mono y financiar la reforestación con semillas de origen local en los rodales andinos degradados.

pt.description: A araucária é uma conífera fóssil viva dos Andes e das montanhas costeiras do Chile e Argentina, com folhas triangulares entrelaçadas que persistem por até 24 anos. Sagrada para o povo Pehuenche, suas grandes sementes — piñones — são uma fonte crítica de alimento.
pt.endangerment: A exploração comercial passada devastou os povoamentos de floresta antiga; agora o ciclo de incêndios patagônico, amplificado pelas mudanças climáticas, e as espécies exóticas invasoras ameaçam as populações restantes; o pastejo de gado impede a regeneração em muitas áreas.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Solos vulcânicos e encostas rochosas dos Andes e da Cordilheira Costeira a 900-1.800 metros nas regiões de Araucanía e Bío Bío do Chile e na província de Neuquén da Argentina, em um clima frio e nevado com alta precipitação anual.
pt.actions: Apoiar a rede de áreas protegidas da araucária da CONAF na região de Araucanía, defender uma proibição total do corte da araucária e financiar o reflorestamento com sementes de origem local nos povoamentos andinos degradados.

fr.description: L'araucaria du Chili est un conifère fossile vivant des Andes et des montagnes côtières du Chili et de l'Argentine, avec des feuilles triangulaires imbriquées qui persistent jusqu'à 24 ans. Sacré pour le peuple Pehuenche, ses grandes graines — piñones — constituent une source d'alimentation critique.
fr.endangerment: L'exploitation commerciale passée a dévasté les peuplements de vieille forêt ; le cycle d'incendies patagonien, amplifié par le changement climatique, et les espèces exotiques envahissantes menacent maintenant les populations restantes ; le pâturage du bétail empêche la régénération dans de nombreuses zones.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Sols volcaniques et pentes rocheuses des Andes et de la Cordillère côtière à 900-1 800 mètres dans les régions d'Araucanía et de Bío Bío au Chili et dans la province de Neuquén en Argentine, dans un climat froid et enneigé avec de fortes précipitations annuelles.
fr.actions: Soutenir le réseau de zones protégées de l'araucaria de la CONAF dans la région d'Araucanía, plaider pour une interdiction totale de l'abattage de l'araucaria et financer le reboisement avec des graines d'origine locale dans les peuplements andins dégradés.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The toromiro is a small flowering tree that was the last surviving native tree of Easter Island (Rapa Nui) when the last wild individual died around 1960. Seeds saved by Thor Heyerdahl in 1956 allowed propagation in botanical gardens worldwide; reintroduction is now underway.
endangerment: Polynesian settlers cleared Easter Island's original forest for agriculture and introduced rats ate seeds; introduced sheep and cattle then prevented regeneration; the final wild tree died in the 1960s.
ecosystemNeeds: The rocky volcanic slopes and crater walls of Easter Island in the southeast Pacific, in the sheltered humid zone of the island's volcanic craters; requires freedom from grazing pressure and invasive plant competition.
actions: Support the Conaf/RBG Gothenburg toromiro reintroduction programme on Easter Island, advocate for exclusion of grazing animals from restoration sites, and fund genetic diversity assessment of botanical garden specimens.

en.description: The toromiro is a small flowering tree that was the last surviving native tree of Easter Island (Rapa Nui) when the last wild individual died around 1960. Seeds saved by Thor Heyerdahl in 1956 allowed propagation in botanical gardens worldwide; reintroduction is now underway.
en.endangerment: Polynesian settlers cleared Easter Island's original forest for agriculture and introduced rats ate seeds; introduced sheep and cattle then prevented regeneration; the final wild tree died in the 1960s.
en.ecosystemNeeds: The rocky volcanic slopes and crater walls of Easter Island in the southeast Pacific, in the sheltered humid zone of the island's volcanic craters; requires freedom from grazing pressure and invasive plant competition.
en.actions: Support the Conaf/RBG Gothenburg toromiro reintroduction programme on Easter Island, advocate for exclusion of grazing animals from restoration sites, and fund genetic diversity assessment of botanical garden specimens.

es.description: El toromiro es un pequeño árbol con flores que era el último árbol nativo superviviente de la Isla de Pascua (Rapa Nui) cuando el último individuo silvestre murió alrededor de 1960. Las semillas guardadas por Thor Heyerdahl en 1956 permitieron la propagación en jardines botánicos de todo el mundo; la reintroducción está ahora en marcha.
es.endangerment: Los colonos polinesios talaron el bosque original de la Isla de Pascua para la agricultura y las ratas introducidas comieron las semillas; las ovejas y el ganado introducidos impidieron la regeneración; el último árbol silvestre murió en los años 60.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Las laderas volcánicas rocosas y las paredes del cráter de la Isla de Pascua en el Pacífico sureste, en la zona húmeda y protegida de los cráteres volcánicos de la isla; requiere libertad de la presión de pastoreo y la competencia de plantas invasoras.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de reintroducción del toromiro de Conaf/RBG Gothenburg en la Isla de Pascua, abogar por la exclusión de animales de pastoreo de los sitios de restauración y financiar la evaluación de diversidad genética de especímenes de jardines botánicos.

pt.description: O toromiro é uma pequena árvore florida que era a última árvore nativa sobrevivente da Ilha de Páscoa (Rapa Nui) quando o último indivíduo selvagem morreu por volta de 1960. Sementes guardadas por Thor Heyerdahl em 1956 permitiram a propagação em jardins botânicos em todo o mundo; a reintrodução está agora em andamento.
pt.endangerment: Os colonos polinésios desmataram a floresta original da Ilha de Páscoa para a agricultura e ratos introduzidos comeram as sementes; ovelhas e gado introduzidos impediram a regeneração; a última árvore selvagem morreu nos anos 1960.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: As encostas vulcânicas rochosas e as paredes da cratera da Ilha de Páscoa no Pacífico sudeste, na zona úmida e abrigada das crateras vulcânicas da ilha; requer ausência de pressão de pastejo e competição de plantas invasoras.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de reintrodução do toromiro da Conaf/RBG Gothenburg na Ilha de Páscoa, defender a exclusão de animais de pastejo dos locais de restauração e financiar a avaliação de diversidade genética de espécimes de jardins botânicos.

fr.description: Le toromiro est un petit arbre à fleurs qui était le dernier arbre indigène survivant de l'île de Pâques (Rapa Nui) lorsque le dernier individu sauvage est mort vers 1960. Des graines conservées par Thor Heyerdahl en 1956 ont permis la propagation dans des jardins botaniques du monde entier ; la réintroduction est maintenant en cours.
fr.endangerment: Les colons polynésiens ont défriché la forêt originelle de l'île de Pâques pour l'agriculture et les rats introduits ont mangé les graines ; les moutons et le bétail introduits ont empêché la régénération ; le dernier arbre sauvage est mort dans les années 1960.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Les versants volcaniques rocheux et les parois des cratères de l'île de Pâques dans le Pacifique Sud-Est, dans la zone humide et abritée des cratères volcaniques de l'île ; nécessite une absence de pression de pâturage et de compétition des plantes envahissantes.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de réintroduction du toromiro de Conaf/RBG Gothenburg sur l'île de Pâques, plaider pour l'exclusion des animaux de pâturage des sites de restauration et financer l'évaluation de la diversité génétique des spécimens des jardins botaniques.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: Palo de rosa is a tall Amazonian tree whose heartwood produces a highly prized essential oil — rosewood oil — used in perfumery and once a key ingredient in Chanel No. 5. Decades of destructive extraction by cutting and distilling entire trees have driven it to near extinction in the wild.
endangerment: Rosewood oil distilleries require around 60 felled trees per tonne of oil; despite a Brazilian export ban, illegal extraction continues in remote Amazonian forests near Manaus and along the Tapajós River.
ecosystemNeeds: Mature non-flooded terra firme rainforest in the central and lower Amazon basin in Brazil and Guiana, particularly in undisturbed interior forest away from river margins, where it reaches the canopy at 30–40 metres.
actions: Support INPA's sustainable rosewood cultivation programme from leaf-only extraction, advocate for strict enforcement of the Brazilian trade ban, and fund community monitoring of extraction hotspots in the Amazon.

en.description: Palo de rosa is a tall Amazonian tree whose heartwood produces a highly prized essential oil — rosewood oil — used in perfumery and once a key ingredient in Chanel No. 5. Decades of destructive extraction by cutting and distilling entire trees have driven it to near extinction in the wild.
en.endangerment: Rosewood oil distilleries require around 60 felled trees per tonne of oil; despite a Brazilian export ban, illegal extraction continues in remote Amazonian forests near Manaus and along the Tapajós River.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Mature non-flooded terra firme rainforest in the central and lower Amazon basin in Brazil and Guiana, particularly in undisturbed interior forest away from river margins, where it reaches the canopy at 30–40 metres.
en.actions: Support INPA's sustainable rosewood cultivation programme from leaf-only extraction, advocate for strict enforcement of the Brazilian trade ban, and fund community monitoring of extraction hotspots in the Amazon.

es.description: El palo de rosa es un alto árbol amazónico cuya madera produce un aceite esencial muy apreciado —aceite de palisandro— utilizado en perfumería y que alguna vez fue un ingrediente clave en Chanel N°5. Décadas de extracción destructiva talando y destilando árboles enteros lo han llevado casi a la extinción en la naturaleza.
es.endangerment: Las destilerías de aceite de palisandro requieren alrededor de 60 árboles talados por tonelada de aceite; a pesar de una prohibición de exportación brasileña, la extracción ilegal continúa en bosques amazónicos remotos cerca de Manaos y a lo largo del Río Tapajós.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Selva tropical de tierra firme no inundada madura en la cuenca central y baja del Amazonas en Brasil y Guiana, particularmente en el interior del bosque sin perturbaciones lejos de los márgenes fluviales, donde alcanza el dosel a 30-40 metros.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cultivo sostenible de palisandro del INPA a partir de la extracción solo de hojas, abogar por la aplicación estricta de la prohibición comercial brasileña y financiar el monitoreo comunitario de los focos de extracción en el Amazonas.

pt.description: O pau-rosa é uma alta árvore amazônica cuja madeira produz um óleo essencial muito apreciado — óleo de pau-rosa — usado em perfumaria e que já foi um ingrediente-chave no Chanel N°5. Décadas de extração destrutiva cortando e destilando árvores inteiras o levaram quase à extinção na natureza.
pt.endangerment: As destilarias de óleo de pau-rosa requerem cerca de 60 árvores abatidas por tonelada de óleo; apesar de uma proibição de exportação brasileira, a extração ilegal continua em florestas amazônicas remotas perto de Manaus e ao longo do Rio Tapajós.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta tropical de terra firme não inundada madura na bacia central e baixa do Amazonas no Brasil e na Guiana, particularmente no interior da floresta sem perturbações longe das margens dos rios, onde atinge o dossel a 30-40 metros.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de cultivo sustentável de pau-rosa do INPA a partir da extração apenas de folhas, defender a aplicação rigorosa da proibição comercial brasileira e financiar o monitoramento comunitário dos focos de extração na Amazônia.

fr.description: Le bois de rose est un grand arbre amazonien dont le bois produit une huile essentielle très prisée — l'huile de bois de rose — utilisée en parfumerie et autrefois ingrédient clé du Chanel No. 5. Des décennies d'extraction destructive par abattage et distillation d'arbres entiers l'ont amené au bord de l'extinction à l'état sauvage.
fr.endangerment: Les distilleries d'huile de bois de rose nécessitent environ 60 arbres abattus par tonne d'huile ; malgré une interdiction d'exportation brésilienne, l'extraction illégale se poursuit dans les forêts amazoniennes éloignées près de Manaus et le long du fleuve Tapajós.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt tropicale humide de terre ferme non inondée dans le bassin central et inférieur de l'Amazone au Brésil et en Guyane, notamment dans l'intérieur forestier non perturbé loin des berges des rivières, où elle atteint la canopée à 30-40 mètres.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme de culture durable du bois de rose de l'INPA à partir de l'extraction foliaire uniquement, plaider pour une application stricte de l'interdiction commerciale brésilienne et financer le suivi communautaire des zones d'extraction dans l'Amazonie.

imageCredit: Image: Wikimedia Commons, license varies — verify before publication.
description: The Oʻahu tree snail is one of 41 native Achatinella species, the celebrated arboreal tree snails of the island of Oʻahu, Hawaii. Their beautifully coloured, banded shells were historically collected into extinction; today introduced predators have eliminated most remaining populations.
endangerment: The introduced wolf snail Euglandina rosea — itself released as a biocontrol agent — hunts Achatinella species to extinction one population at a time; rats, chameleons, and fire kill remaining snails; habitat loss from urbanisation compounds the threat.
ecosystemNeeds: Native ʻōhiʻa and koa forest in the Koʻolau and Waianae mountain ranges of Oʻahu, particularly on specific host plant species in the canopy and understorey; each snail population is often restricted to a single valley or ridge.
actions: Support DLNR's Oʻahu tree snail captive rearing programme and predator-free exclosures, fund removal of wolf snails and chameleons from snail habitat, and advocate for ʻōhiʻa forest protection on Oʻahu's ridges.

en.description: The Oʻahu tree snail is one of 41 native Achatinella species, the celebrated arboreal tree snails of the island of Oʻahu, Hawaii. Their beautifully coloured, banded shells were historically collected into extinction; today introduced predators have eliminated most remaining populations.
en.endangerment: The introduced wolf snail Euglandina rosea — itself released as a biocontrol agent — hunts Achatinella species to extinction one population at a time; rats, chameleons, and fire kill remaining snails; habitat loss from urbanisation compounds the threat.
en.ecosystemNeeds: Native ʻōhiʻa and koa forest in the Koʻolau and Waianae mountain ranges of Oʻahu, particularly on specific host plant species in the canopy and understorey; each snail population is often restricted to a single valley or ridge.
en.actions: Support DLNR's Oʻahu tree snail captive rearing programme and predator-free exclosures, fund removal of wolf snails and chameleons from snail habitat, and advocate for ʻōhiʻa forest protection on Oʻahu's ridges.

es.description: El caracol arbóreo de Oʻahu es una de las 41 especies nativas de Achatinella, los célebres caracoles arbóreos de la isla de Oʻahu, Hawái. Sus hermosas conchas de colores y bandas fueron históricamente recolectadas hasta la extinción; hoy los depredadores introducidos han eliminado la mayoría de las poblaciones restantes.
es.endangerment: El caracol lobo introducido Euglandina rosea —él mismo liberado como agente de biocontrol— caza las especies de Achatinella hasta la extinción una población a la vez; las ratas, los camaleones y el fuego matan a los caracoles restantes; la pérdida de hábitat por la urbanización agrava la amenaza.
es.ecosystemNeeds: Bosque nativo de ʻōhiʻa y koa en las cordilleras Koʻolau y Waianae de Oʻahu, particularmente en especies de plantas huésped específicas en el dosel y el sotobosque; cada población de caracoles a menudo está restringida a un solo valle o cresta.
es.actions: Apoyar el programa de cría de caracoles arbóreos de Oʻahu del DLNR y las cercas libres de depredadores, financiar la eliminación de caracoles lobo y camaleones del hábitat de caracoles y abogar por la protección del bosque de ʻōhiʻa en las crestas de Oʻahu.

pt.description: O caracol-arbóreo-de-oahu é uma das 41 espécies nativas de Achatinella, os famosos caracóis arbóreos da ilha de Oʻahu, Havaí. Suas conchas lindamente coloridas e listradas foram historicamente coletadas até a extinção; hoje predadores introduzidos eliminaram a maioria das populações restantes.
pt.endangerment: O caracol-lobo introduzido Euglandina rosea — ele próprio liberado como agente de biocontrole — caça as espécies de Achatinella até a extinção uma população de cada vez; ratos, camaleões e fogo matam os caracóis restantes; a perda de habitat pela urbanização agrava a ameaça.
pt.ecosystemNeeds: Floresta nativa de ʻōhiʻa e koa nas cordilheiras Koʻolau e Waianae de Oʻahu, particularmente em espécies de plantas hospedeiras específicas no dossel e sub-bosque; cada população de caracóis muitas vezes está restrita a um único vale ou crista.
pt.actions: Apoiar o programa de criação de caracóis arbóreos de Oʻahu do DLNR e os cercados livres de predadores, financiar a remoção de caracóis-lobo e camaleões do habitat de caracóis e defender a proteção da floresta de ʻōhiʻa nas cristas de Oʻahu.

fr.description: L'escargot arboricole d'Oʻahu est l'une des 41 espèces indigènes d'Achatinella, les célèbres escargots arboricoles de l'île d'Oʻahu, Hawaii. Leurs magnifiques coquilles colorées et rayées ont été historiquement collectées jusqu'à l'extinction ; aujourd'hui, les prédateurs introduits ont éliminé la plupart des populations restantes.
fr.endangerment: L'escargot loup introduit Euglandina rosea — lui-même relâché comme agent de lutte biologique — chasse les espèces d'Achatinella jusqu'à l'extinction, une population à la fois ; les rats, les caméléons et le feu tuent les escargots restants ; la perte d'habitat due à l'urbanisation aggrave la menace.
fr.ecosystemNeeds: Forêt indigène de ʻōhiʻa et de koa dans les chaînes de montagnes Koʻolau et Waianae d'Oʻahu, notamment sur des espèces de plantes hôtes spécifiques dans la canopée et le sous-bois ; chaque population d'escargots est souvent limitée à une seule vallée ou crête.
fr.actions: Soutenir le programme d'élevage des escargots arboricoles d'Oʻahu du DLNR et les enclos sans prédateurs, financer l'élimination des escargots loups et des caméléons de l'habitat des escargots et plaider pour la protection de la forêt de ʻōhiʻa sur les crêtes d'Oʻahu.
